details: A militarized BattleMech version of Achernar's Loader King IndustrialMech, the Roughneck is a low-cost heavy 'Mech intended for price conscious buyers such as planetary militias and down-on-their-luck mercenary units. A pricier and more fragile variant, the 2A is based on the 1A, replacing the standard engine with a 260-rated extralight engine. Matching the 1A's speed, the weight saved is used to bulk up the Roughneck 2A's weapon loadout, dropping the machine guns and AC/10 for an AC/2 in each arm and the right torso and upgrading the head laser to a medium laser along with adding two to the center torso. Identical to the 1A in all other respect, including mounting only 14 standard heat sinks, the RGH-2A carries three tons each of SRM and autocannon ammunition.
yangsThoughts: A bit more fragile than the 1A but the improved firepower helps a lot, keep an eye on the ammo though, it's got a lot of it and it can go up easily.

details: A variant with firepower on par with the ancient Awesome, the 'Mech comes with three Extended Range Particle Projection Cannons, mounted in each arm and left torso. While retaining the Radical Heat Sink System, the 'Mech loses its secondary weapons and receives two Small X-Pulse Lasers, one in the right torso and a second in the head.
yangsThoughts: Simple and to the point as always for this mech. 3 big guns, and enough armor to keep firing them for a while without fear. If you really want to bring the pain, swap it to Clan-spec ER PPC instead.

details: One of the so-called Clanbuster variants developed in 3052 by ComStar in the lead-up to the Battle of Tukayyid, this variant is a major upgrade that replaces the standard fusion engine with a lighter but more vulnerable Vlar 300 extralight engine, the heat sinks have been upgraded to double heat sinks and two Nightwind large lasers, surplus from the Clanbuster upgrade of the Wyvern, have been added to the 'Mech's unoccupied arms.
yangsThoughts: Better weaponry, but not any more durable than the regular Champion, you might want to upgrade the armor on this thing if you want it to stay intact long enough to actually contribute well to a fight.

details: This variant was built by the Clans using their advanced technology, although it differs little from the original. Built in 2867 it uses an XL engine with the weight saved going into the addition of five jump jets that enable the 'Mech to jump up to 150 meters at a time. The medium lasers were upgraded to medium pulse lasers and a pair of ER medium lasers were added. The small lasers are also upgraded to small pulse lasers. The rest of the weaponry is upgraded to Clan standards and the remaining tonnage went to additional armor.
yangsThoughts: The lighter clan weaponry means this thing can mount more firepower and yet also have way more armor at the same time. A huge improvement over the original.

details: An upgraded version of the standard 1N that adds similarly improved heatsinking as the Grand Dragon, but without removing the ballistic weapon mounting.
yangsThoughts: This thing is interesting, it runs upgraded cooling which allows it to mount more heat wasteful firepower, but sticks with the ballistic mount of the 1N, should be a good platform for big ultra autocannons or rotary stuff.

details: An upgraded variant of the original 1A, it upgrades the heat sinks to doubles and also changes the chassis to endosteel and changes to an XL engine, the additional saved weight is used to change to a loadout of multiple snubnosed PPCs instead of the original.
yangsThoughts: A definite upgrade over the base mech, it completely reworks the weapons loadout for the better, though it is very short ranged.

details: This version of the Merlin keeps the generalist approach of prior models by only upgrading a few systems. Each arm mounted medium laser has been replaced by a Light PPC increasing the Merlin's long range punch while also hindering it up close. The original machine gun has been replaced with a Heavy Machine Gun and a half ton of ammunition. CASE in the left torso protects both the HMG and LRM ammunition. Thirteen double heat sinks do a good job of keeping the 'Mech cool
yangsThoughts: A nicely versatile mech, which can be customized extensively to make it better, I suggest replacing some of the energy weapons with ER versions or maybe even some clan-spec equipment if we have any.

details: A subvariant of sorts of the MLN-1D this variant drops the Heavy Machine Gun and one heat sink to replace the LRM with an MML-5 and two tons of ammunition protected by CASE II.
yangsThoughts: The MML definitely helps this output better firepower at range than the original LRM, still the same upgradeable mech otherwise.

details: This Merlin variant is a deadly infighter. Only the flamer and LRM-5 remain from its original armament. Each arm now holds a Medium Variable Speed Pulse Laser and the right torso now contains a Large Variable Speed Pulse Laser. These highly accurate lasers give this Merlin a devastating damage capacity up close. Heavy Ferro-Fibrous armor covers this variant and thirteen double heat sinks can struggle to keep this variant cool.
yangsThoughts: I feel like this mech would be better off without the flamer, honestly, the range is so short that you're going to struggle to use it effectively, and you'd probably be better off just running a an extra heat sink.

details: Another completely reworked variant with weight saving technologies, this time it changes to a ballistic focused loadout with twin ultra autocannon 5s to keep the heat generation down.
yangsThoughts: An interesting change up, 60 tons is a bit light to really get full power out of ballistic weapons but it can work similar to a rifleman if you want.

details: An upgraded model of the original 2M variant, this one improves the heatsinking and weaponry quality
yangsThoughts: I'm not sure if all that jumping ability is that useful, honestly, I think it might be better to rework this thing to focus on the firepower some more.

details: An upgraded model of the 8E variant, this version upgrades the weaponry array and protection to better function as the core of a lance.
yangsThoughts: A nice level of all energy firepower make this a great design for when you don't want to deal with ammo supplies, but the designers were nice enough to still leave some ballistic hardpoints in there, in case you want to have us techs put something in there for you.

details: An upgraded model of the 8E variant, this version upgrades the weaponry array and protection to better function as the core of a lance.
yangsThoughts: A nice level of all energy firepower make this a great design for when you don't want to deal with ammo supplies, but the designers were nice enough to still leave some ballistic hardpoints in there, in case you want to have us techs put something in there for you.

details: The 8K variant of the Quickdraw brings it up to 3062 weapons standards. The 'Mech is built using an endo steel chassis and is armored with eight and a half tons of ferro-fibrous armor. The 'Mech's arsenal has also been stripped and upgraded, carrying four forward-mounted ER medium lasers and an MRM-30 launcher, a compromise between the LRMs and SRMs of past models, supplied by two tons of ammunition.
yangsThoughts: A nice upgrade of the standard model, with a nice longish range weapons package.

details: The 5K Quickdraw is an upgraded variant using Star League technology produced by Luthien Armor Works in 3049. Carrying the same weaponry as the 5A model, its use of seventeen double heat sinks solves the problem of excessive waste heat.
yangsThoughts: The double heat sinks mean this version doesn't run hot at all, which lets that weapons loadout really go all out at close range.

details: This is the Jihad-era variant of the 'Mech which introduces newer equipment to the design. The LRM-15s have been removed in favor of four MML-5 Launchers which have been given 3 tons of ammunition. These new launchers give the 'Mech a better means of protecting itself at short ranges. The 'Mech has been given Jump Jets and an XL Gyro. The weight saved with these changes allows for additional armor to be installed on the arms and legs.
yangsThoughts: It's still a bit limited in terms of close range defense, but at least it has something better to use when the ranges get close. It really would work better if you can find us some clan grade tech to put on it instead though.

details: The Kintaro is designed primarily around its missile beacon and its weapons loadout reflects this. For long range sparring, and to provide some form of striking capability as it closes, the Kintaro carries a single Holly-5 LRM-5 launcher. When the Kintaro moves into range to actually hit the enemy with the Narc beacon, it has two HoverTec-6 SRM-6 launchers and two Magna medium lasers that come into play, making it a competent and deadly close combat 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: Not as much direct firepower as the more common 18, but that NARC lets both this mech and others hit the target much harder with missiles once they're tagged. Watch out though, missiles tend to ruin your salvage chances since they blow up everything evenly.

details: The KTO-19b was an upgrade utilized by the Royal Brigades of the SLDF. It used an XL engine and double heat sinks to free up space to upgrade the KTO-19's LRM-5 launcher to a larger LRM-15 with two tons of CASE-protected ammunition. CASE was also added to the SRM ammo bins, with the leftover half ton devoted to more armor.
yangsThoughts: I bet Lady Kamea would have loved to get her hands on one of these, all that freed tonage lets you really stack up on the missiles and it's still got that NARC to power them up. Do try to bring some other missiles along on the rest of the lance to really pilo on the damage though.

details: The original Naja is an extensive refit of the SLDF Kintaro. Despite using a bulkier Inner Sphere XL engine and double heat sinks it was an effective 'Mech. The medium lasers and small laser are standard models, and it lacks CASE.
yangsThoughts: I'm not sure who brought these antiquated clan models here, but it's still effective in the right hands, extensive missile firepower at both close and long range will make short work of most things it can't outrun.

details: The Naja was built around a combination of long and short range missile launchers. The Clan-spec LRM-20 provides most of the firepower, while at close range the SRM-6s and SRM-4s can unleash a deadly barrage of missiles, supported by a pair of ER medium lasers and an ER small laser. Protected by 12 tons of standard armor plate, the Naja is heavily armored.
yangsThoughts: An older clan mech design, I didn't realize this model had made its way to the inner sphere given how few of them are supposedly left in Cloud Cobra hands, but maybe someone else has started making them here?.

details: The modern Osprey's main ranged weapon was its dorsally mounted M-7 Gauss rifle supported by Holly MML-7 rack underneath. At closer ranges the 'Mech carried a pair of Diverse Optics ER medium lasers and a single ER small laser. The small laser and a Beagle active probe were designed to fit into mountings identical to the medium lasers, thus giving the impression the 'Mech mounts two medium lasers in each of its stubby arms.
yangsThoughts: A definite improvement over the original Osprey, this mech has an extremely heavy weapons loadout for a medium mech. It may not be particularly fast, but getting a full size Gauss Rifle around on the battlefield while still mounting extra defenses lets this thing go toe to toe with things way bigger.

details: The D3D variant of this 'Mech is a potent long-range sniper, first built in 3060. It uses the same chassis and engine combination as the D3 variant but uses MASC to augment its movement to give it a maximum speed of 129.6 km/h. The primary weapon on the D3D is a light Gauss rifle, with an LRM-10 for added long range assistance, and an ER medium laser for close range combat.
yangsThoughts: This thing is a nice twist on the classic mech, though I'm not sold on that light gauss rifle, if we have one spare, we might want to consider a clan-spec version for increased firepower.

details: The D variant of the Centurion, introduced in 3049, is the first of many upgrades using Star League technology. The chassis was reengineered to use endo steel construction techniques, and the power plant was changed from the standard engine to a GM 300 extralight engine, increasing its maximum speed to 97.2 km/h. Finally, the weapons were upgraded, with the AC/10 swapped for a Mydron Excel LB 10-X autocannon and an Artemis IV fire control system was added to the LRM-10 launcher. The two medium lasers were retained, although whether the rear-facing laser has been switched to facing forward is a matter of pilot preference.
yangsThoughts: A powerful close range brawling upgrade, that extra speed allows for it to get in range for the autocannon very easily.

details: First walking off the production line in late 3050, the 5D Enforcer is a major upgrade to the standard 'Mech, meeting many of its pilots' desire for something faster, better protected, and with more endurance. The Nissan 200 engine has been removed and replaced with a Magna 250 extralight engine, increasing the Enforcer's top speed to 86.4 km/h and the amount of space available for other additions. The armor has been stripped from the 'Mech and replaced with StarGuard CIV ferro-fibrous armor with CASE to protect against an ammunition explosion while the weapons payload has also been changed. Retaining the small laser, both the AC/10 and the large laser were replaced with a new Mydron Excel LB 10-X autocannon, fed by two tons of ammunition, and a BlazeFire Sweetshot ER large laser. Finally, an additional jump jet was added, increasing the jumping distance by 30 meters, up to 150 meters, to keep up with the Enforcer's new speed profile.
yangsThoughts: Nice upgrade, it's faster, better armored and better protected, what's not to love.

details: The redesigned Bombard-013 was released in 3064 after a humiliating Davion Arena match defeat in 3054. It replaced the twin autocannons with two thunderbolt launchers and two ER Medium Lasers. It mounts 12 reloads for each of the launchers which is enough for decent endurance in the arenas it is designed for.
yangsThoughts: It can at least hit things at more than point blank range, but its actual performance otherwise is pretty abysmal for how slow it is.

details: A mixed tech version designed to finally address the painfully slow movement of the standard design, it upgrades from the 100 XL engine to a 150 XL augmented by MASC.  The additional weight of the system is compensated for by switching to dual Clan-spec gauss rifles augmented by leg mounted antipersonnel weaponry.
yangsThoughts: This one can at least move at a reasonable speed, so it's actually kind of viable as a general combat mech.

details: A mixed tech version designed to finally address the painfully slow movement of the standard design, it upgrades from the 100 XL engine to a 150 XL augmented by MASC.  An alternate setup to the 1X, this one is inspired by the hunchback IIC to mount twin Clan-spec Ultra AC20s for extreme close in firepower.
yangsThoughts: This one can at least move at a reasonable speed, so it's actually kind of viable as a general combat mech.

details: Like their Inner Sphere counterparts, Hunchback IICs mounting smaller weapons are frequently referred to as Swayback IICs. This Hunchback IIC 2, a Crusader variant from 3061, utilizes four heavy large lasers that can quickly cause a heat-induced shutdown when fired without thought. As secondary weapons the variant includes two medium pulse lasers. The 19 double heat sinks cannot handle the heat buildup for long.
yangsThoughts: Huge firepower just like the regular one, and just as fragile, it can hammer things at close range but keep it out of the line of fire until it's time to strike if you want it to live.

details: The Warden variant of 3062 makes use of three ATM-12s with four tons of ammunition, and keeps the ER medium lasers of the original.
yangsThoughts: A ton of long range fire support power here, this thing is probably the most survivable version, just cause it can stay farther away.

details: This variant debuted in 3075 during the Jihad and carries a pair of experimental Clan-made RAC/5s, each with two tons of ammo, an ECM suite, and additional armor in place of the standard UAC/20s.
yangsThoughts: The change to RACs reduces the damage a bit, but the improvement in armor helps it stay alive long enough to deliver the damage much more effectively.

details: The 3L variant is a basic upgrade using recovered Star League technology introduced in 3051. The PPC was upgraded to a Ceres Arms Warrior ER PPC and the medium and small lasers have been removed in favor of a single Ceres Arms Model W Medium Pulse Laser. To handle the extra heat from the new ER PPC double heat sinks were added.
yangsThoughts: That extra range on the ER PPC is a great help in letting this mech snipe from the edges of a battle, and it can still mix it up in close range if needed.

details: Extending the 3L variant even more, this Jihad-era 3077 field refit drops all the existing weaponry and replaces them with a Snub-Nose PPC, MML-5, and ER medium laser.
yangsThoughts: A medium to close range brawling specialized variant, it still has the indirect fire support from the MML but now it can use the launcher even at point blank.

details: A 3050's tech version of the -30, the -26 also uses an endo steel chassis but mounts an XL engine, the extra weight saved allowing the 'Mech to carry an UAC/5 and a pair of medium pulse lasers for offensive armament. Field endurance is limited however, as six and half tons of armor provided basic protection, and a single ton of autocannon reloads are chewed through rapidly. The 10 double heat sinks struggle to manage heat strain.
yangsThoughts: A nice variant upgrade, but it does suffer from some heat issues and ammo problems, just say the word though and we can make use of all that additional tonnage to mount some other weapons.

details: A 3050 era take on -99, the -27 drops the TAG and sword to upgrade the light PPC to a full size PPC thanks to the use of endo steel, retaining the Guardian ECM suite but replacing the stealth armor with an equal volume of standard armor.
yangsThoughts: Another interesting variant, kind of wish they had kept the stealth armor so I could get a peek at how it works, but I guess they don't want that sort of tech to reach the periphery.

details: The Clint was primarily designed as a cheap recon unit, with the capabilities of a well-armed, low-end medium weight 'Mech, and in this regard it served well. With a top speed of 97.2 km/h and a jumping capability of one hundred and eighty meters, the Clint can easily keep up with other recon elements. The 2-3U is a Capellan field upgrade of the Clint that strips all of the armaments from the chassis and replaces them with more potent and enduring weaponry, the most radical of which is the primary weapon. The AC/5 has been replaced with a Magna Firestar ER PPC while both the medium lasers were replaced with Magna 400P medium pulse lasers; as a result, it runs much hotter than the original. The 3U also packs double heat sinks in place of single models and is more deadly in combat. The 3Us were later deployed by the Armed Forces of the Federated Commonwealth in the mid-3050s up until the breakup of the Federated Commonwealth; they have since been adopted by the Lyran Alliance and several mercenary outfits.
yangsThoughts: A nice sniper mech and it can still keep itself decently cool with the double heat sinks, overall a good versatile medium.

details: TThe Icarus II was the first homegrown BattleMech design to be mass-produced by the Free Worlds League. Originally designed as a speedy striker to compliment the Mackie, the Icarus II was fast enough for its day though it is pitfully slow by modern standards. The 2S is an upgraded design produced in very limited quantities by Gilmour Militech. The small manufacturing concern resurrected the design in 3073. It is built on an endo steel chassis and uses an XL engine to reach a top speed of 86 km/h, and an extra jump jet allows it to reach 150 meters per leap. The AC/10 is upgraded to an LB 10-X autocannon, and the two medium lasers have been upgraded to a medium pulse laser. An extra single heat sink keeps heat in check and extra armor keeps the fragile engine safe.[2] Gilmour Militech produces a mere three of these 'Mechs per year.
yangsThoughts: A pretty nice upgrade with more tonnage free for guns, not a lot of hardpoints though.

details: One of the earliest Inner Sphere OmniMechs to be deployed, this mech was a conversion of the long used Cicada design. It trades off the standard Cicadas speed for increased armor and weaponry. Unfortunately, the designers choice to use single heat sinks, limits the flexibility of the mech's variants, and most are variants on the theme of missile weapons in the arms and supporting energy weapons in the torso. The Strider is built on an Alshain Type KR240 Endo Steel chassis to help save on weight. Built with the rugged GM 240 fusion engine, the Strider has a top speed of 97.2 km/h, a reduction by a third in comparison to the sprightly Cicada. Outfitted with CASE, built around the durable GM 240, and protected with seven and a half tons of armor, the Strider is a durable 'Mech for its weight class; capable of suffering considerable damage before being disabled or destroyed. While its twelve tons of OmniPod space in theory can carry any equipment required, due to LAW's use of standard heat sinks rather than double-capacity models, almost every standardized configuration developed has sported a mix of arm-mounted missile pods with supporting lasers or eletronic equipment in its torso bays. The Primary configuration of the Strider is a classic example. Configured to be a short-range strike 'Mech, its primary weapons are a pair of SRM-6 launchers that are linked to an Artemis IV fire control system that increases missile accuracy. Backing this up is a single small laser giving the 'Mech some protection once its ammunition bins run dry. The Strider also carries a Beagle active probe in this configuration, allowing the 'Mech to find hidden units when acting in reconnaissance roles.
yangsThoughts: It hits harder than a Cicada, for sure, and it's more durable, but it's not something you want to bring in for a heavy brawl.

details: One of the earliest Inner Sphere OmniMechs to be deployed, this mech was a conversion of the long used Cicada design. It trades off the standard Cicadas speed for increased armor and weaponry. Unfortunately, the designers choice to use single heat sinks, limits the flexibility of the mech's variants, and most are variants on the theme of missile weapons in the arms and supporting energy weapons in the torso. The Strider is built on an Alshain Type KR240 Endo Steel chassis to help save on weight. Built with the rugged GM 240 fusion engine, the Strider has a top speed of 97.2 km/h, a reduction by a third in comparison to the sprightly Cicada. Outfitted with CASE, built around the durable GM 240, and protected with seven and a half tons of armor, the Strider is a durable 'Mech for its weight class; capable of suffering considerable damage before being disabled or destroyed. Intended for long range combat and fire support, the Strider B carries a simple payload of two LRM-10 racks with one ton of ammunition for each.
yangsThoughts: A bit more missile focused, it's got enough missiles to at least hit things decently, and has enough speed to stay away from most things, but its actual damage is a bit lacking.

details: One of the earliest Inner Sphere OmniMechs to be deployed, this mech was a conversion of the long used Cicada design. It trades off the standard Cicadas speed for increased armor and weaponry. Unfortunately, the designers choice to use single heat sinks, limits the flexibility of the mech's variants, and most are variants on the theme of missile weapons in the arms and supporting energy weapons in the torso. The Strider is built on an Alshain Type KR240 Endo Steel chassis to help save on weight. Built with the rugged GM 240 fusion engine, the Strider has a top speed of 97.2 km/h, a reduction by a third in comparison to the sprightly Cicada. Outfitted with CASE, built around the durable GM 240, and protected with seven and a half tons of armor, the Strider is a durable 'Mech for its weight class; capable of suffering considerable damage before being disabled or destroyed. The F configuration of the Strider is intended for close range combat but unlike the Strider E it is not as dependent on ammunition based weapons. The primary weapons are a pair of Streak SRM-4 launchers backed up by two medium lasers. The 'Mech has also had six jump jets added, allowing it to jump up to one hundred and eighty meters. Considered by many pilots as the most effective configuration prior to the Jihad, the Strider F was also been jokingly called the 'Grand Jenner' by some members of the DCMS due its similar abilities of the Jenner.
yangsThoughts: People claim this is the most effective variant, I dunno if I believe that hype, but it does seem to be a nice solid setup for close range brawling, though you'll need to run away fast if you get overmatched.

details: Also armed for short-range combat, the B configuration has an Artemis IV FCS–enhanced SRM-6 launcher as its primary weapon, backed up by four small lasers.
yangsThoughts: Very short ranged, not particularly powerful, but very fast. Could really use some better firepower for its weight.

details: The Rattlesnake was an upgrade to the Jenner, designed to make use of cutting-edge technology recovered from the Helm Memory Core while outwardly appearing as a standard Jenner. As far as can be established, they were created by refitting existing Jenners as opposed to being built in a proper factory. As part of Project Trapdoor, the Rattlesnakes were deployed with an eye to sowing confusion and uncertainty among Draconis Combine forces, and they were also used for MIIO black ops. One mission which involved the destruction of a Warhawk (Masakari) OmniMech by Rattlesnakes was embellished and altered to an unspecified degree before being published as part of the psyops. As of 3084 the 'Mech was widely regarded as an urban legend. The original JR7-31 Rattlesnake from the 3040s reportedly featured cutting-edge technologies including Ferro-fibrous armor, an XL Engine and a Guardian ECM Suite. Its main armament are seven medium lasers. Its 11 double heat sinks are only just capable of keeping its heat in check. Two jump jets were added to the original five, for a total of seven.
yangsThoughts: This thing has a LOT of firepower for how fast it is, but it does run pretty hot, so keep an eye on it. Best used to flank and shoot things in the rear.

details: Introduced in 3108, this variant re-imagines the Toro with modern weaponry. It removes the PPC in favor of a Light PPC and a medium laser. It also drops both LRM-5s in exchange for twin MML-5s with a ton each of LRM and SRM ammo protected by CASE. Finally, the variant uses weight savings from switching to ferro-fibrous armor and a Small cockpit to install a Guardian ECM suite.
yangsThoughts: A nice upgrade to the old classic design, this thing is much more versatile than the original with those flexible launchers and better heat handling. It could use some better damage output than a light PPC though.

details: The B-12 Toro is a B-9 variant upgrade that released in 3146. The B-12 has an endo steel structure while returning to standard armor. It upgrades to 10 double heat sinks and an extralight engine which ups its maximum speed to 97.2 km/h. The medium laser is upgraded to an ER medium laser that a targeting computer ensures pinpoint accuracy with. Ammunition is now protected by CASE II and electronic warfare capabilities are bolstered by a Angel ECM suite.
yangsThoughts: A targeting computer, faster speed and better weapons range are all great upgrades, you'd have to switch to Clan equipment to upgrade this thing any further at this point.

details: An upgraded variant of the standard 3L that mounts improved lostech weaponry.
yangsThoughts: A decent light fire support mech that's fast enough to avoid most things, but you might need more ammo if you want to challenge large enemies.

details: An upgraded variant of the standard 3T that mounts improved lostech weaponry.
yangsThoughts: A decent energy mech that doesn't need resupply, but you're gonna need better cooling if you want it to stay in the fight for long.

details: The 7K is an upgrade of the base design, introduced by Nimakachi in 3067 and based on the success of other revitalized classic designs. It's built using an endo steel structure, five and a half tons of ferro-fibrous armor, an extra-light fusion engine in place of the standard model, and carries two medium pulse lasers. Perhaps most critically, Nimakachi was able to finally install an ejection system in the 7K.
yangsThoughts: At least it's not made of paper anymore, but it's still pretty fragile, but with speed like this it doesn't really matter that much as you can outrun basically anything that tries to kill you.

details: The -9M is a further Dark Age evolution of the -8M. Utilizing light ferro-fibrous, endo steel, and an XL engine leaves enough room to mount two medium variable speed pulse lasers, one in each side torso. These weapons give this Spider a very powerful bite up close, but with range no better than standard medium lasers. This loadout can leave it outranged by most other 'Mechs on the modern battlefield, but its speed and maneuverability help mitigate this. Repeated firing of both lasers and jumping can overburden the 'Mech's 10 double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Huge close in damage for this design, but you might want to consider replacing one of the pulse lasers with something with a bit more range.

details: This Clan Jade Falcon version carries CASE with an ER medium laser in the center torso section and two Streak SRM-2s in each arm. SRM munitions are stored in the arms with a cross-feed mechanism.
yangsThoughts: Decent close in firepower, though nowhere near the firpower of the regular variant, the extra range from the streaks though lets it actually apply its damage without getting as close.

details: Wolf's Dragoons fielded this variant, which eschewed the anti-missile system in favor of a full 150 meters of jumping and a small laser.
yangsThoughts: Tiny, fragile and poorly armed, this thing is mostly only useful in an urban setting where you can get around behind light enemies and harass them.

details: Based on the 161 and produced by Coalition Armory Inc., this Hornet variant was produced by using a 100-rated XL engine with 10 compact heat sinks. It is protected by three and a half tons of ferro-fibrous armor, uses an endo steel chassis, and is armed with two small pulse lasers, and an MML-5 with two tons of ammunition in a CASE-protected bay. It is most frequently seen in the service of AFFS and Filtvelt Coalition militias.
yangsThoughts: The armor is still fragile, but the extra weight gives you some extra weaponry options, you might want to go for heavier energy weapons and reduce the missile weight.

details: The LCT-1VX is an attempt to increase the survivability and firepower of Locust. This version packs increased armor, an upgraded laser and added support weaponry.
yangsThoughts: Still fragile, still blazing fast and still lightly armed, this version is a bit stronger but not that much.

details: The Royal Locust introduced in 2610 mounts a small pulse laser and a medium pulse laser in each arm, while keeping the torso-mounted medium laser. It also uses an XL engine, endo steel chassis, and ferro-fibrous armor, and the single heat sinks have also been upgraded to double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Pretty much as good as you're gonna get with this thing in terms of survivability and firepower, it's still a bit fragile, but you can't really do anything about that at this weight.

details: The G variant was designed as a dedicated 'Mech killer, mounting a Gauss Rifle and two tons of ammunition in each arm, it also has a slightly more flexible set of mounting brackets allowing for easier reconfiguration.
yangsThoughts: Two Gauss Rifles on a mech this size is some serious firepower, but the more important change is the more flexible hardpoint layout, letting us techs reconfigure it even more flexibly.

details: The first of the Inner Sphere OmniMechs to be conceptualized and one of the last to reach production by the Draconis Combine as part of its 'first generation', the Avatar's design is based on captured Vulture OmniMechs.
yangsThoughts: The first of the Inner Sphere OmniMechs to be conceptualized and one of the last to reach production by the Draconis Combine as part of its 'first generation', the Avatar's design is based on captured Vulture OmniMechs.

details: A fire support variant, the B configuration carries two LRM-15 racks and two LRM-10 racks. These are all mated to an Artemis IV Fire Control System. The only short range weaponry on the B configuration consists wholly of the two Medium Lasers mounted on the base chassis.
yangsThoughts: A fire support variant, the B configuration carries two LRM-15 racks and two LRM-10 racks. These are all mated to an Artemis IV Fire Control System. The only short range weaponry on the B configuration consists wholly of the two Medium Lasers mounted on the base chassis.

details: The 2K is another upgrade of the Orion using Star League technology. Released at the same time as the cheaper (and more popular) 2M variant the 'Mech uses an Endo Steel chassis and an XL engine to allow for even more saved weight for weaponry. For long-range combat, the ON2-K is armed with a Gauss rifle as well as an LRM-15 linked to an Artemis IV FCS for increased accuracy. For closer ranges, the 'Mech has a blistering array of five medium pulse lasers. Fourteen double heat sinks are needed to keep the heat fully undercontrol, but this required using Standard armor instead of Ferro-Fibrous to save space.
yangsThoughts: A beast at close ranges with all those pulse lasers and still capable of extensive long range firepower, this thing is a great upgrade to the older version, just watch out for how much heat that pulse laser array puts out.

details: This variant of the Cataphract takes advantage of more modern tech to free extra tonnage for its returning to the original prototype electronic warfare equipment from the 0X. Mounting significantly better firepower than the 0X model it brings an ER PPC, Gauss Rifle, two ER medium lasers and an MML-5, giving it weaponry for any range.
yangsThoughts: I'm surprised to see another variant with that prototype ewar equipment, but I guess it did perform better than the modern Guardians. This thing is also no slouch on the firepower or armor either, so it can actually hold its own without problem.

details: The Grand Titan is built on an Corean Model 101BLP Endo Steel chassis and with an LTV 400 XL engine, which propels the Grand Titan to a top speed of 64.8 km/h. Clad in eighteen tons of Durallex Heavy armor, the 'Mech carries a large arsenal of weapons that allow it to work best at short to medium range. The longest-range weapon carried is a Holly LRM-15 launcher linked to an Artemis IV fire control system for greater accuracy. The 'Mech also carries two Diverse Optics Type 20 Large Pulse Lasers which it brings to bear on an enemy as it closes ranges. Once at close range, the 'Mech carries two Diverse Optics Medium Pulse Lasers, two Diverse Optics Small Pulse Lasers, and a pair of Holly SRM-6 launchers with Artemis IV fire control systems. For protection from missile attacks, the 'Mech has a McArthur anti-missile system.
yangsThoughts: This thing is a strong combatant at most ranges, and has enough hardpoints to be refitted to work with all sorts of more updated weaponry. I suggest considering some xpulse lasers or even some clan spec stuff if we have it available.

details: Introduced in 3058, the N11M model of the Grand Titan is a major modification of the design. The 'Mech carries as its primary long-range weapons a Light Gauss Rifle and an ER PPC which gives the 'Mech powerful long-range reach. The 'Mech has for short-range combat four Medium Pulse Lasers and two Streak SRM-4 launchers. It is also protected by a Guardian ECM Suite. This upgrade brings the Grand Titan to modern standards and makes it as powerful on the battlefield now as it was when it was first released, arguably the only flaw being an embarrassing typo in the public relations advertising for the variant constantly calling it the Grant Titan.
yangsThoughts: This thing is a strong combatant at most ranges, and has enough hardpoints to be refitted to work with all sorts of more updated weaponry. I suggest considering some xpulse lasers or even some clan spec stuff if we have it available.

details: The Fox is an early example of a Clan medium BattleMech, originally created as Clan Sea Fox's first BattleMech. The Sea Foxes didn't sell the Fox to any other Clans directly, but it was commonly used and participated in numerous Trials in the twenty-ninth century. This varriant is a pure Clan-tech version of the Fox that uses the same endo steel chassis and XL engine. The weapons are upgraded to Clan-spec LRM-15s, Streak SRM-2s, machine guns, and ER medium lasers in the arms. The center torso medium laser is removed and replaced by an ER large laser. To handle the higher heat load it carries an extra double heat sink. Ferro-fibrous armor protects the Fox.
yangsThoughts: A very nice medium mech, lots of firepower and speed. The only real issue is that it is heavily ammo dependent.

details:
The Glass Spider is the direct descendant of the Star League Galahad, so it was only natural that Inner Sphere observers gave it that code name when they first encountered the BattleMech. The Glass Spider was one of the most common 'Mechs in second-line formations and could be found in the touman of every Clan. It served in the dual role of providing direct-fire support for friendly forces and serving as the first line of air defense for most garrison units. While this mission profile made it unpopular among those trueborns assigned to pilot a Glass Spider, it was a popular choice for any MechWarrior engaging an aerospace fighter in an honor duel. Its ability to quickly kill a target, and thus quickly win honor, also made it popular among some warrior.
The weapons on the Glass Spider are simple and straightforward: two Gauss rifles, one in each arm. The long-range, powerful guns devastate most opponents quickly and, like the Rifleman's weapons, can rotate completely to cover the rear arc.
yangsThoughts: This thing is a simple and straight-forward fire support mech similar to the Rifleman in concept and use, but with FAR more firepower. It's vulnerable if anything manages to get close, but with the firepower this thing puts out most things won't make it that far.

details:
The Glass Spider is the direct descendant of the Star League Galahad, so it was only natural that Inner Sphere observers gave it that code name when they first encountered the BattleMech. The Glass Spider was one of the most common 'Mechs in second-line formations and could be found in the touman of every Clan. It served in the dual role of providing direct-fire support for friendly forces and serving as the first line of air defense for most garrison units. While this mission profile made it unpopular among those trueborns assigned to pilot a Glass Spider, it was a popular choice for any MechWarrior engaging an aerospace fighter in an honor duel. Its ability to quickly kill a target, and thus quickly win honor, also made it popular among some warrior.
Created by Clan Wolf in 2952, this variant replaces the weapons with an all-energy configuration. The arm-mounted gauss cannons are replaced with two large pulse lasers, while the removal of the ammunition bins allows for two medium pulse lasers in the left torso and three small pulse lasers in the right. All are guided by a targeting computer that makes this 'Mech deadly accurate while sixteen double heat sinks keep heat build-up under wraps. This variant is incredibly popular, though it is not very prevalent.
yangsThoughts: This variant strips out some of the ballistic equipment for energy weapons, it's got a very powerful pulse laser loadout and none of the ammo limitations or minimum range issues of the standard model, it's also fairly heat stable for how much it mounts.

details:
The Glass Spider is the direct descendant of the Star League Galahad, so it was only natural that Inner Sphere observers gave it that code name when they first encountered the BattleMech. The Glass Spider was one of the most common 'Mechs in second-line formations and could be found in the touman of every Clan. It served in the dual role of providing direct-fire support for friendly forces and serving as the first line of air defense for most garrison units. While this mission profile made it unpopular among those trueborns assigned to pilot a Glass Spider, it was a popular choice for any MechWarrior engaging an aerospace fighter in an honor duel. Its ability to quickly kill a target, and thus quickly win honor, also made it popular among some warrior.
Clan Star Adder first fielded this variant in 3065 which, while based on the Glass Spider 2, is so thorough a redesign it even looks different. A pair of Type DDS 'Kingston' ER PPCs in place of the pulse lasers gives it almost the same long-range firepower as the original. The two Series PPS-XIX medium pulse lasers are retained, though the smalls are exchanged for three Series 6A heavy medium lasers. The targeting computer remains to guide all the weapons, however the improved XS-485 Mk. IV communications system is second-to-none and the advanced GH D8j targeting and tracking system can track over a hundred land and air targets at once.
yangsThoughts: This variant strips out the ballistic equipment for energy weapons, same general idea as the 2 version, but it's way stronger with those PPCs packing the same long range punch as the original rifles and then multiple medium pulse lasers and heavy mediums for close in punch

details:
The Glass Spider is the direct descendant of the Star League Galahad, so it was only natural that Inner Sphere observers gave it that code name when they first encountered the BattleMech. The Glass Spider was one of the most common 'Mechs in second-line formations and could be found in the touman of every Clan. It served in the dual role of providing direct-fire support for friendly forces and serving as the first line of air defense for most garrison units. While this mission profile made it unpopular among those trueborns assigned to pilot a Glass Spider, it was a popular choice for any MechWarrior engaging an aerospace fighter in an honor duel. Its ability to quickly kill a target, and thus quickly win honor, also made it popular among some warrior.
Introduced by the Wolf Empire, this configuration fits an XL engine and supercharger to the standard chassis. In place of the standard Gauss rifles, each arm carries a HAG 20 which share seven tons of ammunition. Three ER small lasers are placed in the right torso. All these weapons are tied into a targeting computer. This design is primarily used for anti-aircraft work.
yangsThoughts: Back to the ballistics with this version, but now it has a way stronger set of twin HAGs with an engine booster to allow for more speed.

details:
The Stag was Clan Wolverine's redesign based on the Vulcan BattleMech. Khan Sarah McEvedy's orders led to the Stag, which transformed the venerable infantry-killer into a cavalry 'Mech that could also scout. As this design was pressed into service shortly after the conclusion of Operation KLONDIKE, it used Star League technology.
The Stag was built on an Endo Steel frame and powered by an XL engine. Ten tons of armor provided the 'Mech with the maximum possible protection. The Stag retained the Vulcan's top speed of 97 km/h, though it contained MASC that allowed the design to push up to 130 km/h in bursts. It was a bit less mobile, however, as it did not keep the jump jets.
The long-range threat from the cavalry Mech comes from one ER Large Laser. At close range it is supplemented by paired medium lasers and SRM-4s. Twelve double heat sinks handle all of the heat generated by the weapons.
yangsThoughts: Where the hell did this antique come from. All of the original versions of these were supposed to have been taken as isorla by the other Clans, and yet a lot of these parts look brand new. Someone must be manufacturing these, but I hesitate to speculate on who it could be.

details:
The Stag was Clan Wolverine's redesign based on the Vulcan BattleMech. Khan Sarah McEvedy's orders led to the Stag, which transformed the venerable infantry-killer into a cavalry 'Mech that could also scout. As this design was pressed into service shortly after the conclusion of Operation KLONDIKE, it used Star League technology.
The Stag was built on an Endo Steel frame and powered by an XL engine. Ten tons of armor provided the 'Mech with the maximum possible protection. The so-called Stag II incorporated a prototype version of the ER PPC that was roughly comparable to the Clan version of the weapon, though it was slightly less destructive. This upgrade of the large laser necessitated removal of the MASC, though the top speed was upgraded to 119 km/h. Additionally, ferro-fibrous weave was incorporated into the armor, though at an overall decrease in protection. The SRMs were removed in favor of a third medium laser, two Medium Pulse Lasers, and two Small Lasers.
yangsThoughts: Another strange antique, though again, some of those parts look brand new, this thing is definitely being made somewhere. As for how to use it, it's a fast energy boat, so keep it moving and blasting as much as you can manage with the heat.

details:
The Stag was Clan Wolverine's redesign based on the Vulcan BattleMech. Khan Sarah McEvedy's orders led to the Stag, which transformed the venerable infantry-killer into a cavalry 'Mech that could also scout. As this design was pressed into service shortly after the conclusion of Operation KLONDIKE, it used Star League technology.
The Stag was built on an Endo Steel frame and powered by an XL engine. Ten tons of armor provided the 'Mech with the maximum possible protection. This apparently new variant mounts some unusual new ballistic and energy weapons giving it efficient performance as a long range sniper.
yangsThoughts: Are you guys messing with me? This has to be some sort of a joke, right? A brand new variant of a centuries old extinct design and I just happen to be able to get it in MY mechbay? Well, however it came to be, this thing is a good sniper but isn't very good at close range, fortunately, it's fast enough to dictate range easily.

details: The Vision Quest was created by Clan Nova Cat to honor the memory of Khan Sandra Rosse and the future she foresaw for both the Nova Cats and Kerensky's Clans as a whole. A solid workhorse design, the Vision Quest was built around a Kaumodaki Gauss rifle, two newly developed Clan spec SearFire ER Large Lasers and three Eclipser Medium Pulse Lasers. Two tons of gauss rounds and twelve double heat sinks give the design the ability to easily support an aggressive offensive or anchor a defensive line.
yangsThoughts: I thought these had all gone extinct, but I guess there were still some examples floating around out there. This thing is a solid workhorse with a nicely varied set of weaponry able to work at any range.

details: The Vision Quest was created by Clan Nova Cat to honor the memory of Khan Sandra Rosse and the future she foresaw for both the Nova Cats and Kerensky's Clans as a whole. Only four years after its introduction, the Vision Quest was fully retooled to Clan tech standards, boosting its armor and swapping out the improved Gauss rifle for a Class-20 Ultra autocannon.
yangsThoughts: I thought these had all gone extinct, but I guess there were still some examples floating around out there. This thing is a solid workhorse with a nicely varied set of weaponry able to work at any range, though it is more short ranged than the original model with its Gauss Rifle.

details: A modernization of the original Woodsman design, this omnimech is 5 tons heavier and the product of refitting the artillery missile based Naga into a direct brawler that is fast but lightly armored for its weight. Its primary configuration mounts twin 10 class autocannons in the arms and an LRM 20 for support.
yangsThoughts: Nice to see the same basic shape as the original Woodsman is still around. This thing is very lightly armored for its weight, so you can't keep it in direct combat for long, the best strategy is to focus on dealing damage as fast as possible and then pull back behind more heavily armored friends.

details: A modernization of the original Woodsman design, this omnimech is 5 tons heavier and the product of refitting the artillery missile based Naga into a direct brawler that is fast but lightly armored for its weight. This even shorter ranged brawling variant a massive LB 20-X AC in the left arm, along with some ER large lasers for ranged sniping and a Streak SRM 6 for extra close in firepower.
yangsThoughts: Nice to see the same basic shape as the original Woodsman is still around. Still lightly armored for its weight, this version goes even more in for dealing damage as fast as possible before it can be destroyed by retaliatory fire.

details: A modernization of the original Woodsman design, this omnimech is 5 tons heavier and the product of refitting the artillery missile based Naga into a direct brawler that is fast but lightly armored for its weight. This variant is designed to cover all ranges and situations with an UAC 5 and medium pulse laser in the right arm, a large and medium pulse laser in the left arm along with a streak srm 6 and an LRM 15 in the left torso to cover long ranges.
yangsThoughts: Nice to see the same basic shape as the original Woodsman is still around. A more versatile variant, this one doesn't have the close in hitting power of the other types, but its ability to hold back from a battle and then move in for the kill once the enemy is weakened is useful.

details: After the Clan invasion showed that Clan tactics often did not hold up against Inner Sphere forces because of the protracted engagements, Clan Coyote scientists designed the Canis. This second-line assault BattleMech primarily utilizes energy weapons, though it does use some ballistic weaponry to provide a stronger punch. In order to provide the tonnage for this powerful weapon array, the Canis utilizes an Endo Steel internal structure, as well as an extralight fusion engine. The energy weapons on the Canis are four arm-mounted Series 7Ja ER Large Lasers. While these pack a powerful, long-range punch, they often overload the design's sixteen double heat sinks. For medium-range support, the Canis utilizes a pair of torso-mounted Type 9 Ultra Autocannon/10s, though it only carries enough ammunition for approximately a minute and a half of sustained firing.
yangsThoughts: Very slow for an 80 ton assault, but well armored and well armed, if you want to work in more protracted engagements you might want to downgrade those lasers a bit and add some more autocannon ammo.

details: After the Clan invasion showed that Clan tactics often did not hold up against Inner Sphere forces because of the protracted engagements, Clan Coyote scientists designed the Canis. This second-line assault BattleMech primarily utilizes energy weapons, though it does use some ballistic weaponry to provide a stronger punch. In order to provide the tonnage for this powerful weapon array, the Canis utilizes an Endo Steel internal structure, as well as an extralight fusion engine. Developed during the Jihad in 3071, this is an upgrade in the design's weaponry. The 'Mech's Ultra Class autocannons are removed, for a pair of Class 20 Hyper-Assault Gauss Rifles with four tons of ammunition. Three of the 'Mech's ER Large Lasers are removed, with one being moved to its head. In their place, they've been given two Large Pulse Lasers.
yangsThoughts: Those hyper assault gauss rifles provide some seriously nasty punch at a distance, so this mech excels as a sniper and fire support, you still might need more ammo if you want to run a long battle though.

details: The Grendel features a powerful Consolidated 315 XL extralight engine, enabling it to move at a fleet 118.8 km/h, and enough jump jets fixed on the chassis to propel it up to 210 meters at a leap. The Sharks spared no expense in weight-saving materials, making use of an the XL engine, endo steel structure, and seven and a half tons of ferro-fibrous armor to provide thirteen and half tons of available pod space. The incredible speed of the Grendel is not terribly helpful in most zellbrigen duels, but it is effective in large-scale actions especially against Inner Sphere foes of similar weight hard pressed to counter its speed or firepower. The Grendel is also popular with technicians, because while many OmniMechs are often hangar queens, the Grendel can go long periods without maintenance and still remain combat effective. The Primary configuration uses an ER large laser at long range, but really excels at closer ranges. Up close, the Grendel makes use of three ER medium lasers that can be devastating to light 'Mechs and a Streak SRM-6 that exploits any holes the lasers make. An ER small laser that is effective at point-blank range seems an afterthought. Two extra double heat sinks help to keep the design mostly cool.
yangsThoughts: A fast, versatile omnimech, best used as a close range brawler that softens enemies up at range first. Apparently, the clans renamed it the Grendel after the name given to it by the Com Guard troops that faced it on Tukayyid.

details: The Grendel features a powerful Consolidated 315 XL extralight engine, enabling it to move at a fleet 118.8 km/h, and enough jump jets fixed on the chassis to propel it up to 210 meters at a leap. The Sharks spared no expense in weight-saving materials, making use of an the XL engine, endo steel structure, and seven and a half tons of ferro-fibrous armor to provide thirteen and half tons of available pod space. The incredible speed of the Grendel is not terribly helpful in most zellbrigen duels, but it is effective in large-scale actions especially against Inner Sphere foes of similar weight hard pressed to counter its speed or firepower. The Grendel is also popular with technicians, because while many OmniMechs are often hangar queens, the Grendel can go long periods without maintenance and still remain combat effective. This missile boat configuration carries a pair of LRM-15s with two tons of ammunition for each. A medium pulse laser and ER small laser provide backup firepower.
yangsThoughts: A missile boat layout of this mech is a good way to get a fast set of lrm barrage onto the field.

details: The Grendel features a powerful Consolidated 315 XL extralight engine, enabling it to move at a fleet 118.8 km/h, and enough jump jets fixed on the chassis to propel it up to 210 meters at a leap. The Sharks spared no expense in weight-saving materials, making use of an the XL engine, endo steel structure, and seven and a half tons of ferro-fibrous armor to provide thirteen and half tons of available pod space. The incredible speed of the Grendel is not terribly helpful in most zellbrigen duels, but it is effective in large-scale actions especially against Inner Sphere foes of similar weight hard pressed to counter its speed or firepower. The Grendel is also popular with technicians, because while many OmniMechs are often hangar queens, the Grendel can go long periods without maintenance and still remain combat effective. This configuration uses an UAC/5 for long range work. It mounts an ER medium laser, a pair of medium pulse lasers, and an ER small laser for close-up engagements.
yangsThoughts: A version that runs some ballistic firepower for more versatility, you may wind up having some ammo problems if you don't add some more ammo though.

details: The Onager is a Clan Jade Falcon assault BattleMech produced by Red Devil Industries on Pandora shortly after the end of the Jihad. Named after a medieval siege engine, the Onager's creation was ordered by Khan Samantha Clees as a replacement for Inner Sphere designs used in various garrison forces. The Onager's weaponry is designed more with close combat in mind than ranged fighting. To handle threats at long range, the 'Mech relies on its massive Class 30 hyper-assault Gauss rifle. The weapon has four tons of ammunition to give it ample time to cut down incoming enemies. At shorter ranges, the 'Mech is capable of bringing the rest of its firepower to bear. This arsenal includes a pair of Medium Pulse Lasers and two SRM-6 launchers. To clear infantry and other smaller targets, the 'Mech also has a pair of Micro Pulse Lasers and a Small Pulse Laser. The heat from its weapons is managed by 12 double heat sinks, though this is not entirely sufficient.
yangsThoughts: A pretty good sniping mech with jumping, pretty much a Highlander but with newer clan tech than the actual Highlander IIC. You might need to beef up that short range firepower more if you plan on engaging a lot, also that free arm is great for actuator upgrades.

Named after a medieval siege engine, the Onager's creation was ordered by Khan Samantha Clees as a replacement for Inner Sphere designs used in various garrison forces. The weapon has four tons of ammunition to give it ample time to cut down incoming enemies. At shorter ranges, the 'Mech is capable of bringing the rest of its firepower to bear. This arsenal includes a pair of Medium Pulse Lasers and two SRM-6 launchers. To clear infantry and other smaller targets, the 'Mech also has a pair of Micro Pulse Lasers and a Small Pulse Laser. The heat from its weapons is managed by 12 double heat sinks, though this is not entirely sufficient.
yangsThoughts: A pretty good sniping mech with jumping, pretty much a Highlander but with newer clan tech than the actual Highlander IIC. You might need to beef up that short range firepower more if you plan on engaging a lot, also that free arm is great for actuator upgrades.

details: The Jade Phoenix serves as a workhorse OmniMech within the Falcon touman. All configurations are fairly straightforward, with few surprises. This leads the Mech to be deployed in honest battles that rely on a Warrior's skills rather than shock tactics or deception. The fixed Partial Wing assembly and high speed give the Jade Phoenix unparalleled maneuverability, allowing the pilot to gain positions that are typically unreachable by assault Mechs. The Primary Configuration is geared towards long-range combat, with two ER PPCs backed up by an HAG 30. Four Jump Jets grant the Primary Configuration its agility, while five additional double heat sinks help to dissipate the tremendous waste heat of jump maneuvers and weapons fire
yangsThoughts: That partial wing assembly is kind of heavy, but the jump distance boost is nice, you might want to pair it with some improved jump jets to really get this thing moving in the air.

details: The Jade Phoenix serves as a workhorse OmniMech within the Falcon touman. All configurations are fairly straightforward, with few surprises. This leads the Mech to be deployed in honest battles that rely on a Warrior's skills rather than shock tactics or deception. The fixed Partial Wing assembly and high speed give the Jade Phoenix unparalleled maneuverability, allowing the pilot to gain positions that are typically unreachable by assault Mechs. A juggernaut designed to fight in the middle of the battle, the A configuration mounts a large pulse laser, medium pulse laser and small pulse laser in the left arm, while a mammoth Class-20 Ultra autocannon occupies the right arm. Four tons of ammunition supplies the autocannon, while Improved Jump Jets allows the Jade Phoenix to reach close-combat ranges.
yangsThoughts: This version mounts improved jets, so it can get into brawling range fast and bring its close range weaponry to bear.

details: The Jade Phoenix serves as a workhorse OmniMech within the Falcon touman. All configurations are fairly straightforward, with few surprises. This leads the Mech to be deployed in honest battles that rely on a Warrior's skills rather than shock tactics or deception. The fixed Partial Wing assembly and high speed give the Jade Phoenix unparalleled maneuverability, allowing the pilot to gain positions that are typically unreachable by assault Mechs. A deadly skirmisher, the B-configuration carries two Gauss rifles in the arms that allow it to keep most enemies at bay. An improved heavy medium and improved heavy large laser supplement the Gauss rifles when ranges close. Four tons of ammunition keep the Gauss rifles well stocked, while four jump jets gives the pilot tactical options.
yangsThoughts: This thing can get into sniping positions easily like a Highlander, but its much longer jump range allows it to easily evade attempts to surround it after its taken a vantage point.

details: The Jade Phoenix serves as a workhorse OmniMech within the Falcon touman. All configurations are fairly straightforward, with few surprises. This leads the Mech to be deployed in honest battles that rely on a Warrior's skills rather than shock tactics or deception. The fixed Partial Wing assembly and high speed give the Jade Phoenix unparalleled maneuverability, allowing the pilot to gain positions that are typically unreachable by assault Mechs.The C-configuration is a mixed missile boat. Dual ATM 12s with six tons of ammunition offer flexibility, while two SRM 6s and two ER medium lasers add to the short range damage potential. The ubiquitous four jump jets are also present.
yangsThoughts: A nice change to the standard layouts, this thing can easily stay behind cliffs to make use of its indirect fire capability, and then pop out when enemies are weakened. Don't mention that tactic to a Jade Falcon warrior though, they'd call it dishonorable.

details: Designed to improve upon the Dire Wolf, the Iron Cheetah mounts a 400-rated XL engine. This enables to the 'Mech to reach speeds of 64 km/h. While designers feared the increased engine mass would excessively reduce the 'Mech's payload, the increased cooling efficiency meant little impact to the 'Mech's practical punch. Both Endo Steel and ferro-fibrous armor were used to save additional weight. The Primary Configuration mounts a number of long-range weapons, giving the Iron Cheetah amazing sniping potential. Dual ER PPCs supported by an Artemis-IV-augmented LRM 15 rack and Gauss rifle allow it to disable or destroy most opponents before they can close. Medium pulse lasers and an ER small laser provided close-in defense, while an anti-missile system handled any incoming missile barrages.
yangsThoughts: Now this is a rare find, those Draconis Combine boys must have lost track of this one, or maybe it fell off the back of a Sea Fox truck. However you managed to 'procure' it for us, it'll be an extremely deadly mech with a massive payload of weaponry that is surprisingly fast for a mech this heavy.

details: Designed to improve upon the Dire Wolf, the Iron Cheetah mounts a 400-rated XL engine. This enables to the 'Mech to reach speeds of 64 km/h. While designers feared the increased engine mass would excessively reduce the 'Mech's payload, the increased cooling efficiency meant little impact to the 'Mech's practical punch. Both Endo Steel and ferro-fibrous armor were used to save additional weight. A close-in brawler, the A's primary weapon is a massive Ultra AC/20 mounted in the right arm. Double Streak SRM 6 racks and multiple Machine Guns support the main weapon. A pair of Large Pulse Lasers provide accurate long-range fire. Finally, a single Active Probe helps prevent ambushes.
yangsThoughts: Ooooh, this will be a very nice close in brawler that still has a decent long range punch with those pulse lasers.

details: Designed to improve upon the Dire Wolf, the Iron Cheetah mounts a 400-rated XL engine. This enables to the 'Mech to reach speeds of 64 km/h. While designers feared the increased engine mass would excessively reduce the 'Mech's payload, the increased cooling efficiency meant little impact to the 'Mech's practical punch. Both Endo Steel and ferro-fibrous armor were used to save additional weight. Mounting paired Large Pulse Lasers and six Medium Pulse Lasers, the B-configuration is capable of highly accurate fire even while utilizing its four Jump Jets to seek cover and firing positions. A Targeting Computer provides additional firing assistance.
yangsThoughts: It's unusual to mount jump jets on something this heavy, and you're gonna have issues with the heat generation, but its pretty much the most maneuverable you can make a behemoth like this.

details: Designed to improve upon the Dire Wolf, the Iron Cheetah mounts a 400-rated XL engine. This enables to the 'Mech to reach speeds of 64 km/h. While designers feared the increased engine mass would excessively reduce the 'Mech's payload, the increased cooling efficiency meant little impact to the 'Mech's practical punch. Both Endo Steel and ferro-fibrous armor were used to save additional weight. The C-configuration is designed for holding the line with direct fire weapons. Quadruple ER Large Lasers and an LRM 15 allow the Iron Cheetah to strike at extreme ranges. Four Medium Pulse Lasers and two Streak SRM 2s handle any opponents who manage to close into shorter distances. Eight additional Double Heat Sinks handle the extra waste heat.
yangsThoughts: This is basically a Supernova but with more weapons variety and a bit less overheating, you might want to take those streaks off though, they seem a bit superflous compared to the rest of the weaponry and it would let you upgrade the LRM rack.

details: Designed to improve upon the Dire Wolf, the Iron Cheetah mounts a 400-rated XL engine. This enables to the 'Mech to reach speeds of 64 km/h. While designers feared the increased engine mass would excessively reduce the 'Mech's payload, the increased cooling efficiency meant little impact to the 'Mech's practical punch. Both Endo Steel and ferro-fibrous armor were used to save additional weight. Configuration D mounts an eclectic assortment of weapons that manage to complement each other fairly well. A single Gauss rifle and paired ER Large Lasers let this configuration punch holes in the enemy. Once the target's armor is in tatters, the D-Configuration can bring its massive LB 20-X AC into play, where its cluster ammunition can devastate internal components. An ECM Suite provides defense against enemy electronics.
yangsThoughts: I don't know who came up with this weapons layout, but it actually does work out fairly well, a heavy long range punch and a heavy short range punch for a followup, you might want to consider swapping the shotgun for a RAC if we have one spare to really focus on the long range firepower.

details: The use of a weaker engine and correspondingly slower speed compared to the Timber Wolf is offset by the inclusion of jump jets and a MASC system in the Sidewinder. Twelve tons of ferro-fibrous armor provide ample protection, and 34.75 tons of pod space are available. In its only known configuration, the Sidewinder mounts a Gauss rifle, two LRM-10 with Artemis IV fire control, a Large Pulse Laser, two Medium Pulse Lasers and an AMS system, with twelve double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: When you first brought this thing into the bay I thought it was a Timber Wolf, but it seems to be a rare Cloud Cobra mech. The weapons loadout is pretty decent overall, but it could probably use a bit more focus if you're gonna use it as part of a unit instead of solo.

details: Intended to push a medium reconnaissance 'Mech to the most extreme limits, the Phantom is built on a lightweight Type-A endo steel chassis and is propelled to a top speed of 151.2 km/h by a Light Force 360 XL extralight engine. The Phantom also boasts strong protection for its size with six tons of Composite A-2 ferro-fibrous armor. In its Primary configuration the Phantom is an electronics marvel. To root out hidden units, the Phantom carries an active probe which is able to find hidden enemy units at up to 180 meters. The Phantom prime is capable of spotting for friendly Arrow IV artillery with a TAG laser designator and, for protection from enemy electronic warfare systems, the 'Mech has an ECM suite. To engage its enemies at long range, the Phantom has an LRM-5 launcher backed up by an ER medium laser and an ER small laser for close combat.
yangsThoughts: A pretty nice little electronics warfare and brawler mech that's extremely fast for its size, nothing is gonna be catching it unless you let them, but you may have trouble mounting enough firepower to kill bigger things.

details: Intended to push a medium reconnaissance 'Mech to the most extreme limits, the Phantom is built on a lightweight Type-A endo steel chassis and is propelled to a top speed of 151.2 km/h by a Light Force 360 XL extralight engine. The Phantom also boasts strong protection for its size with six tons of Composite A-2 ferro-fibrous armor. Capable of working as a long-range fire support 'Mech or very short-range brawler, the Phantom A is armed with a pair of LRM-5 launchers for long-range fire support capabilities. For close range combat, the 'Mech has five ER small lasers, one firing to the rear, and a single ER medium laser, making it a powerful medium 'Mech at any range.
yangsThoughts: It lacks the electronics of the prime, but it's got a lot of short range firepower to compensate, it should have no trouble getting close and striking enemies in the rear.

details: Intended to push a medium reconnaissance 'Mech to the most extreme limits, the Phantom is built on a lightweight Type-A endo steel chassis and is propelled to a top speed of 151.2 km/h by a Light Force 360 XL extralight engine. The Phantom also boasts strong protection for its size with six tons of Composite A-2 ferro-fibrous armor. A dedicated close combat machine, the B configuration carries two SRM-4 launchers as its primary short range weapons. These are supported by an ER medium laser and a rear-mounted ER small laser. Additionally, an active probe protects the 'Mech from ambush.
yangsThoughts: It has the probe of the prime but not the ECM, so it will not be as good at getting close sneakily, but if you can get it close with it's speed then it has a lot of firepower to tear things apart.

details: Intended to push a medium reconnaissance 'Mech to the most extreme limits, the Phantom is built on a lightweight Type-A endo steel chassis and is propelled to a top speed of 151.2 km/h by a Light Force 360 XL extralight engine. The Phantom also boasts strong protection for its size with six tons of Composite A-2 ferro-fibrous armor. Capable of working as a long-range fire support 'Mech or very short-range brawler, the Phantom A is armed with a pair of LRM-5 launchers for long-range fire support capabilities. For close range combat, the 'Mech has five ER small lasers, one firing to the rear, and a single ER medium laser, making it a powerful medium 'Mech at any range.
yangsThoughts: A ton of support weapons mean you can get a lot of short range firepower here, but you don't have a lot of options for long range, get it close though, and those small lasers will show their power nicely.

details: This variant is apparently an attempt by the Jade Falcons to use experimental technology in a combat unit. Originally mistaken for a LAM, the Cougar-XR uses a partial wing to extend its jump range to 210 meters. An LRM-15 launcher equipped with Artemis V FCS is located in each side torso, while an ER medium pulse laser provides direct fire. To make room for these systems and the wing, the Cougar-XR uses a small cockpit, sourced from the Inner Sphere, an endo steel chassis, and an extralight engine. As of 3077 it appears that the Cougar-XR is a standard BattleMech instead of being an OmniMech like its progenitor design.
yangsThoughts: That partial wing system looks weird, but it gives it a much longer jump range than a normal mech, I just don't know if it's overall worth the weight though its probably overall better than the limitations of a LAM in the long run.

details: The Bruin is a Clan Ghost Bear assault BattleMech which was produced at the Dominion's Bergan Industries factory on Alshain. Its conception was to help unify and bolster the Inner Sphere economy while continuing integration with Clan Ghost Bear. The Bruin's long-range firepower includes a right arm mounted Omega 12-coil Gauss rifle and a center torso mounted Type X Short Bow LRM-10 Launcher. These two weapons give the 'Mech far reaching power, providing it the ability to penetrate an opponent's armor and damage its interior equipment.For its intermediate and short range brackets, the 'Mech is armed with a Clan Kolibri Delta Series Large Pulse Laser in its left arm, a Series 2f ER Medium Laser in each arm, and a Smartshot Mark V Streak SRM-4 launcher in each torso.
yangsThoughts: Yet another sensible and efficient Clan ranged combat mech that mounts jump jets and a gauss rifle. Tactics, as always, are similar to those of the highlander, unless you want to change it up and go for close range weaponry instead.

details: The Bruin is a Clan Ghost Bear assault BattleMech which was produced at the Dominion's Bergan Industries factory on Alshain. Its conception was to help unify and bolster the Inner Sphere economy while continuing integration with Clan Ghost Bear. This variant introduced in 3083[4] was designed using lessons learned from the liberation of Terra from the Word of Blake. The Bruin 2 drops the Gauss Rifle and both Medium Lasers to mount 2 Large Pulse Lasers and 2 Ultra Class 5 Autocannons. Also added is a standard SRM 2 Launcher with inferno SRM missiles as ammunition. To augment the 'Mech's ability to handle infantry, it has been provided with Machine Guns.
yangsThoughts: I'm not really sure about those machine guns, you might want to swap them out for small pulse lasers or something similar to avoid that ammo cooking off when you don't expect it, but otherwise it's a solid variant for all ranges.

details: The Chimera is built on a Chameleon Light Endo 2 endo steel chassis that carries a GM 240 Extra Light XL engine which provides the 'Mech with a top speed of 97.2 km/h. The Chimera's mobility is enhanced by six Rawling 40 jump jets that allow the 'Mech to jump up to 180 meters. The Chimera is protected by seven tons of StarGuard CCM armor.[6]The Chimera carries an eclectic mix of weapons. The 'Mech has, as its two primary weapons, an ExoStar ER large laser that provides the 'Mech with effective long range firepower, and a Shigunga MRM-20 launcher that gives the 'Mech a powerful medium range punch for its size. For close combat, the 'Mech mounts a Brightbloom ER medium laser and a Scatter Gun Light machine gun.
yangsThoughts: A solid little medium mech, it has a nice punch from that MRM launcher and a long jump range, but it's a bit weak compared to other medium mechs, you'll need to maneuver well to bring your missiles to bear without getting shot up.

details: The Jinggau is built on a lightweight Chariot Type II Endo Steel chassis and is powered by a VOX 325 XL engine that gives it a top speed of 86.4 km/h. To increase its mobility, the Jinggau has five Hellespont Leaper jump jets that allow it to jump up to one hundred and fifty meters. For protection, the Jinggau carries eleven and a half tons of armor that protects the 'Mech from enemy fire. To dissipate the waste heat produced by the Jinggau's weapons and jump jets, the 'Mech has thirteen double heat sinks that are more than adequate. Overall, with its high ground speed, superior mobility, armor, and firepower the Jinggau is a 'Mech more than capable of handling most enemies on today's battlefield. The primary weapon on the Jinggau is a powerful Zhi-tong-yao Gauss rifle fed from a two-ton ammunition bay, giving the 'Mech a maximum striking distance of up to six hundred and sixty meters which allows the Jinggau the opportunity to draw the first blood on the battlefield. For close combat, the Jinggau has a set of four Diverse Optics Type ER medium lasers and a single, highly accurate, Raker-IV medium pulse laser.
yangsThoughts: A solid heavy mech, it has a tendency to overheat just a bit, and it could use some more gauss ammo but otherwise it's a great choice for most uses.

details: The Jinggau is built on a lightweight Chariot Type II Endo Steel chassis and is powered by a VOX 325 XL engine that gives it a top speed of 86.4 km/h. To increase its mobility, the Jinggau has five Hellespont Leaper jump jets that allow it to jump up to one hundred and fifty meters. For protection, the Jinggau carries eleven and a half tons of armor that protects the 'Mech from enemy fire. To dissipate the waste heat produced by the Jinggau's weapons and jump jets, the 'Mech has thirteen double heat sinks that are more than adequate. Overall, with its high ground speed, superior mobility, armor, and firepower the Jinggau is a 'Mech more than capable of handling most enemies on today's battlefield. This variant was introduced just after the start of the Jihad, applying the latest Capellan technology to the 'Mech. A pair of plasma rifles are mounted in the right arm, while in the left arm, the 'Mech has been given six standard medium lasers. To help cope with the heat, the 'Mech's double heat sink count has been raised to fifteen.
yangsThoughts: An all energy loadout, this thing is unusual these days in that it actually mounts regular medium lasers, you might want to consider swapping them out for something with better range.

details: The Jinggau is built on a lightweight Chariot Type II Endo Steel chassis and is powered by a VOX 325 XL engine that gives it a top speed of 86.4 km/h. To increase its mobility, the Jinggau has five Hellespont Leaper jump jets that allow it to jump up to one hundred and fifty meters. For protection, the Jinggau carries eleven and a half tons of armor that protects the 'Mech from enemy fire. To dissipate the waste heat produced by the Jinggau's weapons and jump jets, the 'Mech has thirteen double heat sinks that are more than adequate. Overall, with its high ground speed, superior mobility, armor, and firepower the Jinggau is a 'Mech more than capable of handling most enemies on today's battlefield. In 3078 Clan Hell's Horses Eleventh Mechanized Cavalry Cluster salvaged many Jinggaus from battles with Capellan forces on Hsien during Operation SCOUR. The Clan was so impressed by the design they rebuilt these salvaged 'Mechs with Clan technology. The G7Lr replaces the IS double heatsinks with smaller Clan versions, and also covers the mech in 11 tons of Clan Ferro-Fibrous. The pair of Plasma Rifles remain unchanged in the right arm, but the left arm now carries six Clan ER Medium Lasers and the head mounts a single Clan ER Small Laser.
