yangsThoughts: Clans just showing off their superior weaponry as usual with this one, it's basically the same as the regular version's loadout but with better weaponry and more free tonnage.

details: The Jinggau is built on a lightweight Chariot Type II Endo Steel chassis and is powered by a VOX 325 XL engine that gives it a top speed of 86.4 km/h. To increase its mobility, the Jinggau has five Hellespont Leaper jump jets that allow it to jump up to one hundred and fifty meters. For protection, the Jinggau carries eleven and a half tons of armor that protects the 'Mech from enemy fire. To dissipate the waste heat produced by the Jinggau's weapons and jump jets, the 'Mech has thirteen double heat sinks that are more than adequate. Overall, with its high ground speed, superior mobility, armor, and firepower the Jinggau is a 'Mech more than capable of handling most enemies on today's battlefield. The G8Ar is the second Hell's Horses variant. Similar to the G7Lr the heatsinks and armor are now Clan grade. The entire weapons compliment remains essentially identical to the standard G8A but all weapons are now Clan tech. An ER Small Laser is added to the head and an ECM suite is added to the center torso.
yangsThoughts: Clan weaponry turns an already solid mech design into an even more deadly machine, this thing is a deadly foe at any range.

details: Continuing the trend toward specialized BattleMechs, the Bombardier was proposed to the Star League in 2735 by Wakazashi Enterprises as an ArtilleryMech. The concept behind the design was to make a BattleMech that could replace less-mobile artillery vehicles, which might fall behind in an advance or be destroyed by counterbattery fire. It would be well-protected with twelve and a half tons of armor and use specialized missile racks which could carry diverse types of advanced missiles. Over years of testing and field exercises the Bombardier proved itself extremely effective at fulfilling the long-range fire support role, easily surpassing its vehicular counterparts and even inspiring talks of disbanding the SLDF's artillery arm once enough Bombardiers were operational.  This plan failed sharply once the succession wars started, however, as the lack of stable supply lines and safe factory logistics meant that the supplies of more specialized LRM ammo were depleted extremely rapidly. Initially forced onto the battlefield, the lack of significant brawling weapons led to the mechs suffering enormous losses. The primary weapons on the Bombardier are a pair of Delphinius-20 LRM-20 launchers. These launchers are specially designed to handle the unique ammunition types in use at the time, such as Swarm and Thunder Missile LRMs, and while they have an excellent reach only two tons of ammunition means they run out after only twelve salvos. The secondary weapon carried by the Bombardier is an Arrowlight-4 SRM-4 built into the right arm. While suitable for driving off light units that get within the LRMs' minimum range, it is not enough to truly protect the 'Mech from a determined attack and it also suffers from having only a single ton of ammunition.
yangsThoughts: This thing still has a horrible ammunition supply issue, though it is nice to see a venerable design like this in my Mechbay.  If you want to use it, i suggest either stripping a bit of armor, or replacing that SRM with SOME sort of energy defense weapon. That said, if you've got any Clan-spec LRMs, they will make this design shine.

details: Continuing the trend toward specialized BattleMechs, the Bombardier was proposed to the Star League in 2735 by Wakazashi Enterprises as an ArtilleryMech. The concept behind the design was to make a BattleMech that could replace less-mobile artillery vehicles, which might fall behind in an advance or be destroyed by counterbattery fire. It would be well-protected with twelve and a half tons of armor and use specialized missile racks which could carry diverse types of advanced missiles. Over years of testing and field exercises the Bombardier proved itself extremely effective at fulfilling the long-range fire support role, easily surpassing its vehicular counterparts and even inspiring talks of disbanding the SLDF's artillery arm once enough Bombardiers were operational.  This plan failed sharply once the succession wars started, however, as the lack of stable supply lines and safe factory logistics meant that the supplies of more specialized LRM ammo were depleted extremely rapidly. Initially forced onto the battlefield, the lack of significant brawling weapons led to the mechs suffering enormous losses. This ComStar variant upgraded the capabilities of the Bombardier while at the same time attempting to address one of the primary issues of the design. The LRM launchers have been mated to a pair of Artemis IV Fire Control Systems while the new electronics package includes an improved C3 computer and the amount of ammunition carried was doubled. These changes increased the overall accuracy and effectiveness of the paired LRM-20s and were implemented by sacrificing the SRM launcher and anti-missile system. For close range defense, the 14C carries an ER Small Laser, while the chassis has been upgraded using Endo-Steel.
yangsThoughts: At least it has more ammo and better electronics to make sure it gets better use of that ammo, it also has at least one non-ammo based weapon, so it can do something when it runs out of ammo.

details: Continuing the trend toward specialized BattleMechs, the Bombardier was proposed to the Star League in 2735 by Wakazashi Enterprises as an ArtilleryMech. The concept behind the design was to make a BattleMech that could replace less-mobile artillery vehicles, which might fall behind in an advance or be destroyed by counterbattery fire. It would be well-protected with twelve and a half tons of armor and use specialized missile racks which could carry diverse types of advanced missiles. Over years of testing and field exercises the Bombardier proved itself extremely effective at fulfilling the long-range fire support role, easily surpassing its vehicular counterparts and even inspiring talks of disbanding the SLDF's artillery arm once enough Bombardiers were operational.  This plan failed sharply once the succession wars started, however, as the lack of stable supply lines and safe factory logistics meant that the supplies of more specialized LRM ammo were depleted extremely rapidly. Initially forced onto the battlefield, the lack of significant brawling weapons led to the mechs suffering enormous losses. An upgrade of mothballed BMB-10Ds and -12Ds reintroduced by the Combine in response to the Alliance's -05As, the -14K strips out the weapons and equipment and adds a half ton of armor. Four MML 7s provide firepower at all ranges, though the five tons of ammunition are vulnerable in their arm mount. A C3 slave unit allows the 14K to share targeting telemetry with like-equipped friendly units and three Machine Guns linked to a Machine Gun Array in the right arm can prove devastating to unarmored infantry.
yangsThoughts: This version is a much needed improvement, it's got decent damage at any distance, and machine guns to defend itself at close range.

details: Also called the Atlas III, this mech is the latest generation of the long running line of battlemechs, and it is built with a combination of Inner Sphere and Clan technology. The D3 version includes an advanced ECM suite for protection from enemy threats along with the Atlas' traditional 19.5 tons of armor. For weaponry, it is equipped with a Clan spec LRM 20 backed by a RAC/2 for extreme ranges, and a quartet of X-Pulse lasers and a Clan spec Streak SRM-6 for closer range engagements.
yangsThoughts: It's still the same Atlas concept that has been a terror on the battlefields for the last three hundred years, just now with more advanced weaponry and better electronics. You might want to consider upgrading those X-Pulses to regular clan pulses though to reduce the heat.

details: Also called the Atlas III, this mech is the latest generation of the long running line of battlemechs, and it is built with a combination of Inner Sphere and Clan technology. The D3 version includes an advanced ECM suite for protection from enemy threats along with the Atlas' traditional 19.5 tons of armor. The D2 variant adds a targeting computer but reduces the ecm suites sophistication to compensate. It also mounts a HAG 30 in place of the RAC/2.
yangsThoughts: It's still the same Atlas concept that has been a terror on the battlefields for the last three hundred years, just now with more advanced weaponry and better electronics. The increased accuracy from the targeting computer is nice, though I'm not sure if the reduction in electronics is worth it.

details: The 3M was a basic upgrade of the BattleMaster utilizing Star League technology. Introduced by Earthwerks Incorporated for the Free Worlds League in 3049, the 'Mech replaces the standard heat sinks with double heat sinks, upgrades its primary weapon to a Fusigon Longtooth ER PPC and removes one of the machine guns to add CASE to the design to protect itself in the event of an ammunition explosion.
yangsThoughts: A nice upgrade to a classic mech, this setup has nicer range but no other major changes to its weapons loadout.

details: This variant was designed in 3070 by Clan Jade Falcon scientists following the capture of Red Devil Industries during the FedCom Civil War. This 'Mech is built on an Endo Steel chassis protected by sixteen and a half tons of armor. Based on the 4S, the Clan-tech variant replaces the gauss rifle with a Hyper-Assault Gauss Rifle 30 and four tons of ammunition. All the medium lasers are front facing, with four medium pulse lasers and two ER medium lasers. All of these weapons are guided by a Targeting Computer. The short-range missile launcher is replaced with an ATM 6 and three tons of ammunition to ensure the ability to make use of the flexibility offered by the Advanced Tactical Missile System.
yangsThoughts: I always appreciate it when the Clan does my job for me of improving a mech to the limit.

details: A rarely seen alternative to the standard BattleMaster that was released by Field Arms Systems Associated based on the plans for the standard BattleMaster they obtained through industrial espionage. Only a few variants of this mech were produced before legal troubles forced the manufacturing to stop. Nevertheless it is just as effective a design as the standard BattleMaster and shares many compatible spare parts, making it feasible to maintain for the forces that wound up purchasing it. The standard armament includes a PPC in a rifle style mount, a large number of medium lasers for closer in firepower and an SRM-6 and pair of machine guns for use against anything that gets close enough to be in range.
yangsThoughts: I haven't seen one of these in years, though it's pretty much just a BattleMaster in terms of operation and tactics.

details: A rarely seen alternative to the standard BattleMaster that was released by Field Arms Systems Associates based on the plans for the standard BattleMaster they obtained through industrial espionage. Only a few variants of this mech were produced before legal troubles forced the manufacturing to stop. Nevertheless it is just as effective a design as the standard BattleMaster and shares many compatible spare parts, making it feasible to maintain for the forces that wound up purchasing it. The 3M variant was apparently the most expensive variant sold by Field Arms Systems, using an ER PPC and double heatsinks, the price meant that very few examples of this were built before the legal troubles ended manufacturing of the mech for good.
yangsThoughts: A nice range upgrade to the standard layout, it's a pity that they didn't manage to make more of them before those lawsuits hit.

details: A rarely seen alternative to the standard Warhammer that was released by Field Arms Systems Associates based on the plans for the standard Warhammer they obtained through industrial espionage. Only a few variants of this mech were produced before legal troubles forced the manufacturing to stop. Nevertheless it is just as effective a design as the standard Warhammer and shares many compatible spare parts, making it feasible to maintain for the forces that wound up purchasing it. The 6R variant was identical in layout to the standard Warhammer loadout of the same model designation, a fact that was instrumental in the lawsuit proving the espionage claims.
yangsThoughts: I haven't seen this mech in years, if you don't know better you'd think it was just a normal Warhammer until you look closely. It does pretty much everything that the regular warhammer does.

details: A rarely seen alternative to the standard Warhammer that was released by Field Arms Systems Associates based on the plans for the standard Warhammer they obtained through industrial espionage. Only a few variants of this mech were produced before legal troubles forced the manufacturing to stop. Nevertheless it is just as effective a design as the standard WarHammer and shares many compatible spare parts, making it feasible to maintain for the forces that wound up purchasing it. The 7M variant was apparently the most expensive variant sold by Field Arms Systems, using ER PPCs and double heatsinks, the price meant that very few examples of this were built before the legal troubles ended manufacturing of the mech for good.
yangsThoughts: A nice range upgrade to the standard layout, it's a pity that they didn't manage to make more of them before those lawsuits hit.

details: This variant was developed by ComStar to more easily traverse the difficult terrain and engage in the ambush tactics intended for the Battle of Tukayyid. The PPCs are replaced by Fusigon Longtooth extended-range models and the heat sinks are traded for double strength models. The secondary weapons of the WHM-6R are removed. Two Martel medium lasers are in each side torso, and the SRM-6 has been replaced by three Hovertec Streak SRM-2 launchers. A ton of Streak ammo is carried in the CASE-protected right torso. Finally four Leviathon Plus jump jets are installed in the legs, allowing this Warhammer to quickly reposition itself within 120 meters. To make room for all these modifications, the WHM-7CS uses a VOX 280 XL engine.
yangsThoughts: A nice, if rare, upgrade made by ComStar, able to work much better without overheating, much better range and more armored, and it can jump too.

details: A Clan-tech retrofit of the venerable Warhammer intended for second-line and solahma troops, this variant replaced most of the Inner Sphere-tech weaponry of the original WHM-6R with Clan equivalents and used the weight savings to add two more heat sinks. The PPCs were replaced with Large Pulse Lasers, the SRM-6 was upgraded to a Streak SRM-6, and all of the lasers were upgraded to ER medium and ER small lasers. Only the machine guns were not replaced with Clan-tech models.
yangsThoughts: Even without Clan double heat sinks, this mech is better at pretty much everything compared to the standard Warhammer, especially with the change from PPCs to Pulse Lasers.

details: A Golden Century refit popular with Wolf's Dragoons, this Warhammer was rebuilt from the ground up with Clan technology. Built using an endo steel internal structure and XL engine, the C 3 has plenty of space for weapons and heat sinks. An ER PPC is carried in each arm. Both side torsos carry an ER Medium Laser, a Medium Pulse Laser, and a Streak SRM-6. An ECM Suite protects this variant, while a Targeting Computer makes the already fearsome weaponry even more dangerous. 17 double heat sinks cannot keep the 'Mech cool unless the pilot fires either its long range or short range weapons.
yangsThoughts: The ideal distillation of Warhammer design in my view, this takes everything that the core Warhammer does best and does it better. Hits harder, farther, and with more armor.

details: Named for the ancient Japanese longbow and first produced in 3055, the Daikyu is armed to strike an enemy with a powerful blow at long range and, as such, is poorly armed for short-range combat. The Daikyu attempts to dictate the range at which it engages with its top speed of 86.4 km/h, which allows the Daikyu to keep up with most medium and heavy 'Mechs and makes it capable of outpacing most assault 'Mechs. The Daikyu is built around long-range firepower. The primary weapon the 'Mech carries is a Lord's Light 2 ER PPC backed up by a pair of Imperator Ultra autocannon/5s. This combination of weapons gives the Daikyu a devastating long-range punch. The 'Mech also carries a LongFire V LRM-5 launcher, giving it limited indirect fire support capabilities. For close combat, the Daikyu carries a single Diverse Optics Type 20 medium laser.
yangsThoughts: A nice and fast heavy mech version, this mech is a bit lacking in terms of close range firepower, but otherwise very well armed.

details: Named for the ancient Japanese longbow and first produced in 3055, the Daikyu is armed to strike an enemy with a powerful blow at long range and, as such, is poorly armed for short-range combat. The Daikyu attempts to dictate the range at which it engages with its top speed of 86.4 km/h, which allows the Daikyu to keep up with most medium and heavy 'Mechs and makes it capable of outpacing most assault 'Mechs. A variant made to address concerns of a lack of short-range firepower, the 02 variant was built soon after Operation Bulldog. The 'Mech carries a pair of Streak SRM-6 launchers in place of the two Ultra autocannon/5s. Additionally, for close combat, the 02 variant of the Daikyu has two ER medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: Much better close combat armament, but missing a lot of the long range blasting power that made the original useful.

details: Based on the King Crab, and built in the same factory that first produced the Mackie, the Vanquisher has a legendary pedigree. The Vanquisher has its genesis in the development of reverse engineered Improved C3 Computers. Once the Word of Blake had perfected their version of C3i, Cameron St. Jamais contacted Skobel MechWorks and contracted them to produce a 'Mech that would showcase the features of the new system. The Vanquisher was the result of that request. Intended to stay back and let faster units spot for it using the C3i system, the Vanquisher carries a pair of Corean Light Gauss Rifles backed up by two Diverse Optics Sunbeam ER Large Lasers, giving the Vanquisher almost unparalleled reach on the battlefield. For close combat, the 'Mech has three Diverse Optics Type ER Medium Lasers, which are usually enough to dissuade most lighter 'Mechs.
yangsThoughts: A long range sniper mech, don't expect this thing to get anywhere in a hurry, but it does have a nicely powerful long range weapon array to make sure it doesn't have to.

details: Based on the King Crab, and built in the same factory that first produced the Mackie, the Vanquisher has a legendary pedigree. The Vanquisher has its genesis in the development of reverse engineered Improved C3 Computers. Once the Word of Blake had perfected their version of C3i, Cameron St. Jamais contacted Skobel MechWorks and contracted them to produce a 'Mech that would showcase the features of the new system. The Vanquisher was the result of that request. The 2B variant of the Vanquisher makes up roughly fifteen percent of the current production run. This variant carries four Ultra Autocannon/5s in place of the Light Gauss Rifles and ER Large Lasers. While this variant is much more reliant on ammunition-based weapons, the four Ultra Autocannon/5s allow the 'Mech to put more fire downrange at a somewhat shorter range envelope than the 2A variant.
yangsThoughts: More ballistic mounts but less energy mounts mean that you're really dependant on the ammo for this one, but its great for getting a lot of bullets onto the field fast.

details: Based on the King Crab, and built in the same factory that first produced the Mackie, the Vanquisher has a legendary pedigree. The Vanquisher has its genesis in the development of reverse engineered Improved C3 Computers. Once the Word of Blake had perfected their version of C3i, Cameron St. Jamais contacted Skobel MechWorks and contracted them to produce a 'Mech that would showcase the features of the new system. The Vanquisher was the result of that request. The 7V variant uses a pair of Rotary AC/5s along with a set of two light PPCs and a Heavy PPC.
yangsThoughts: PPC and bullet heavy, but very good at quickly ripping foes apart if you can get them into your crosshairs.

details: The Wight's weapon array suits its role as a short ranged brawler, based around one of the newly introduced Lord's Light heavy PPCs in the right torso, backed up by dual mounted Diverse Optics ER medium lasers. Its all energy weapon loadout allows the 'Mech to stay in action as long as its bulky armor and its pilot's endurance will allow.
yangsThoughts: Not particularly fast for a light mech, but it's got enough durability to bring that big heavy ppc to bear on enemies.

details: Despite sharing the cost of the design, StarCorps has never produced the base model, blasting LAW for its low mobility by light 'Mech standards. StarCorp's base version of the Wight instead uses standard ferro-fibrous armor and carries a snub-nose PPC as its main weapon to allow it to mount jump jets, trading firepower for mobility.
yangsThoughts: It's more maneuverable, sure, but that spread out damage from the snub nose ppc really hurts it's ability to kill things efficiently.

details: LAW's response to StarCorps' scathing review, this enhanced mobility Wight variant greatly downgrades its protection and firepower by using light ferro-fibrous armor and a light PPC to free up the weight needed for improved jump jets.
yangsThoughts: It's even more maneuverable in this form, but what's the point when you've lost that much firepower? I feel like the solution to this is ultimately to switch to a Clan-spec ER PPC so you can get both the damage and the maneuverability.

details: The Anvil was originally intended to act as a brawler BattleMech in its original design and testing phase. The Anvil carries a powerful short range arsenal. The primary weapons are a pair of Sutel Precision Line Large Pulse Lasers which provide powerful medium to long range firepower. The short range firepower is bolstered with a pair of Martell medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: A decently fast blasting mech, but it's not amazingly high in firepower. You might want to consider downgrading the large pulses to mediums to free up more weight for other weapons.

details: Looking to increase the long range firepower of the Anvil, the 5M replaces the Large Pulse Lasers with ER Large Lasers while adding two more medium lasers and the full complement of jump jets to the 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: Overall I like this setup better, the higher damage makes it way better at blasting, even if it does run quite hot.

details: A massive close in brawling mech, with a gigantic 400XL engine and a MASC system to allow it to reach a top speed of 86.4 km/h. It runs an arsenal of energy weapons for ranged operations while it gets in range for its massive 7 ton hatchet. For long range it uses an ER PPC then twin large pulse lasers for closer in work. It does mount a flamer in the head but more for the psychological effect than any actual combat use.
yangsThoughts: Basically an axman or hatchetman but even bigger, this thing is a monster in melee and it's at least got enough firepower to blow up the things it can't catch.

details: The B3 variant is a modification of the Berserker that changes the 'Mech from a powerful brawler to a well-armed fire support 'Mech. The twin large pulse lasers have been replaced with a pair of Coventry StarFire LRM-10 launchers which allow the 'Mech to sit at long ranges and bombard the enemy. If a 'Mech closes to short range, the Berserker can act as its own bodyguard by using its hatchet.
yangsThoughts: Not a hugely effective firesupport mech for the weight, but at least it has a lot of armor and can defend itself well with the hatchet.

details: The Caesar was built to do as much damage to an enemy at as great a stand-off range as possible. The 'Mech therefore carries primarily long-ranged armament.   The primary long-ranged weapon on the Caesar is a Poland Main Model A Gauss rifle that can shear off just under a ton of armor with each shot, fed from a 16-round magazine. This is backed up at long ranges by a Johnston High Speed ER PPC, which can do two-thirds of the damage of the Gauss rifle but is not dependent on limited ammunition. For close-range engagements, the Caesar carries four Sutel Precision Line medium pulse lasers, allowing it to put up a crippling defense against any enemies that can get close to the 'Mech. The large volume of heat sinks carried ensure the Caesar can fire all its weapons while hardly breaking a sweat, making it a popular choice for missions in areas of extremely high temperatures.
yangsThoughts: A good long range sniper mech, it might be wise to replace a pulse laser for more armor if you can't keep the enemy away from it

details: The Caesar was built to do as much damage to an enemy at as great a stand-off range as possible. The 'Mech therefore carries primarily long-ranged armament. A variant that first saw action during the Jihad, the -4R attempts to improve upon the -3R variant marketed in the Federated Suns. Designed with short-range combat in mind, it drops the rear-facing pulse lasers and three heat sinks in favor of additional armor and three ER medium lasers. MASC enables the Caesar to close more quickly and bring its snub-nose PPC to bear. The large volume of heat sinks carried ensure the Caesar can fire all its weapons while hardly breaking a sweat, making it a popular choice for missions in areas of extremely high temperatures.
yangsThoughts: A strange build, this one changes to be mostly close range focused, though it still keeps the gauss rifle for sniping.

details: Originally conceived of in the twenty-eighth century as a part of the same development project that birthed the Marauder, the Dragon Fire didn't initially make the transition from a design on paper to an actual production model. The discovery of the Gray Death Memory Core gave the Dragon Fire a new lease on life and the 'Mech first saw production in late 3058. The primary weapon on the Dragon Fire is a Zeus Slingshot Gauss rifle that provides excellent long range firepower. This is backed up by a Mydron Excel LB-X Autocannon/10, which is capable of firing both standard and cluster ammunition, making the Dragon Fire a capable vehicle hunter and anti-aircraft 'Mech. An Exostar Large Laser provides secondary firepower to the Dragon Fire's ammunition dependent weapons. For close combat, the Dragon Fire carries a pair of Defiance B4M Medium Pulse Lasers.
yangsThoughts: Good at all ranges, it could really use an upgrade to that large laser to give it a bit more long range firepower.

details: Exclusively manufactured for the Word of Blake, this version of the Dragon Fire carries the ER large laser of the 4F but downgrades the medium pulse lasers to extended-range versions. Most of the hitting power comes from a Gauss rifle and an LB 10-X autocannon. It also carries an extra heat sink and a C3i computer to coordinate attacks with its fellows; the ECM suite is removed.
yangsThoughts: Good at pretty much any range like the 3F, this version adds more coordination ability with its lancemates.

details: Originally conceived of in the twenty-eighth century as a part of the same development project that birthed the Marauder, the Dragon Fire didn't initially make the transition from a design on paper to an actual production model. The discovery of the Gray Death Memory Core gave the Dragon Fire a new lease on life and the 'Mech first saw production in late 3058. This Dark Age era variant of the Dragon Fire was introduced by General Motors as a pocket assault. A downgraded Pitban 225 XL, endo-composite internals, and an XL gyro made room for a heavier arsenal. In addition to the Zeus Slingshot Gauss, it carries a Mydron Tornado rotary AC/5 and a range of ExoStar Pinnacle Clan-spec energy weapons: an ER PPC, an ER large laser, and two ER medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: Strange that they put all the Clan-spec energy weapons but they didn't put a Clan-spec Gauss, if we have one spare we could fix that oversight and upgrade some of the energy weapons with the spare tonnage.

details: The Enfield was originally designed to replace the Wyvern as an urban combat 'Mech, but the design never made it to mass production. The plans for the Enfield were lost during the Succession Wars and were recovered by the Federated Suns on the world of Achernar in 2870. Knowledge of the design was kept hidden until the 3050s, when the NAIS selected the Enfield as a companion 'Mech to the Enforcer ENF-5D. The Enfield thus began production in 3055 and has seen heavy deployment to the Lyran Alliance because of the constant threat presented by the Clans. The Enfield carries a Defiance Disintegrator LB 10-X AC as its primary weapon, capable of firing both solid and cluster ammunition, making it a highly effective anti-aircraft weapon. For close combat, the Enfield has a Thunderbolt-12 large pulse laser, two Defiance B3M medium lasers, and a ChisComp 32 small laser.
yangsThoughts: A nice brawler medium, solid all around, though it could possibly use a bit more range for battles in more open areas.

details: The Enfield was originally designed to replace the Wyvern as an urban combat 'Mech, but the design never made it to mass production. The plans for the Enfield were lost during the Succession Wars and were recovered by the Federated Suns on the world of Achernar in 2870. Knowledge of the design was kept hidden until the 3050s, when the NAIS selected the Enfield as a companion 'Mech to the Enforcer ENF-5D. The Enfield thus began production in 3055 and has seen heavy deployment to the Lyran Alliance because of the constant threat presented by the Clans. In the Golden Century Clan Mongoose replaced the autocannon with a prototype UAC/10 and an improved large laser.
yangsThoughts: A nice improvement in firepower overall with that ultra ac, might as well finish upgrading the weapons to clan spec though.

details: The Gunslinger is a product of the Clan Invasion and would not exist if not for that violent conflict. The 'Mech was jointly designed by engineers from both the Draconis Combine and the Federated Commonwealth who were in the process of assisting the Free Rasalhague Republic. To power the Gunslinger, a VOX 255 XL engine is used to give the 'Mech a top speed of 54 km/h. This is augmented by a pair of HildCo Model 12 jump jets that allow the Gunslinger to traverse rough terrain and provide extra mobility at close ranges. The 'Mech is also equipped with a Guardian ECM Suite and a Beagle Active Probe, giving the Gunslinger superior detection capabilities and protection from advanced enemy electronic warfare equipment. The Gunslinger carries as its primary weapons two arm-mounted Poland Main Model A Gauss rifles that can each strip off a ton of armor at ranges of over six hundred meters. As the Gunslinger closes with the enemy it is able to bring to bear its four Defiance B3M medium lasers and a Defiance A5L Small Laser that help to make up for the ineffectiveness of the Gauss rifles at extremely short ranges.
yangsThoughts: I think this is a new one for me, leg mounted lasers, other than those it's a very efficient and powerful sniper, similar to the Highlander but with more Gausses and no missiles.

details: The Gunslinger is a product of the Clan Invasion and would not exist if not for that violent conflict. The 'Mech was jointly designed by engineers from both the Draconis Combine and the Federated Commonwealth who were in the process of assisting the Free Rasalhague Republic. To power the Gunslinger, a VOX 255 XL engine is used to give the 'Mech a top speed of 54 km/h. This is augmented by a pair of HildCo Model 12 jump jets that allow the Gunslinger to traverse rough terrain and provide extra mobility at close ranges. The 'Mech is also equipped with a Guardian ECM Suite and a Beagle Active Probe, giving the Gunslinger superior detection capabilities and protection from advanced enemy electronic warfare equipment. A Dark Age Era variant introduced in 3131 by the Draconis Combine, this version of the Gunslinger uses a mix of Clan and Inner Sphere technologies. Powered by a 255-rated Clan XL Fusion Engine, the 'Mech retains the speed and limited Jump Jet capacity of the original, while the gyro is replaced by an XL version. Weapons-wise, its primary weapons remain its arm mounted Gauss rifles with four tons of ammunition. The four Medium Lasers in the side torsos are replaced by Clan ER Medium Lasers, while three X-Pulse Small Lasers are fitted.
yangsThoughts: I think this is a new one for me, leg mounted lasers, other than those it's a very efficient and powerful sniper, similar to the Highlander but with more Gausses and no missiles.

details: With a military that utilized expensive assault 'Mechs like the Emperor, Pillager, and Victor in large numbers, the St. Ives Compact had a distinct need to protect those investments. To do this, the St. Ives Compact worked with engineers at Ceres Metals Industries to design a 'Mech that could both protect and support its heavier charges. The Helios is also produced by Ceres Metals for use by ComStar due to the secular organization quietly financing an increase in the number of production lines for the 'Mech to four. The Helios carries a MilDouglas Gauss rifle as its primary weapon. This allows the Helios to strike an enemy at ranges similar to that of the 'Mechs it protects with respectable firepower. For close combat, the Helios has two BlazeFire Longshot ER medium lasers and a pair of MilDouglas SRM-6 launchers which can usually make short work of an enemy 'Mech that makes it into short range.
yangsThoughts: A nice flexible heavy mech that can keep up with most combat. Armor is a bit light, but it's still pretty durable.

details: With a military that utilized expensive assault 'Mechs like the Emperor, Pillager, and Victor in large numbers, the St. Ives Compact had a distinct need to protect those investments. To do this, the St. Ives Compact worked with engineers at Ceres Metals Industries to design a 'Mech that could both protect and support its heavier charges. The Helios is also produced by Ceres Metals for use by ComStar due to the secular organization quietly financing an increase in the number of production lines for the 'Mech to four. A more energy weapon dependent version of the Helios spawned by the Combine trade deal, the 4A strips all of the 'Mech's weapons and adds two tons of armor. It has an ER PPC as its primary weapon backed up by two medium lasers and an MRM-20 launcher.
yangsThoughts: I like the MRM loadout a bit better than the SRMs as it lets the thing stay a bit farther away from bigger mechs that go in for close range brawling.

details: The product of what was learned from captured Loki and Thor OmniMechs, the Sunder is the assault class 'Mech of the Draconis Combine's first generation of Inner Sphere OmniMechs. Much like Avatar, the Sunder stems from Combine engineers efforts to re-engineer captured Clan OmniMechs for manufacture, selecting examples of the Loki and Thor to embody an assault class design. The Combine engineers rightly determined the legs of both progenitor designs could easily support twenty extra tons, but while the internal frame was able to accommodate the extra weight the leg actuators of prototype Sunders burned out at an alarmingly rapid rate, forcing significant rework with more robust versions. The Sunder weighs ninety tons and uses a Hermes 360 XL engine to save weight while giving the 'Mech a top speed of 65 km/h, the bulky engine forcing the designers to move the torso OmniPod bays above the cockpit. The 'Mech carries two Large Lasers for its primary long-range weapons. For short-range engagements, the Sunder has an Autocannon/20 that comprises the bulk of its firepower. This is supported by two medium lasers and a trio of SRM-4 launchers, making the Sunder prime most lethal at short ranges.
yangsThoughts: A pretty conservative but decent weapons mix, it definitely suffers from the heavier weight of Inner Sphere weapons. If you've got some Clan-spec gear available, you can definitely get way more out of the thing.

details: The product of what was learned from captured Loki and Thor OmniMechs, the Sunder is the assault class 'Mech of the Draconis Combine's first generation of Inner Sphere OmniMechs. Much like Avatar, the Sunder stems from Combine engineers efforts to re-engineer captured Clan OmniMechs for manufacture, selecting examples of the Loki and Thor to embody an assault class design. The Combine engineers rightly determined the legs of both progenitor designs could easily support twenty extra tons, but while the internal frame was able to accommodate the extra weight the leg actuators of prototype Sunders burned out at an alarmingly rapid rate, forcing significant rework with more robust versions. The Sunder weighs ninety tons and uses a Hermes 360 XL engine to save weight while giving the 'Mech a top speed of 65 km/h, the bulky engine forcing the designers to move the torso OmniPod bays above the cockpit. The Sunder A is armed primarily for long-range engagements. The primary weapon is an ER PPC supported by a Gauss rifle for direct fire engagements. The Sunder A carries three LRM-5 launchers, allowing it to attack an enemy with indirect missile fire in support of allied units. For close range defense, the Sunder A has two Medium Pulse Lasers, which provide enough firepower to defend itself from lighter units at close ranges.
yangsThoughts: Another decent build held back by Inner Sphere weapons, swap those LRM's for Clan-spec ones and you can literally double the missile firepower for the same weight.

details: The product of what was learned from captured Loki and Thor OmniMechs, the Sunder is the assault class 'Mech of the Draconis Combine's first generation of Inner Sphere OmniMechs. Much like Avatar, the Sunder stems from Combine engineers efforts to re-engineer captured Clan OmniMechs for manufacture, selecting examples of the Loki and Thor to embody an assault class design. The Combine engineers rightly determined the legs of both progenitor designs could easily support twenty extra tons, but while the internal frame was able to accommodate the extra weight the leg actuators of prototype Sunders burned out at an alarmingly rapid rate, forcing significant rework with more robust versions. The Sunder weighs ninety tons and uses a Hermes 360 XL engine to save weight while giving the 'Mech a top speed of 65 km/h, the bulky engine forcing the designers to move the torso OmniPod bays above the cockpit. This version is an upgrade of the primary configuration, the use of Endo Steel structure allows the replacement of the SRM 4s with two additional Large Lasers, bringing the total to four, accompanied by additional Double Heat Sinks. Additionally, the Autocannon/20 is upgraded to a LB-X version.
yangsThoughts: Only one ammunition dependant weapon here and another 4.5 tons of pod space, this seems like the best of the Sunder variants for actually customizing.

details: The Viking began production shortly after the Beowulf in a joint effort by the Free Rasalhague Republic and ComStar. The 'Mech is intended to take over in the role that has been traditionally filled by the Bombardier. The Viking is built on a GC Type 1 Endo Steel chassis and powered by a GM 270 fusion engine, giving the 'Mech a top speed of 54.0 km/h. The Viking is protected by fifteen and a half tons of GC Slab armor with CASE, providing the 'Mech with good armor protection as well as protection against ammunition explosions. To dissipate heat from the Viking's impressive arsenal, the 'Mech has ten double heat sinks. The Viking has proven itself a powerful assault and fire support 'Mech capable of putting out almost twice as much firepower as the 'Mech it replaces. The Viking has a singular mission of long-range missile fire support. To do this, the Viking carries two Shigunga LRM-15 launchers and two Shigunga LRM-20 launchers, all of which are linked to an Artemis IV fire control system for increased missile accuracy. For close combat, the Viking has two torso mounted ChisComp 32 Small Lasers and four Sperry Browning Machine Guns for anti-infantry combat.
yangsThoughts: A powerhouse of long range missile support, it can hammer targets with a lot of missiles. If you've got any Clan-Spec grade LRMs to spare, you can use them to make this thing an absolute beast, or go for MRMs for brawling.

details: The Viking began production shortly after the Beowulf in a joint effort by the Free Rasalhague Republic and ComStar. The 'Mech is intended to take over in the role that has been traditionally filled by the Bombardier. The Viking is built on a GC Type 1 Endo Steel chassis and powered by a GM 270 fusion engine, giving the 'Mech a top speed of 54.0 km/h. The Viking is protected by fifteen and a half tons of GC Slab armor with CASE, providing the 'Mech with good armor protection as well as protection against ammunition explosions. To dissipate heat from the Viking's impressive arsenal, the 'Mech has ten double heat sinks. The Viking has proven itself a powerful assault and fire support 'Mech capable of putting out almost twice as much firepower as the 'Mech it replaces. Intended to act as a front line assault 'Mech, the 2G variant of the Viking has all of the weapons stripped out and two more double heat sinks have been added in addition to two more tons of armor. The 2G carries as its primary weapons an LB-X Autocannon/10 which is capable of firing both standard and cluster rounds as well as a Gauss rifle, giving the 2G a powerful long range punch. For medium to close range combat, the 2G has a Large Pulse Laser and three Medium Pulse Lasers. To protect itself from advanced enemy electronic warfare systems, the 2G has a Guardian ECM Suite.
yangsThoughts: I'm honestly not sure why they made this thing, sure it's got some decent brawling power, but there's plenty of other mechs that can fill this role, so why force a specialized fire support mech into it?

details: The designers at Kallon Industries used the Black Hawk Clan OmniMech as the basis for the Penetrator. The Penetrator has an all energy weapons arsenal and uses twelve double heat sinks to handle the massive heat load from its weapons. The 'Mech is lacking in speed though when compared to the Black Hawk. This is because the 'Mechs designers used a Vlar 300 fusion engine for its combat durability, giving the 'Mech a top speed of 64.8 km/h. To help offset the 'Mech's lack of mobility, the Penetrator has four Anderson 398 jump jets giving it added mobility with a jumping distance of one hundred and twenty meters. The Penetrator also carries a respectable thirteen tons of armor. The 'Mech's heavy armor protection, combined with its standard engine and unlimited amounts of ammunition, make for a 'Mech that is very rugged and hard to kill. The first Penetrators started coming off the production line in 3053. The Penetrator carries a blistering laser array. The primary weapons are a pair of arm mounted Diverse Optics Sunbeam ER Large Lasers for long range firepower. For close range combat, the 'Mech carries six Sutel Precision Line Medium Pulse Lasers which are mounted in its right and left torsos. Finally, the 'Mech carries its only ammunition-based weapon, a Yori Flyswatter anti-missile system, for added defense against missile attacks.
yangsThoughts: Well, it's very slightly better at handling heat than the Black Hawk I guess, it's still awful at overheating though. You might want to remove some of the lasers for more heat stability.

details: The designers at Kallon Industries used the Black Hawk Clan OmniMech as the basis for the Penetrator. The Penetrator has an all energy weapons arsenal and uses twelve double heat sinks to handle the massive heat load from its weapons. The 'Mech is lacking in speed though when compared to the Black Hawk. This is because the 'Mechs designers used a Vlar 300 fusion engine for its combat durability, giving the 'Mech a top speed of 64.8 km/h. To help offset the 'Mech's lack of mobility, the Penetrator has four Anderson 398 jump jets giving it added mobility with a jumping distance of one hundred and twenty meters. The Penetrator also carries a respectable thirteen tons of armor. The 'Mech's heavy armor protection, combined with its standard engine and unlimited amounts of ammunition, make for a 'Mech that is very rugged and hard to kill. The first Penetrators started coming off the production line in 3053. Intended to act as a fire support 'Mech, The PTR-4F Penetrator sacrifices two of its Medium Pulse Lasers and the ER Large Lasers, freeing enough tonnage to add two LRM-10 missile launchers. The LRM-10s were then mated to Artemis IV fire control systems for added accuracy.
yangsThoughts: An interesting rebuild, it swaps out the energy hardpoints in the arms for missiles, which allows for some interesting builds, but you'll have to deal with ammo supplies.

details: Designed by engineers at the New Avalon Institute of Science in an attempt to copy the Mad Cat, while not as powerful as the OmniMech it emulates, the Rakshasa is a formidable 'Mech with an undeserved bad reputation.  Since the Inner Sphere first saw the Mad Cat they could only marvel at such a potent mix of speed, armor and firepower and how they themselves could produce a copy of such a design. Unlike the Draconis Combine whose reverse engineering efforts ultimately focused on the Clans OmniPod technology, technicians and students at the New Avalon Institute of Science labored for five long years attempting to specifically replicate the Mad Cat and its iconic primary configuration, rumors insisting the final breakthrough being the in-depth analysis of a captured Mad Cat. Its designers settled on calling the result the Rakshasa after a mythical creature that mimicked its adversaries abilities and appearance. The Rakshasa is powered by a GM 375 XL engine, giving it comparable ground speed to the 'Mech it attempts to emulate, and built on a GM Marauder Special MD Endo Steel chassis to save weight and allow the 'Mech to carry as similar a weapons payload as possible to the Mad Cat. The Rakshasa also carries eleven and a half tons of StarGuard ferro-fibrous armor giving it slightly less than maximum armor protection. A total of fifteen double heat sinks are woefully inadequate for the Rakshasa's heavy energy weapon loadout, requiring careful heat management on part of its pilot. Along with its visual similarity, offensively the Rakshasa tries to clone the weapons array of the Mad Cat Prime with boxy LRM launcher torsos supported by double barrelled laser-carrying arms.
yangsThoughts: I get what they were trying for with immitating the Timber Wolf, but the lack of Clan-Spec weaponry really hinders this mech, the obvious solution being to actually give it the weapons loadout of the mech it's trying to be if we have any spare.

details: Designed by engineers at the New Avalon Institute of Science in an attempt to copy the Mad Cat, while not as powerful as the OmniMech it emulates, the Rakshasa is a formidable 'Mech with an undeserved bad reputation.  Since the Inner Sphere first saw the Mad Cat they could only marvel at such a potent mix of speed, armor and firepower and how they themselves could produce a copy of such a design. Unlike the Draconis Combine whose reverse engineering efforts ultimately focused on the Clans OmniPod technology, technicians and students at the New Avalon Institute of Science labored for five long years attempting to specifically replicate the Mad Cat and its iconic primary configuration, rumors insisting the final breakthrough being the in-depth analysis of a captured Mad Cat. Its designers settled on calling the result the Rakshasa after a mythical creature that mimicked its adversaries abilities and appearance. The Rakshasa is powered by a GM 375 XL engine, giving it comparable ground speed to the 'Mech it attempts to emulate, and built on a GM Marauder Special MD Endo Steel chassis to save weight and allow the 'Mech to carry as similar a weapons payload as possible to the Mad Cat. The Rakshasa also carries eleven and a half tons of StarGuard ferro-fibrous armor giving it slightly less than maximum armor protection. A total of fifteen double heat sinks are woefully inadequate for the Rakshasa's heavy energy weapon loadout, requiring careful heat management on part of its pilot. This Jihad era field refit drops all the weapons except the medium lasers. In their place are a pair of Snub-Nose PPCs in the arms and a pair of MML-7s in the left and right torsos.
yangsThoughts: Now this is a better setup, instead of trying to just blindly copy something you can't properly emulate, actually try to do something different.

details: Designed by engineers at the New Avalon Institute of Science in an attempt to copy the Mad Cat, while not as powerful as the OmniMech it emulates, the Rakshasa is a formidable 'Mech with an undeserved bad reputation.  Since the Inner Sphere first saw the Mad Cat they could only marvel at such a potent mix of speed, armor and firepower and how they themselves could produce a copy of such a design. Unlike the Draconis Combine whose reverse engineering efforts ultimately focused on the Clans OmniPod technology, technicians and students at the New Avalon Institute of Science labored for five long years attempting to specifically replicate the Mad Cat and its iconic primary configuration, rumors insisting the final breakthrough being the in-depth analysis of a captured Mad Cat. Its designers settled on calling the result the Rakshasa after a mythical creature that mimicked its adversaries abilities and appearance. The Rakshasa is powered by a GM 375 XL engine, giving it comparable ground speed to the 'Mech it attempts to emulate, and built on a GM Marauder Special MD Endo Steel chassis to save weight and allow the 'Mech to carry as similar a weapons payload as possible to the Mad Cat. The Rakshasa also carries eleven and a half tons of StarGuard ferro-fibrous armor giving it slightly less than maximum armor protection. A total of fifteen double heat sinks are woefully inadequate for the Rakshasa's heavy energy weapon loadout, requiring careful heat management on part of its pilot. A departure from the attempt to emulate the Mad Cat, the factory built 2A model removes the 'Mechs arsenal and upgrades it with a new weapons mix geared toward close combat. The primary weapon is a rotary autocannon/5. This is backed up at shorter ranges by a Large Pulse Laser and for point-blank combat, the 'Mech carries four Medium Pulse Lasers.
yangsThoughts: Another redesign, this time a production version, that stops trying to be a Timber Wolf and starts trying to be something different and effective.

details: The Salamander is an assault fire support 'Mech that is built to carry out a mission similar to the Awesome: long range fire support and bombardment. The Salamander is powered by a Pitban 320 XL engine and is built on a Chariot Type IV Endo Steel chassis, allowing the 'Mech to dedicate over half of its weight to its weapons and the equipment needed to carry out its mission. The Salamander is armored with fifteen and a half tons of armor, which is heavy even for an assault class fire support 'Mech.  The Salamander's arsenal centers around its primary long-range weapons systems; three Doombud LRM-20 Racks. This heavy LRM payload allows the Salamander to put out one and a half times the downrange fire of an Archer. Additionally, the Salamander can sustain a barrage for three minutes at its maximum rate of fire, which is an impressive feat for a dedicated fire support 'Mech. For close range defense, the 'Mech carries two Defiance B3M medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: A nice powerful long range fire support mech, basically an Archer on steroids. It's kind of slow, but no worse than the Archer, and that massive barrage is really useful. If you have the funds for it, I'd love to see what this baby can do with a full set of Clan-spec missile racks.

details: The Salamander is an assault fire support 'Mech that is built to carry out a mission similar to the Awesome: long range fire support and bombardment. The Salamander is powered by a Pitban 320 XL engine and is built on a Chariot Type IV Endo Steel chassis, allowing the 'Mech to dedicate over half of its weight to its weapons and the equipment needed to carry out its mission. The Salamander is armored with fifteen and a half tons of armor, which is heavy even for an assault class fire support 'Mech. The 6T model also makes the Salamander more powerful at close range. One of the LRM-20 launchers have been removed to make room for a pair of medium pulse lasers and a large pulse laser. This makes the Salamander very potent if forced into a short-range firefight.
yangsThoughts: It's still got most of the hardpoints for missiles, even if it's only using two of them. This thing has enough short range firepower to be a serious threat to anything that gets too close.

details: The Thunder is a product of the aftermath of the Fourth Succession War. The 'Mech was built to make the most of new technologies that had made their way to the Capellan Confederation at the beginning of the 3050s. This was done by building the 'Mech around a Magna 350 XL engine to maximize the available weight in the 'Mech. The Thunder also uses double heat sinks to make it capable of handling a high heat load from its weapons systems. The 'Mech is well protected with thirteen and a half tons of armor. Its weapons payload, and the design in general, uses a mix of advanced and off the shelf parts. This balanced mix makes the Thunder a valuable asset on the modern battlefield. The primary weapon on the Thunder is the massive Kali Yama Big Bore Autocannon/20 which is capable of devastating most lighter 'Mechs with a single shot. This is backed up with three highly accurate Ceres Arms Model W Medium Pulse Lasers. Finally, for token long range defense capabilities, and giving the 'Mech limited indirect fire capabilities, the Thunder carries a Sian/Ceres Jaguar LRM-5 launcher.
yangsThoughts: It can mount a decent weapons array and is nice and fast, only real problem is that it could use a weaponry refresh to bring it up to more recent standards.

details: The Thunder is a product of the aftermath of the Fourth Succession War. The 'Mech was built to make the most of new technologies that had made their way to the Capellan Confederation at the beginning of the 3050s. This was done by building the 'Mech around a Magna 350 XL engine to maximize the available weight in the 'Mech. The Thunder also uses double heat sinks to make it capable of handling a high heat load from its weapons systems. The 'Mech is well protected with thirteen and a half tons of armor. Its weapons payload, and the design in general, uses a mix of advanced and off the shelf parts. This balanced mix makes the Thunder a valuable asset on the modern battlefield. The 2L model of the Thunder upgrades the design with some of the latest technology, making it a highly destructive close combat 'Mech. The Autocannon/20 has been replaced with an LB 20-X AC which acts like a giant 'Mech-scale shotgun. The 'Mech is also armed with two ER Medium Lasers and an ER Small Laser. Additionally, the LRM-5 has been dropped in favor of a Streak SRM-6 launcher for additional close range firepower. The final touch on the 'Mech is addition of a Guardian ECM suite which makes the 'Mech much harder for an enemy unit to target.
yangsThoughts: This version has gotten that weaponry refresh and is overall better at close range, though the lack of any significant long range firepower means you'll have to be cautious to avoid getting sniped.

details: The Thunder is a product of the aftermath of the Fourth Succession War. The 'Mech was built to make the most of new technologies that had made their way to the Capellan Confederation at the beginning of the 3050s. This was done by building the 'Mech around a Magna 350 XL engine to maximize the available weight in the 'Mech. The Thunder also uses double heat sinks to make it capable of handling a high heat load from its weapons systems. The 'Mech is well protected with thirteen and a half tons of armor. Its weapons payload, and the design in general, uses a mix of advanced and off the shelf parts. This balanced mix makes the Thunder a valuable asset on the modern battlefield. This version of the Thunder replaces the heavy autocannon with a Sian/Ceres Hellcat Thunderbolt 20 missile launcher. It also mounts three Ceres Arms ER Medium Lasers and a single Ceres Arms Model X Medium X-Pulse Laser for close combat. The XL engine is smaller than the standard Thunders engine, reducing the top speed to 64 km/h. To offset this loss of speed, the C4 mounts six Anderson High Propulsion 12s Improved Jump Jets. Built on an Endo Steel chassis, the rest of the 'Mech is covered in thirteen tons of armor.
yangsThoughts: An interesting variant, that thunderbolt lets it hit hard at distance, and the much bigger weight allowance allows for a lot more firepower options. If you plan on using the thunderbolt for indirect fire a lot, you might want to pull some of the jump jets off for more missiles.

details: The Diomede is an early post-Jihad IndustrialMech that was produced by Curtiss Hydroponics of Silver. Starting with the D-M3D-3 model, this massive ConstructionMech was designed to handle the rigors of harsh realities that face potential customers. In addition to handling potential construction tasks, the Diomede can also serve as a SecurityMech if needed. Because of this combat equipment, the Republic of the Sphere requires users to pay large amounts in licensing fees to help keep it from falling into the wrong hands. Built to circumvent the Republic's restrictions on BattleMech production, this Diomede is a cheap assault 'Mech introduced in 3092.[6] None of the standard industrial equipment remains in this variant. It has been replaced by a pair of medium lasers, twin machine guns, and a Imperator Gauss rifle. The 'Mech retains the twin MML-7 launchers. The tracks used by the standard Diomede remain.
yangsThoughts: Is that construction equipment in my mechbay? I'll grant you that it's got an ok weapons loadout for an assault mech, just barely, but it's so crazily slow that you're gonna be dead before you reach the enemy unless you put more long range weapons on there.

details: Originally designed for the Republic of the Sphere, the Doloire was created as the Republic Armed Forces flagship OmniMech. The Republic utilized scientists from Clan enclaves within The Republic to design the assault OmniMech as a powerful and important symbol of cooperation between all citizens, Inner Sphere and Clan. The Doloire was first produced within The Republic in 3121, being produced by StarCorps on Terra. The design would continue into service and secretly be part of the rebuilding of the RAF behind the walls of Fortress Republic. Elements outside of Fortress, including mercenaries, remaining Republican forces, and invaders of former Republic space claimed some examples of the design. The Doloire was designed using advanced forms of Clan and Inner Sphere technology of the era. Designed as an OmniMech with thirty-two tons of pod space for weapons and equipment, engineers used Endo-Composite for its chassis as well as a durable Clan 320-rated Extra Light Fusion Engine. While not the fastest among its peers on the battlefield, the 'Mech is able to handle a respectable amount of incoming fire with its nearly-fifteen tons of ArcShield VII Mk.7 standard armor. The 'Mech's fixed armaments are a pair of Clan Series 2f Extended Range Medium Lasers mounted in the center torso. The primary configuration of the Doloire mounts a pair of highly-accurate Clan Large Pulse Lasers mounted each of the 'Mech's arms. Their accuracy is increased with the presence of Actuator Enhancement Systems placed in each arm. For long-ranged attacks, the prime configuration includes a Clan-tech Gauss rifle with sixteen rounds of ammunition found in the right torso. To help with the 'Mech's heat, four additional Clan-made Double Heat Sinks are added to the configuration. Rounding off the primary's equipment is an ECM Suite
yangsThoughts: A nice flexible omnimech with advanced construction, good armor, potent clan-spec weaponry and versatile electronics.

While not the fastest among its peers on the battlefield, the 'Mech is able to handle a respectable amount of incoming fire with its nearly-fifteen tons of ArcShield VII Mk.7 standard armor. The 'Mech's fixed armaments are a pair of Clan Series 2f Extended Range Medium Lasers mounted in the center torso. The B is designed for intermediate- and long-range combat. The configuration's firepower primarily comes from a pair of Clan ER PPCs mounted in the left arm. In the right is a rotary autocannon/5, drawing from four tons of CASE II-protected ammunition located in the right torso. Five additional Clan-made heat sinks round out the equipment for the B.
yangsThoughts: A long range specialized layout, watch out for the heat output on this one.

While not the fastest among its peers on the battlefield, the 'Mech is able to handle a respectable amount of incoming fire with its nearly-fifteen tons of ArcShield VII Mk.7 standard armor. The 'Mech's fixed armaments are a pair of Clan Series 2f Extended Range Medium Lasers mounted in the center torso. The E laoutput is designed to output a lot of missile firepower, mounting Clan-spec Streak SRMs and LRM 20s
yangsThoughts: A heavy missile boat setup, this thing is good at either long or short range, but you'll have to watch out for the ammo explosions and your supplies.

details: The prototype of the BattleAxe originally appeared in 2459, and relied on the standard armament of the time: lasers and missiles. Over the next fifteen years, the speed was increased by a third and the mass of the cockpit and armor were reduced. In the late 2460s, unsure of the BattleAxe's maneuverability, the introduction of the WSP-1 Wasp inspired designers to mount experimental jump jets onto the upper legs, allowing it to vault over anything it could not run through. Unfortunately, MechWarriors had to go through training for the new jump jets and the landing process, as many died early on. The Federated Suns subsequently switched the Large Lasers in the arms for new Particle Projection Cannons. The BattleAxe only has nine tons of armor, which is comparatively light for a 'Mech of its size. Massive overheating problems, and the introduction of the Hammerhands, eventually ended the operational service of the BattleAxe. The last BattleAxes, considered ancient, were destroyed in a desperate defense of Robinson against conventionally equipped Combat Vehicle formations during the Second Succession War. The original production variant of this 'Mech, the BKX-7K, utilizes two massive Donal Particle Projection Cannons, which were relatively new at the time. Supporting these massive cannons are two Federated 5-Shot LRM-5 launchers with one ton of ammo split between them, as well as a Harpoon-6 SRM-6. These secondary weapons give the BattleAxe something to hit with at all ranges.
yangsThoughts: A true classic antique, from the same era as the Mackie, I feel priviledged just being able to see this thing in my mechbay. The weapons layout for this thing is still viable, similar to the WarHammer but with a little bit less firepower.

Due to the uncertainty of the BattleAxe's jump jets, many MechWarriors had technicians remove the jump jets in favor of two more LRM-5 launchers, one on each side of the torso. It was labeled the 7NC because of its nickname 'No Crash'.
yangsThoughts: A true classic antique, from the same era as the Mackie, I feel priviledged just being able to see this thing in my mechbay. The weapons layout for this thing is still viable, similar to the WarHammer but with a little bit less firepower.

details: The Bellerophon was a failed prototype BattleMech developed in 2442 by what would later be known as Defiance Industries. Designed during the infancy of BattleMech technology, Defiance Industries used what later was known as Primitive Technology to create the Bellerophon. It was envisioned that the design would complement the Mackie with its higher speed, while having nearly as much armor as its companion. Using technology based on the original Mackie prototype, the design inherited a host of design flaws from its predecessor. Chief among these were stability issues: the weapon arrangement would amplify its balance problems despite numerous improvements to the gyro system. After four prototypes were produced, the program was canceled due to the Terran Hegemony's lack of interest in the design. The design incorporated a pair of Large Lasers coupled with an SRM-4 which it would use to provide supporting fire for the Mackie. However, the two large lasers were mounted in the right arm, and the small SRM launcher found in the left arm was hardly able to keep the design balanced and upright, particularly while maneuvering. The Bellerophon's 14 heat sinks were not enough to offset any heat spikes should the MechWarrior choose to fire all their weapons at once, or even fire the two Large Lasers in tandem. This example is one of the rebuilt versions built during the Jihad, and uses Standard armor, chassis and engine instead of the original Primitive tech.
yangsThoughts: I know I asked you to bring me any special mechs and antiques you found, but why THIS thing? I suppose the rebuild using modern gyros at least fixed the balance so it's only under armed as opposed to also unable to stand up.

details: The second exclusive Federated Suns BattleMech, the Hammerhands was developed in 2475 after a decade reviewing the performance and complaints received about the earlier BattleAxe. Little more than a heavier and slower version of the BattleAxe, the design received its name based on the impressive weapons carried in each arm, serving to trigger the Federated Suns' seeming love affair with autocannons. Much like its sibling design, the Hammerhands went on to influence and be outdone by the immensely popular Warhammer, causing it to fade into history, all but forgotten. The Achernar designers' solution for the high heat generated by the BattleAxe's PPCs was to replace them with a pair of Mydron Model B Autocannon/10s backed up by ChisComp 38 medium lasers in each arm, while retaining the earlier design's shoulder-mounted Harpoon-6 SRM-6. Carrying four tons of ammunition spread between the arms and torsos, the Hammerhands autocannons were rarely silenced during most Age of War era conflicts.
yangsThoughts: Now this is a true classic mech, they don't design them like this anymore, although that's probably a good thing for pilot survivability. It's got two powerful autocannons, and some basic additional weapons, but I'd say it could really use updating to the latest ultra autocannons or rotary autocannons to get some proper damage. If you've got them, you could even put some clan gausses on there.

details: The second exclusive Federated Suns BattleMech, the Hammerhands was developed in 2475 after a decade reviewing the performance and complaints received about the earlier BattleAxe. Little more than a heavier and slower version of the BattleAxe, the design received its name based on the impressive weapons carried in each arm, serving to trigger the Federated Suns' seeming love affair with autocannons. Much like its sibling design, the Hammerhands went on to influence and be outdone by the immensely popular Warhammer, causing it to fade into history, all but forgotten. A solution to both Federated Suns MechWarriors' early dislike of jump jets and desire for decreased reliance upon supply chains, the 4D strips out the jump jets and SRM-6 rack for a single PPC.
yangsThoughts: Now this is a true classic mech, they don't design them like this anymore, although that's probably a good thing for pilot survivability. This variant strips out the SRM and jump jets for a PPC, more because the pilots were scared of the jump jets than any actual tactical reason.

details: The second BattleMech model introduced by the Free Worlds League, the Hector entered service in 2471, only one year after the original Icarus. Built by Corean Enterprises MacAdams-Suharno on Stewart, it used a simple design suited for mass production and quickly became the workhorse of the Free Worlds League Military's 'Mech regiments. Limited by the Primitive Technology it was built with, the Hector's performance was generally considered lackluster. Its good top speed came at a big price; the large engine took up so much mass that the 'Mech ended up undergunned. It also had endurance problems as its twelve tons of primitive armor did not provide adequate protection to deal with heavy opponents and it also lacked enough heat sinks for its armament. Overall the Hector proved largely unpopular in its time but was just effective enough to last into the 2520s when it was taken out of service in favor of newer, better designs. No Hectors survived the Age of War, making it one of the earliest lost 'Mech designs. The Hector carries an I.W.W. Large Laser in each arm, giving it a respectable punch at the price of easily overheating. A curious design choice was the inclusion of Sperry Browning machine guns mounted on its upper legs. Meant to deter infantry attacks, the placement of the guns, and especially their ammo bins, in the legs had the opposite effect - enemy infantry soon began specifically targeting the Hector's legs to cripple them via ammunition explosions. This specific mech is a modern reconstruction of the original design, mounting standard equipment and having additional weaponry.
yangsThoughts: Somebody must really like this classic design as they rebuilt it using standard equipment, and used the weight savings to put more weaponry on. It's at least more viable than the original setup, but you'll pretty much have to upgrade this thing heavily to make it useful.
