details: The Conjurer, designated the Hellhound by the IS, is a medium to short range fighter. The mech is, for all intents and purposes, a Wolverine IIC however the Clans would not be willing to name a mech after Clan Wolverine, and so it was renamed. As a second line mech, most variants use a standard engine, but do mount an endo-steel chassis for reduced weight. This variant, however, also uses an extra-light engine to save as much weight as possible for weaponry. A very late variant produced for the Wolf Empire, this version mounts a mix of clan-spec and Inner Sphere weaponry. It downgrades the engine to a 250-xl and uses the weight savings to mount a rotary AC/5, MASC, Clan Stream SRM-6 and two Clan medium pulse lasers.
yangsThoughts: This variant is interesting, the big heavy RAC/5 is unusual to find on a mech this size, but gives it far more firepower than an AC20 without the short range.

details: The Grizzly is a BattleMech that was developed by Clan Ghost Bear scientists in the thirtieth century. Unfortunately, the design excels at no one thing, so it never carved a niche in the Clan's touman. Thirteen tons of armor are mounted on an Endo Steel frame. Jump jets are mounted so that the Grizzly can leap up to 120 meters at a time. Aside from the 'jack of all trades, master of none moniker,' the Grizzly is also poorly engineered. Slagged armor tends to get stuck in joints and weapons, while the actuators are difficult to repair in their own right. Production ceased in 3013 and the remaining 'Mechs were relegated to green Provisional Garrison Clusters.  The Grizzly actually packs a solid long-range punch with a Omega 12-coil Gauss rifle and a Type X Short Bow LRM-10 Launcher. At closer ranges, a brace of pulse lasers provides accurate fire in the form of a Kolibri Delta Series Large Pulse Laser, Kolibri Delta Series Medium Pulse Laser, and a Chi Series Small Pulse Laser.
yangsThoughts: If it weren't for the production quality issues, this mech would have been a solid design with good firepower at all ranges. However, the lack of focus in the firepower holds it back a bit.

details: The Grizzly is a BattleMech that was developed by Clan Ghost Bear scientists in the thirtieth century. Unfortunately, the design excels at no one thing, so it never carved a niche in the Clan's touman. Thirteen tons of armor are mounted on an Endo Steel frame. Jump jets are mounted so that the Grizzly can leap up to 120 meters at a time. Aside from the 'jack of all trades, master of none moniker,' the Grizzly is also poorly engineered. Slagged armor tends to get stuck in joints and weapons, while the actuators are difficult to repair in their own right. Production ceased in 3013 and the remaining 'Mechs were relegated to green Provisional Garrison Clusters.  THis variant design refits the mech into a potent urban combat unit specialized in close range firepower, but with a decent amount of long range still preserved. It Keeps the Gauss rifle, but swaps from pulse lasers to an ER Large Laser and 6 ER Micros. Indirect firepower is provided by an ATM-9.
yangsThoughts: The close range laser array really only works in urban environments, you might want to consider replacing it with an array of small pulse lasers if you want to get some more range.

details: The Dark Crow is a Clan Snow Raven second line BattleMech, which was produced only at its Inner Sphere facilities in the Outworlds Alliance after the Jihad. Named after a strange cave-dwelling bird found on the planet Dante, the 'Mech has proven to be a popular design among second line troops. The 'Mech's principal weaponry is its two Type 31 UAC/5s, each found in its rather unusual mid-torso arms. Augmenting the dual autocannons at closer ranges is a single Mk. 22 SRM-6 launcher found in the right torso. The 'Mech's left torso-mounted Series 6b ER large laser is generally utilized for long-range attacks and sniping. The Dark Crow has been fitted with 10 double heat sinks to handle its movement and weapons heat. Four tons of ammunition supply this 'Mech's autocannon pair, and a single ton of ammunition feeds its missile launcher system. An endo steel frame holds the 330-rated XL engine, both of which helping to free weight in order to mount the 'Mech's equipment. Protecting the Dark Crow's hull is eight tons of standard armor, which has been criticized for being thinner than that of most typical Medium 'Mechs of its size.
yangsThoughts: A pretty good second line medium, but that armor is a bit thin for my tastes. I wonder if we could fit two rotary autocannons on in place of those ultras.

details: The 'Mech's origins can be traced to the Onager. The Eagle Craft Group of Erewhon took the older design and styled it similarly to the earlier Eyrie and Gyrfalcon. Admired for its capacities, the Shrike became a highly sought-out design for Jade Falcon commanders after its performance during a raid on La Grave against Clan Wolf in 3115. The Shrike is outfitted with a powerful Targeting Computer that allows it to bring accurate firepower to bear on the battlefield. It has a hard-hitting armament that gives the 'Mech a powerful sting in combat at most ranges. A Type X Long Bow LRM-10-rack allows pilots to engage enemies at long range, and two Type 9 Ultra Autocannon/5s are effective against even other assault-class 'Mechs. Lastly, two Series 2b ER Large Lasers provide a fairly powerful punch should the other weapon systems deplete their ammunition. The design owes its large weapon capacity to its Endo Steel construction and its large 380-rated Extra-Light Fusion Engine. Engineers enhanced the design by adding a Partial Wing, giving the Shrike extra jump capacity as well as additional cooling abilities under normal atmospheric conditions.
yangsThoughts: Another Jade Falcon design with a big wing for jumping distance. This thing has pretty good firepower but you might want to replace the autocannons with something with more punch.

details: The 'Mech's origins can be traced to the Onager. The Eagle Craft Group of Erewhon took the older design and styled it similarly to the earlier Eyrie and Gyrfalcon. Admired for its capacities, the Shrike became a highly sought-out design for Jade Falcon commanders after its performance during a raid on La Grave against Clan Wolf in 3115. The Shrike is outfitted with a powerful Targeting Computer that allows it to bring accurate firepower to bear on the battlefield. It has a hard-hitting armament that gives the 'Mech a powerful sting in combat at most ranges. Introduced in 3115, the second variant of the Shrike replaces the ER Large Lasers with ER medium lasers, which allows the autocannons to be upgraded to pair of Ultra Autocannon/10s.
yangsThoughts: Another Jade Falcon design with a big wing for jumping distance. I see I wasn't the only one who thought those autocannons needed some more punch, those UAC/10s should improve the close in firepower nicely.

details: The 'Mech's origins can be traced to the Onager. The Eagle Craft Group of Erewhon took the older design and styled it similarly to the earlier Eyrie and Gyrfalcon. Admired for its capacities, the Shrike became a highly sought-out design for Jade Falcon commanders after its performance during a raid on La Grave against Clan Wolf in 3115. The Shrike is outfitted with a powerful Targeting Computer that allows it to bring accurate firepower to bear on the battlefield. It has a hard-hitting armament that gives the 'Mech a powerful sting in combat at most ranges. This variant of the Shrike was introduced in 3116. The Shrike 3 is similar to the standard model, save the swapping out of the 'Mech's autocannons for pair of Extended Range Particle Projection Cannons.
yangsThoughts: Another Jade Falcon design with a big wing for jumping distance. Twin ER PPCs instead of autocannons eliminate most of the ammo limits for this thing, but it does make it run much hotter.

details: The Solitaire is a fast, heavily armored light BattleMech. This 25-ton design is one of the fastest in existence, allowing it to pick off enemy units that stray too far from support, like a lioness hunting a pack of wildebeest. The design seems an odd one for the cost-conscious Clan Diamond Shark to deploy, as it makes use of every weight saving material available to the Clans in 3067, including an extralight engine, ferro-fibrous armor, and an endo steel structure. The Solitaire relies on a full complement of heavy energy weapons, which are not only powerful but gives it significant endurance so long as heat is managed properly. Even though the 10 double heat sinks cannot possibly dissipate the massive spike in heat, typical warriors will move into position and then perform a full alpha strike with the single Model X heavy large laser, the Model II heavy small laser, and the two Model V heavy medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: It's crazy fast, and that lets it easily outmaneuver most things that can fight it, just keep in mind that if you overheat and shutdown then all the speed in the world won't save you.

details: The Tiburon is a name that harkens back to when Clan Sea Fox was known as Clan Diamond Shark. The Tiburon is named after a large Terran shark found in the Caribbean. The design was created as the Clan transformed itself from a decimated-yet-centralized military force to a nomadic, spacefaring society of warrior-merchants. Unlike most other 'Mechs built by the Sea Foxes, the Tiburon was, as of 3145, not for sale, used exclusively by its own warriors. Fast and well-armed for its weight, the Tiburon can be used in scout and attack formations alike. Mounting a pair of Series 6b ER medium lasers in each arm, and backed up by two Pattern Alpha SRM-2 launchers and twin Series 6c ER small lasers, the Tiburon can quickly close with an enemy and inflict damage before moving out of range again. Making matters worse for its opponents, the Tiburon carries a Watchdog CEWS to not only interfere with their electronics, but also assist in finding them from farther distances, and the lasers are all connected to an accurate targeting computer.
yangsThoughts: Fast and with a lot of short range firepower, this thing is great at doing a quick strike and then retreating before it can be focused on by the enemy.

details: The Tiburon is a name that harkens back to when Clan Sea Fox was known as Clan Diamond Shark. The Tiburon is named after a large Terran shark found in the Caribbean. The design was created as the Clan transformed itself from a decimated-yet-centralized military force to a nomadic, spacefaring society of warrior-merchants. Unlike most other 'Mechs built by the Sea Foxes, the Tiburon was, as of 3145, not for sale, used exclusively by its own warriors. Fast and well-armed for its weight, the Tiburon can be used in scout and attack formations alike. Mounting a pair of Series 6b ER medium lasers in each arm, and backed up by two Pattern Alpha SRM-2 launchers and twin Series 6c ER small lasers, the Tiburon can quickly close with an enemy and inflict damage before moving out of range again. Making matters worse for its opponents, the Tiburon carries a Watchdog CEWS to not only interfere with their electronics, but also assist in finding them from farther distances, and the lasers are all connected to an accurate targeting computer. This variant replaces the ER medium lasers with improved heavy medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: Even more short range firepower, but even more heat problems mean that you need to be careful to manage that heat.

details: The Cossack is named after the mercenary unit Khorsakov's Cossacks in honor of their faithful service to the St. Ives Compact. The Cossack is a practice in compromise, originally requested as the fastest 'Mech that Ceres Metals Industries could produce, the emphasis of the design changed throughout its development to a slow moving, heavily armored light 'Mech. The final design that was accepted became the current incarnation of the Cossack, built on a weight saving Taijian Series X endo steel chassis, and mounting a lightweight Hermes 120 XL engine that gives the 'Mech a top speed of 97.2 km/h. Six Pitban LFT-65 jump jets allow the Cossack to jump up to 180 meters, allowing the 'Mech to easily handle the roughest terrain. For protection, the Cossack has three tons of armor and has 10 single heat sinks to handle the heat produced by the weapons payload carried by the 'Mech. Overall, the compromises that created the Cossack turned it into a light trooper 'Mech that works well as part of a team, but alone excels at no one role. The primary weapons on the Cossack are a Kajuka Type 2 'Bright Blossom' medium laser and a MilDouglas 'Duke' SRM-6. These are backed up by a pair of Jackson Model 12 small lasers. While this is not an impressive arsenal, the Cossack is capable of defending itself from most light 'Mechs and vehicles.
yangsThoughts: An interesting compromise mech, it's not the strongest thing, but for a 20 ton 'Mech you can't really expect too much, and this thing performs better than many things in this weight class.

details: The Cossack is named after the mercenary unit Khorsakov's Cossacks in honor of their faithful service to the St. Ives Compact. The Cossack is a practice in compromise, originally requested as the fastest 'Mech that Ceres Metals Industries could produce, the emphasis of the design changed throughout its development to a slow moving, heavily armored light 'Mech. The final design that was accepted became the current incarnation of the Cossack, built on a weight saving Taijian Series X endo steel chassis, and mounting a lightweight Hermes 120 XL engine that gives the 'Mech a top speed of 97.2 km/h. Six Pitban LFT-65 jump jets allow the Cossack to jump up to 180 meters, allowing the 'Mech to easily handle the roughest terrain. For protection, the Cossack has three tons of armor and has 10 single heat sinks to handle the heat produced by the weapons payload carried by the 'Mech. Overall, the compromises that created the Cossack turned it into a light trooper 'Mech that works well as part of a team, but alone excels at no one role. The C-1FC variant first appeared during the Jihad, where it was given minor advanced technology upgrades to its arsenal. The 'Mech's armor was switched from standard armor to light ferro-fibrous, which allowed for an increase in protection of the 'Mech's vitals. All weapons were removed in favor of three RL-10s, spread across the Cossack's torso sections. The only energy weapon available, a medium variable speed pulse laser, is mounted in the left arm.
yangsThoughts: Ok, this thing is difficult to really justify deploying, sure the RL-10s do a lot of damage for very light weight and low heat, but they only do one shot, so anything more than one or two enemies in a contract and you'll be massively outclassed.

details: The Albatross was originally designed by the Free Worlds League in 3053 with an eye toward a possible future conflict with the then advancing Clans. The fruit of 'Project Omega,' a Free Worlds League Military project in 3052 to create an assault 'Mech capable of facing Clan OmniMechs based off scant reports of Clan fighting tactics and weapons and refit kits requested by the Federated Commonwealth and Draconis Combine, the first Albatross was delivered after only five months of design and testing with mass production being authorized by the League High Command a mere ten months after it was commissioned. Built using a Hermes 380 XL engine to minimize weight and maximize free tonnage for its weapon loadout, the Albatross also uses a variety of weapons in an attempt to make the 'Mech effective at any range. The 'Mech has average mobility with a maximum speed of 64.8 km/h and also makes use of double heat sinks for their greater heat dissipation capability. The Albatross carries thirteen tons of Starslab/3 armor. Finally, the 'Mech is also equipped with CASE to protect against ammunition explosions. The Albatross does not specialize in one role and is more of a workhorse design than anything else. The 'Mech carries an Irian Weapons Works V7 LRM-15 launcher which gives it long range and indirect fire support capabilities. This is backed up by an Oriente Model O LB-X Autocannon/10 and a Diverse Optics Sunbeam ER Large Laser which give the 'Mech a powerful long-range punch. The 'Mech carries a Tronel PPL-20 Large Pulse Laser which allows the 'Mech to engage an enemy at the edge of the medium to long-range envelope. Finally, for close combat, the 'Mech has an Irian Weapons Works 60mm SRM-6 and two Irian Weapons Works Super 6 medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: The only reason this thing ever got a bad rap was that some of the later versions used a composite internal structure which made it fragile if the armor was breached. The earlier versions are pefectly solid mechs, though they're a bit too generalist to really reach maximum effectiveness.

details: The Albatross was originally designed by the Free Worlds League in 3053 with an eye toward a possible future conflict with the then advancing Clans. The fruit of 'Project Omega,' a Free Worlds League Military project in 3052 to create an assault 'Mech capable of facing Clan OmniMechs based off scant reports of Clan fighting tactics and weapons and refit kits requested by the Federated Commonwealth and Draconis Combine, the first Albatross was delivered after only five months of design and testing with mass production being authorized by the League High Command a mere ten months after it was commissioned. Built using a Hermes 380 XL engine to minimize weight and maximize free tonnage for its weapon loadout, the Albatross also uses a variety of weapons in an attempt to make the 'Mech effective at any range. The 'Mech has average mobility with a maximum speed of 64.8 km/h and also makes use of double heat sinks for their greater heat dissipation capability. The Albatross carries thirteen tons of Starslab/3 armor. Finally, the 'Mech is also equipped with CASE to protect against ammunition explosions. The Albatross does not specialize in one role and is more of a workhorse design than anything else. The 'Mech carries an Irian Weapons Works V7 LRM-15 launcher which gives it long range and indirect fire support capabilities. This is backed up by an Oriente Model O LB-X Autocannon/10 and a Diverse Optics Sunbeam ER Large Laser which give the 'Mech a powerful long-range punch. The 'Mech carries a Tronel PPL-20 Large Pulse Laser which allows the 'Mech to engage an enemy at the edge of the medium to long-range envelope. Finally, for close combat, the 'Mech has an Irian Weapons Works 60mm SRM-6 and two Irian Weapons Works Super 6 medium lasers. This version replaces the missile launchers with a single MML-9 and the autocannon and Large Laser are replaced with a Gauss rifle and Snub-Nose PPC.
yangsThoughts: The only reason this thing ever got a bad rap was that some of the later versions used a composite internal structure which made it fragile if the armor was breached. The earlier versions are pefectly solid mechs, though they're a bit too generalist to really reach maximum effectiveness. The Gauss Rifle and MML make this a bit more flexible at the cost of more explosion risk.

details: The Albatross was originally designed by the Free Worlds League in 3053 with an eye toward a possible future conflict with the then advancing Clans. The fruit of 'Project Omega,' a Free Worlds League Military project in 3052 to create an assault 'Mech capable of facing Clan OmniMechs based off scant reports of Clan fighting tactics and weapons and refit kits requested by the Federated Commonwealth and Draconis Combine, the first Albatross was delivered after only five months of design and testing with mass production being authorized by the League High Command a mere ten months after it was commissioned. Built using a Hermes 380 XL engine to minimize weight and maximize free tonnage for its weapon loadout, the Albatross also uses a variety of weapons in an attempt to make the 'Mech effective at any range. The 'Mech has average mobility with a maximum speed of 64.8 km/h and also makes use of double heat sinks for their greater heat dissipation capability. The Albatross carries thirteen tons of Starslab/3 armor. Finally, the 'Mech is also equipped with CASE to protect against ammunition explosions. The Albatross does not specialize in one role and is more of a workhorse design than anything else. The 4U variant of the Albatross is a basic upgrade of the design. The 'Mech replaces the medium lasers with a pair of ER medium lasers, the LB 10-X Autocannon with a Light Gauss Rifle, the SRM-6 with a Streak SRM-6 launcher. The ER large laser is removed and replaced by an ER small laser, while the armor is increased by more than three tons and upgraded to ferro-fibrous.
yangsThoughts: The only reason this thing ever got a bad rap was that some of the later versions used a composite internal structure which made it fragile if the armor was breached. The earlier versions are pefectly solid mechs, though they're a bit too generalist to really reach maximum effectiveness. This rework definitely improves the overall firepower while also improving the armor, but if you have a clanspec gauss then it'll be a better upgrade.

details: The Albatross was originally designed by the Free Worlds League in 3053 with an eye toward a possible future conflict with the then advancing Clans. The fruit of 'Project Omega,' a Free Worlds League Military project in 3052 to create an assault 'Mech capable of facing Clan OmniMechs based off scant reports of Clan fighting tactics and weapons and refit kits requested by the Federated Commonwealth and Draconis Combine, the first Albatross was delivered after only five months of design and testing with mass production being authorized by the League High Command a mere ten months after it was commissioned. Built using a Hermes 380 XL engine to minimize weight and maximize free tonnage for its weapon loadout, the Albatross also uses a variety of weapons in an attempt to make the 'Mech effective at any range. The 'Mech has average mobility with a maximum speed of 64.8 km/h and also makes use of double heat sinks for their greater heat dissipation capability. The Albatross carries thirteen tons of Starslab/3 armor. Finally, the 'Mech is also equipped with CASE to protect against ammunition explosions. The Albatross does not specialize in one role and is more of a workhorse design than anything else. The 4Ur is more a direct upgrade of the 3U than a refit of the 4U. The armor is maxed out with 16.5 tons of Ferro-Fibrous. The Light Gauss Rifle of the 4U is replaced with a familiar LBX-10 like the original 3U. A standard LRM-15 and SRM-6 fill out the right torso. A pair of ER Medium Lasers replace the standard lasers and sit atop the traditional Large Pulse Laser. A Light PPC sits in the left torso mount. When the 5U debuted to lack luster reception the AMSC fell back on the dependable performance of the 4Ur.
yangsThoughts: The only reason this thing ever got a bad rap was that some of the later versions used a composite internal structure which made it fragile if the armor was breached. The earlier versions are pefectly solid mechs, though they're a bit too generalist to really reach maximum effectiveness. Another dependable rework, which of these versions you like really does depend on preference of the pilot and what grade of weaponry you have available.

details: The Naginata, named as an old Samurai lance-like weapon, is designed as a command and fire support 'Mech that carries a C3I Computer. The 'Mech is built using a massive Pitban 285 fusion engine, giving it a top speed of 54 km/h. The Naginata makes use of double heat sink technology for its added heat dissipation capabilities to handle the waste heat that is produced by the 'Mech's massive arsenal. For protection, the Naginata carries fifteen tons of armor and includes CASE to protect against an ammunition explosion. The mix of heavy armor, CASE, and a standard engine make the Naginata a zombie of a 'Mech that can take a tremendous amount of damage. The Naginata serves in the dual role of command and fire support 'Mech. The C3I computer allows the 'Mech to share the targeting data of its lancemates and to take advantage of their targeting information, making the three Coventry Star Fire LRM-15s with Artemis IV fire control systems and the Lord's Light 2 ER PPC extremely accurate. The Artemis IV fire control system gives the 'Mech increased missile accuracy.
yangsThoughts: A useful command mech that also has decent firesupport abilities, it suffers, like all fire support mechs, from the massive inferiority of Inner Sphere LRMs.

details: The Naginata, named as an old Samurai lance-like weapon, is designed as a command and fire support 'Mech that carries a C3I Computer. The 'Mech is built using a massive Pitban 285 fusion engine, giving it a top speed of 54 km/h. The Naginata makes use of double heat sink technology for its added heat dissipation capabilities to handle the waste heat that is produced by the 'Mech's massive arsenal. For protection, the Naginata carries fifteen tons of armor and includes CASE to protect against an ammunition explosion. The mix of heavy armor, CASE, and a standard engine make the Naginata a zombie of a 'Mech that can take a tremendous amount of damage. This variant removes the left torso equipment and replaces it with a Large Variable Speed Pulse Laser and improved comms equipment taken from the Word of Blake.
yangsThoughts: Even better command electronics, if only they had replaced the missiles at the same time.

details: The Raptor was the first Inner Sphere OmniMech. Luthien Armor Works designed the Raptor as a proof of concept after months of studying OmniMech salvage recovered from the Battle of Luthien. The inherent ease of repair and ability to reconfigure loadout made the resulting OmniMech a premier recon and fast strike unit. The Raptor is built on an Alshain Type KR140 endo steel chassis and uses a weight-saving Omni 175 XL engine, which gives the Raptor a top speed of 118.8 km/h. Three and a half tons of armor provides protection to the Raptor from most light weapons, but the Raptor relies more on its speed than anything else to protect it from taking damage, as this armor protection is thin. With the 'Mech featuring eleven and half tons of available pod space, the Primary configuration of the Raptor is built to engage an enemy at long ranges with its three LRM-5 launchers. In the case that the enemy somehow manages to close with the swift moving Raptor, the 'Mech carries two medium lasers and two small lasers. Additionally, in the primary configuration, the Raptor carries two machine guns to defend against infantry attacks.
yangsThoughts: Honestly, you'd really think that the idea of standardizing weapons connections to allow for fast refits would have occurred to people centuries ago, but I guess better late than never. This thing is good as a firesupport or ambusher, just try not to let it get shot.

details: The Raptor was the first Inner Sphere OmniMech. Luthien Armor Works designed the Raptor as a proof of concept after months of studying OmniMech salvage recovered from the Battle of Luthien. The inherent ease of repair and ability to reconfigure loadout made the resulting OmniMech a premier recon and fast strike unit. The Raptor is built on an Alshain Type KR140 endo steel chassis and uses a weight-saving Omni 175 XL engine, which gives the Raptor a top speed of 118.8 km/h. Three and a half tons of armor provides protection to the Raptor from most light weapons, but the Raptor relies more on its speed than anything else to protect it from taking damage, as this armor protection is thin. The Raptor A is intended for extended operations from supply lines. As such, its primary weapons are two large lasers, supported in close range combat by three small lasers.
yangsThoughts: Honestly, you'd really think that the idea of standardizing weapons connections to allow for fast refits would have occurred to people centuries ago, but I guess better late than never. All energy for this version, so you never need to resupply, though you gotta watch that heat.

details: The Raptor was the first Inner Sphere OmniMech. Luthien Armor Works designed the Raptor as a proof of concept after months of studying OmniMech salvage recovered from the Battle of Luthien. The inherent ease of repair and ability to reconfigure loadout made the resulting OmniMech a premier recon and fast strike unit. The Raptor is built on an Alshain Type KR140 endo steel chassis and uses a weight-saving Omni 175 XL engine, which gives the Raptor a top speed of 118.8 km/h. Three and a half tons of armor provides protection to the Raptor from most light weapons, but the Raptor relies more on its speed than anything else to protect it from taking damage, as this armor protection is thin. The B configuration carries three SRM-6 launchers and a small laser. This configuration is intended for close combat and works well in a vehicle-hunting role as well as in mop-up operations.
yangsThoughts: Honestly, you'd really think that the idea of standardizing weapons connections to allow for fast refits would have occurred to people centuries ago, but I guess better late than never. Mostly missiles, so it's got good heat management, but is very easy to blow up with an ammo explosion.

details: The Raptor was the first Inner Sphere OmniMech. Luthien Armor Works designed the Raptor as a proof of concept after months of studying OmniMech salvage recovered from the Battle of Luthien. The inherent ease of repair and ability to reconfigure loadout made the resulting OmniMech a premier recon and fast strike unit. The Raptor is built on an Alshain Type KR140 endo steel chassis and uses a weight-saving Omni 175 XL engine, which gives the Raptor a top speed of 118.8 km/h. Three and a half tons of armor provides protection to the Raptor from most light weapons, but the Raptor relies more on its speed than anything else to protect it from taking damage, as this armor protection is thin. Configured for medium to close range combat, the C configuration of the Raptor is built to swoop in on a target and overwhelm an enemy with fire from its six medium lasers and two small pulse lasers; to deal with this large heat load it also adds two more double heat sinks. For missile defense, the Raptor C carried an anti-missile system.
yangsThoughts: Honestly, you'd really think that the idea of standardizing weapons connections to allow for fast refits would have occurred to people centuries ago, but I guess better late than never. Another all energy version, this one is designed to get in, do as much damage as possible, then get out before you die.

details: Built due to an awkward exchange of technology between the Free Worlds League and the Word of Blake, the Toyama was designed by Dr. Thaddeus Anable and then given to the Word of Blake through an agreement made by Captain-General Thomas Marik. The Toyama is intended to be a multirole BattleMech that can operate somewhat independently of supply lines. The 'Mech is built on a lightweight Endo Steel chassis which supports a Vlar 300 XL engine. The Toyama has a top speed of 64.8 km/h, making it capable of keeping pace with most heavy 'Mechs. The Toyama has an impressive twelve tons of Durallex Heavy armor. To dissipate heat, the Toyama carries fourteen double heat sinks. The 'Mech also carries a Garret/Guardian Interface Guardian ECM Suite to protect the 'Mech from advanced enemy electronic warfare systems. The Toyama has a Doombud LRM-20 launcher for long range direct and indirect bombardment capabilities. The Toyama also has two Diverse Optics Sunbeam ER large lasers and an Imperator Code Red LB 10-X autocannon, which can fire both standard ammunition and cluster rounds, for long range combat. For close range engagements, the Toyama has three Magna Mk II medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: A pretty standard heavy mech with decent weaponry layout, it's got too much ammo based weaponry to really be independent of supply lines, we can probably rework it to fit any role you want if you've got the guns for it.

details: Built due to an awkward exchange of technology between the Free Worlds League and the Word of Blake, the Toyama was designed by Dr. Thaddeus Anable and then given to the Word of Blake through an agreement made by Captain-General Thomas Marik. The Toyama is intended to be a multirole BattleMech that can operate somewhat independently of supply lines. The 'Mech is built on a lightweight Endo Steel chassis which supports a Vlar 300 XL engine. The Toyama has a top speed of 64.8 km/h, making it capable of keeping pace with most heavy 'Mechs. The Toyama has an impressive twelve tons of Durallex Heavy armor. To dissipate heat, the Toyama carries fourteen double heat sinks. The 'Mech also carries a Garret/Guardian Interface Guardian ECM Suite to protect the 'Mech from advanced enemy electronic warfare systems. An Advanced Tech version of the Toyama that was introduced during the Jihad. The 1C was redesigned as a fire support 'Mech. Its long-range weaponry includes; an Artemis IV-enhanced LRM 20 launcher with 2 tons of CASE-protected ammunition and a Gauss rifle with 2 tons of ammunition. For the 'Mech's secondary weaponry, it has two medium variable speed pulse lasers mounted in the right arm.
yangsThoughts: A pretty standard heavy mech with decent weaponry layout, it's got too much ammo based weaponry to really be independent of supply lines. A more sniping focused variant, this one mounts some better electronics for lance coordination.

details: Built due to an awkward exchange of technology between the Free Worlds League and the Word of Blake, the Toyama was designed by Dr. Thaddeus Anable and then given to the Word of Blake through an agreement made by Captain-General Thomas Marik. The Toyama is intended to be a multirole BattleMech that can operate somewhat independently of supply lines. The 'Mech is built on a lightweight Endo Steel chassis which supports a Vlar 300 XL engine. The Toyama has a top speed of 64.8 km/h, making it capable of keeping pace with most heavy 'Mechs. The Toyama has an impressive twelve tons of Durallex Heavy armor. To dissipate heat, the Toyama carries fourteen double heat sinks. The 'Mech also carries a Garret/Guardian Interface Guardian ECM Suite to protect the 'Mech from advanced enemy electronic warfare systems. This variant on the -1A downgrades the extralight engine to a light engine that is heavier, but makes the Toyama more durable. All of the weaponry of the -1A is also stripped out and replaced. The LRM was downsized to a Doombud 15-pack and the medium lasers were replaced with a pair of Diverse Optics ER medium lasers. The autocannon and large lasers were replaced with new Blankenburg PPCs that were originally developed by the Draconis Combine: two lights and a heavy. The ECM Suite was replaced with a now-ubiquitous C3i computer. The new weapons package left enough room to shore up the armor on the arms and legs. Sixteen double heat sinks keeps the -1B cooler than the -1A.
yangsThoughts: A pretty standard heavy mech with decent weaponry layout, this one has almost no ammo weapons other than the missiles which makes it good in long battles.

details: The Wraith marked Curtiss Militech's entry into BattleMech production. When the design was announced, many felt that the design would be little more than a walking water cooler. What the critics got was a shock. The Wraith is a 'Mech that, when properly employed, can wreak havoc on an enemy with its astounding speed and maneuverability. It is built around a massive LTV 385 XL engine that allows it to reach speeds of just under 119 km/h. The 'Mech is also fitted with seven CurtissJet 55 jump jets which allow it to jump up to two hundred and ten meters, giving it the ability to outmaneuver most BattleMechs in existence, and also allowing the Wraith to quickly traverse any rough terrain. The 'Mech's speed, firepower, and decent armor make the Wraith a considerable threat to many 'Mechs, some almost twice its weight. The Wraith has a simple, yet effective, weapons payload. The 'Mech is armed with a Tronel XIII large pulse laser that provides the majority of its firepower. Backing this up is a pair of Tronel XII medium pulse lasers that provide firepower for close-in work. This pulse laser weaponry allows the 'Mech to negate most of the side effects on accuracy that are introduced by the Wraith's heavy use of jump jets, making it accurate and powerful for such a lightly armed 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: Lightly armed, but maneuverable and fast, if you use it properly you can jump rings around anything on the field.

details: The Wraith marked Curtiss Militech's entry into BattleMech production. When the design was announced, many felt that the design would be little more than a walking water cooler. What the critics got was a shock. The Wraith is a 'Mech that, when properly employed, can wreak havoc on an enemy with its astounding speed and maneuverability. It is built around a massive LTV 385 XL engine that allows it to reach speeds of just under 119 km/h. The 'Mech is also fitted with seven CurtissJet 55 jump jets which allow it to jump up to two hundred and ten meters, giving it the ability to outmaneuver most BattleMechs in existence, and also allowing the Wraith to quickly traverse any rough terrain. The 'Mech's speed, firepower, and decent armor make the Wraith a considerable threat to many 'Mechs, some almost twice its weight. A simple modification of the Wraith, the TR2 simply replaces the large pulse laser with an ER PPC, turning the 'Mech into a highly capable sniper.
yangsThoughts: Better at sniping and still incredibly fast and maneuverable, so you can pretty much just dictate the engagement range to anything bigger.

details: The Wraith marked Curtiss Militech's entry into BattleMech production. When the design was announced, many felt that the design would be little more than a walking water cooler. What the critics got was a shock. The Wraith is a 'Mech that, when properly employed, can wreak havoc on an enemy with its astounding speed and maneuverability. It is built around a massive LTV 385 XL engine that allows it to reach speeds of just under 119 km/h. The 'Mech is also fitted with seven CurtissJet 55 jump jets which allow it to jump up to two hundred and ten meters, giving it the ability to outmaneuver most BattleMechs in existence, and also allowing the Wraith to quickly traverse any rough terrain. The 'Mech's speed, firepower, and decent armor make the Wraith a considerable threat to many 'Mechs, some almost twice its weight. A modification of the Wraith made by the Word of Blake, the TR3 replaces the two medium pulse lasers with two ER medium lasers, while one of the jump jets is removed to make room for an improved C3 computer, making the Wraith an incredibly powerful point 'Mech for units equipped with C3i equipment.
yangsThoughts: Pretty much the same as the regular mech but with better ability to work with lancemates, I think the lesser jumping is worth the trade.

details: The Komodo was designed in 3053 to hunt and kill Clan Elemental battle armor. The 'Mech is built to be able to catch up to and destroy them with exceptional speed and maneuverability. The Komodo is powered by a VOX 225 XL extralight engine, which propels it to a maximum speed of 86.4 km/h, and can jump up to 150 meters thanks to five Icarus 81 jump jets, allowing it to traverse just about any terrain with ease. It is protected by seven and a half tons of standard armor, and is also equipped with a Matabushi Guardian ECM suite to defend against any enemy electronic warfare systems. Finally, the 'Mech is equipped with a Forward-1 TAG, allowing the Komodo to designate a target for improved direct fire damage. The Komodo is armed with 10 Victory 23R medium lasers as its primary weapons. While these have a range of only 270 meters, the 'Mech has enough firepower to annihilate an entire point of Elementals. The 'Mech is also armed with two Yori Flyswatter anti-missile systems, to defend against missile attacks.
yangsThoughts: This is basically the same design concept as the Clan Nova, but the regular medium lasers make it much easier to keep cool so it can get more of that firepower out consistently.

details: The Komodo was designed in 3053 to hunt and kill Clan Elemental battle armor. The 'Mech is built to be able to catch up to and destroy them with exceptional speed and maneuverability. The Komodo is powered by a VOX 225 XL extralight engine, which propels it to a maximum speed of 86.4 km/h, and can jump up to 150 meters thanks to five Icarus 81 jump jets, allowing it to traverse just about any terrain with ease. It is protected by seven and a half tons of standard armor, and is also equipped with a Matabushi Guardian ECM suite to defend against any enemy electronic warfare systems. Finally, the 'Mech is equipped with a Forward-1 TAG, allowing the Komodo to designate a target for improved direct fire damage. The Komodo is armed with 10 Victory 23R medium lasers as its primary weapons. While these have a range of only 270 meters, the 'Mech has enough firepower to annihilate an entire point of Elementals. The 'Mech is also armed with two Yori Flyswatter anti-missile systems, to defend against missile attacks. The 2A variant of the Komodo is a modification that looks to increase the 'Mech's reach by replacing five of the medium lasers with a single ER large laser, allowing the Komodo to strike at a much farther range at the cost of raw firepower.
yangsThoughts: This version can basically fire all of its weapons constantly which means even if it doesn't have as much firepower, it can unload all of that firepower continuously, which produces better overall damage.

details: Powered by a VOX 280 extralight fusion engine, the Carronade could reach speeds of 64 km/h on the battlefield, although it lacked Jump Jets. The Carronade was protected by thirteen and a half tons of ArcShield VII Mk.5 standard armor, while CASE II installed in the Carronade protected the 'Mech and its MechWarrior if either of the main weapons was damaged. As of 3125, it was believed (at least by the NAIS) that the Carronade was the only 'Mech outfitted with the Silver Bullet Gauss rifle at a large scale of production. As secondary weapons, the Carronade mounted a Diverse Optics Sunfire Extended-Range Medium Laser in the head and each side torso. The primary weapons of the Carronade were the two Gauss rifles it mounted. However, the arrangement was somewhat unusual. An Oriente MagCoil Model M Gauss rifle was mounted in the right arm and was used to strip large chunks of armor off individual locations on target units. The Oriente MagCoil Model K Silver Bullet Gauss rifle mounted in the left arm then exploited those holes. Each Gauss rifle was supplied with two tons of ammunition stored in the corresponding torso location.
yangsThoughts: The close range laser array really only works in urban environments, you might want to consider replacing it with an array of small pulse lasers if you want to get some more range.

details: The Eris began as an attempt by Nashan Diversified to branch out of IndustrialMechs and into BattleMechs along with the Inner Sphere's other major BattleMech manufacturers. It was originally called the Hel, but the LCAF rejected the design, and Nashan Diversified was only able to sell a few to mercenaries and various governments within the former Free Worlds League before Defiance Industries stopped supplying Nashan with necessary components to what had become a competitor. Left with a powerful 'Mech it was no longer able to build, Nashan sold the specifications for the Hel to Kallon Weapon Industries, who went on to redesign the 'Mech as a skirmisher ideally suited for the sort of fighting that was going on in the former Free Worlds League following the end of the Jihad. The armament of the Hel was very similar to that of the Wolverine, which was also manufactured by Kallon. As such, they tried to distinguish the Eris by adding a partial wing, which improved its mobility in rough terrain and also allowed them to market it as a competitor to the Phoenix Hawk being sold by Earthwerks. The energy weaponry that the Hel had used were replaced with a Fusigon Smarttooth snub-nose PPC and three Diverse Optics small X-Pulse lasers, and the Hel's single Streak Missile Launcher were replaced with a pair of Holly MML-5s fed from two CASE II protected ammo bins.
yangsThoughts: A highly maneuverable medium mech with a long jump range, this thing is great at evading fire and hitting hard, but with the short range of its weapons you'll need to be careful to not overextend.

details: The Eris began as an attempt by Nashan Diversified to branch out of IndustrialMechs and into BattleMechs along with the Inner Sphere's other major BattleMech manufacturers. It was originally called the Hel, but the LCAF rejected the design, and Nashan Diversified was only able to sell a few to mercenaries and various governments within the former Free Worlds League before Defiance Industries stopped supplying Nashan with necessary components to what had become a competitor. Left with a powerful 'Mech it was no longer able to build, Nashan sold the specifications for the Hel to Kallon Weapon Industries, who went on to redesign the 'Mech as a skirmisher ideally suited for the sort of fighting that was going on in the former Free Worlds League following the end of the Jihad. The armament of the Hel was very similar to that of the Wolverine, which was also manufactured by Kallon. As such, they tried to distinguish the Eris by adding a partial wing, which improved its mobility in rough terrain and also allowed them to market it as a competitor to the Phoenix Hawk being sold by Earthwerks. Save for different component manufacturers, this variant was functionally identical to the HL-1 Hel, and became popular after Clan Wolf conquered Thermopolis, cutting off access to new Wolverines. This means a Heavy PPC in the right arm, with a Streak SRM-6 in the left torso and a pair of ER Medium Lasers in the left arm.
yangsThoughts: A highly maneuverable medium mech with a long jump range. This variants improved weapons ranges allow for it to operate better as a direct competitor to the phoenix hawk, but it does lack the range of the MMLs.

details: The product of heavy government investment, the Koschei was the second BattleMech produced in the Capellan Confederation. Since the Confederation Defense Company was not interested in helping, Earthwerks and the Confederation invested in improving the infrastructure on Tikonov and recruiting local engineers. The first Koscheis were produced in 2504. Production of the 3I stopped in 2714 to make way for an improved variant. By the time of the Succession Wars there were few such 'Mechs on the battlefield. The primary weapon of the Koschei is an Imperator-B Autocannon/10. This low-heat weapon still has respectable range and firepower. It is backed up by Magna Mk II medium lasers. The standard allotment of heat sinks is almost thoroughly sufficient for the 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: Another antique for the mech bay, not sure how much we can really improve this thing other than swapping out that AC for UAC or RAC but it's still a tolerable design.

details: The product of heavy government investment, the Koschei was the second BattleMech produced in the Capellan Confederation. Since the Confederation Defense Company was not interested in helping, Earthwerks and the Confederation invested in improving the infrastructure on Tikonov and recruiting local engineers. The first Koscheis were produced in 2504. Production of the 3I stopped in 2714 to make way for an improved variant. By the time of the Succession Wars there were few such 'Mechs on the battlefield. An effort to improve long-range firepower, the 3L took out one laser and the autocannon to make room for a Large Laser and LRM-15.
yangsThoughts: Another antique for the mech bay, this time a variant with LRMs instead of an autocannon, at least in this case the path to upgrade is obvious and leads right through Clan spec LRMs.

details: The product of heavy government investment, the Koschei was the second BattleMech produced in the Capellan Confederation. Since the Confederation Defense Company was not interested in helping, Earthwerks and the Confederation invested in improving the infrastructure on Tikonov and recruiting local engineers. The first Koscheis were produced in 2504. Production of the 3I stopped in 2714 to make way for an improved variant. By the time of the Succession Wars there were few such 'Mechs on the battlefield. The upgraded version of the 3I debuted in 2719. It is built on an Endo Steel chassis with ferro-fibrous armor. It features an LB 10-X AC and more lasers, but was not well-received.
yangsThoughts: Another antique for the mech bay, at least the endo steel chassis gives some more room to upgrade this one.

details: The product of heavy government investment, the Koschei was the second BattleMech produced in the Capellan Confederation. Since the Confederation Defense Company was not interested in helping, Earthwerks and the Confederation invested in improving the infrastructure on Tikonov and recruiting local engineers. The first Koscheis were produced in 2504. Production of the 3I stopped in 2714 to make way for an improved variant. By the time of the Succession Wars there were few such 'Mechs on the battlefield. An upgraded version of the 3L, the 4L utilizes an Endo Steel structure and enhances the accuracy of the missile launcher with an Artemis IV Fire Control System. The remaining weight savings goes to upgrading the lasers to a Large and Medium Pulse Laser, respectively, while double heat sinks counter the increased heat.
yangsThoughts: Another antique for the mech bay, the double heat sinks are a big help in opening up room to upgrade this thing, but it's still a bit limited on hardpoints.

details: The product of heavy government investment, the Koschei was the second BattleMech produced in the Capellan Confederation. Since the Confederation Defense Company was not interested in helping, Earthwerks and the Confederation invested in improving the infrastructure on Tikonov and recruiting local engineers. The first Koscheis were produced in 2504. Production of the 3I stopped in 2714 to make way for an improved variant. By the time of the Succession Wars there were few such 'Mechs on the battlefield. The 5I was introduced by Earthwerks in 3071, primarily for the mercenary market. It saves weight with an XL engine, Endo Steel, and light ferro-fibrous armor. Thirteen double heat sinks keep the heat buildup from a Heavy and a Snub-Nose PPC and five Extended Range Small Lasers under control. The machine also mounts jump jets and a Guardian ECM Suite.
yangsThoughts: Not an antique this time, this upgrade gives the boost to hardpoints that the mech really needed, and the XL engine gives it the extra weight it needs to actually put stuff in those hardpoints.

details: The product of heavy government investment, the Koschei was the second BattleMech produced in the Capellan Confederation. Since the Confederation Defense Company was not interested in helping, Earthwerks and the Confederation invested in improving the infrastructure on Tikonov and recruiting local engineers. The first Koscheis were produced in 2504. Production of the 3I stopped in 2714 to make way for an improved variant. By the time of the Succession Wars there were few such 'Mechs on the battlefield. As part of the Trinity Alliance, the Magistracy of Canopus acquired plans for the Koschei in 3068. Majesty Metals and Manufacturing produces their variation on the 5I at Dunianshire. It removes the jump jets and weapons in favor of a different configuration, which hearkens back to the earlier versions with a massive Rotary Autocannon. ER lasers and a Snub-Nose PPC necessitates the addition of fourteenth heat sink to remain cool.
yangsThoughts: Not an antique this time, I kind of like this one more than the 5I in concept, that big RAC really gets the firepower hitting hard.

details: The Ymir was the Lyran Commonwealth's first homegrown BattleMech. The first primitive machines, then known as the Bipedal Weapons Platform, saw action in 2462. Named for the frost giants of Scandinavian mythology, the Ymir was developed by Coventry Defense Conglomerate by taking the basic frame of the Mackie, shaving ten tons from it and then modifying it to form a sleeker design, that offered greater stability and the ability to maneuver more easily. CDC chose to further differentiate the Ymir from its predecessor by giving it a more angled appearance and distributing the weapons load more evenly. With a weapons load designed to enable it to switch between engaging at long range and short range, the BWP-1X Ymir was, on paper, a capable machine; however, it was no faster than the Mackie and its prototype heat dissipation system meant that it frequently ran hot. The Lyran High Command elected not to buy the BWP-X1, preferring to stick with the Mackie, but did offer enough support to CDC to enable them to build a handful of the earliest Ymir model as prototype testbeds, allowing CDC to continue development work. CDC struggled along until more advanced components enabled them to increase the 'Mech's top speed to 64 km/h and protect it with ten and a half tons of armor. Coventry eventually produced upgraded versions of the Ymir in 2475 (-2B) and 2478 (-2E). These variants of the Ymir remained in production well into the Star League era, halting in 2648. The advanced materials allowed engineers to improve upon the prototype Ymir's weaponry as well. At long range, the assault 'Mech can call upon an Arcturan Arms Longarm Series Autocannon/5 and two CDC Single-Five LRM-5 launchers. As it gets closer to an enemy, the Ymir can add an Accufire Optics Large Laser. At close range, four Coventry B4 SRM-2 launchers and an Accufire Mini Small Laser can exploit any holes the larger guns made in an enemy's armor.
yangsThoughts: Commander, where do you find these things, how do you consistently manage to find working examples of five hundred year old designs? This thing has a decent set of hardpoints, but that huge engine means it doesn't have a huge amount of room for weaponry. It does have a lot of hardpoints we can use though.

Named for the frost giants of Scandinavian mythology, the Ymir was developed by Coventry Defense Conglomerate by taking the basic frame of the Mackie, shaving ten tons from it and then modifying it to form a sleeker design, that offered greater stability and the ability to maneuver more easily. CDC chose to further differentiate the Ymir from its predecessor by giving it a more angled appearance and distributing the weapons load more evenly. With a weapons load designed to enable it to switch between engaging at long range and short range, the BWP-1X Ymir was, on paper, a capable machine; however, it was no faster than the Mackie and its prototype heat dissipation system meant that it frequently ran hot. The Lyran High Command elected not to buy the BWP-X1, preferring to stick with the Mackie, but did offer enough support to CDC to enable them to build a handful of the earliest Ymir model as prototype testbeds, allowing CDC to continue development work. CDC struggled along until more advanced components enabled them to increase the 'Mech's top speed to 64 km/h and protect it with ten and a half tons of armor. Coventry eventually produced upgraded versions of the Ymir in 2475 (-2B) and 2478 (-2E). These variants of the Ymir remained in production well into the Star League era, halting in 2648. An 'upgunned' version of the 2B , Coventry's 2E replaced the LRMs with a single LRM-10 and the SRMs with one SRM-6. The Small Laser was replaced with an extra half-ton of armor. The Large Laser and autocannon were swapped out for two PPCs. Because they did not upgrade the heat dissipation system along with the weapons, MechWarriors had to use volley fire, leading to displeasure with the design.
yangsThoughts: Commander, where do you find these things, how do you consistently manage to find working examples of five hundred year old designs? The hardpoints are honestly a slight downgrade compared teh base model, and the heat issues make this not much of an upgrade really.

details: The Ymir was the Lyran Commonwealth's first homegrown BattleMech. The first primitive machines, then known as the Bipedal Weapons Platform, saw action in 2462. Named for the frost giants of Scandinavian mythology, the Ymir was developed by Coventry Defense Conglomerate by taking the basic frame of the Mackie, shaving ten tons from it and then modifying it to form a sleeker design, that offered greater stability and the ability to maneuver more easily. CDC chose to further differentiate the Ymir from its predecessor by giving it a more angled appearance and distributing the weapons load more evenly. With a weapons load designed to enable it to switch between engaging at long range and short range, the BWP-1X Ymir was, on paper, a capable machine; however, it was no faster than the Mackie and its prototype heat dissipation system meant that it frequently ran hot. The Lyran High Command elected not to buy the BWP-X1, preferring to stick with the Mackie, but did offer enough support to CDC to enable them to build a handful of the earliest Ymir model as prototype testbeds, allowing CDC to continue development work. CDC struggled along until more advanced components enabled them to increase the 'Mech's top speed to 64 km/h and protect it with ten and a half tons of armor. Coventry eventually produced upgraded versions of the Ymir in 2475 (-2B) and 2478 (-2E). These variants of the Ymir remained in production well into the Star League era, halting in 2648. The 3A is an upgrade of the 2E Ymir using Star League technology and was developed in 2733. It downgraded the PPCs to standard Large Lasers even as double heat sinks were used. An Artemis IV Fire Control System was added to each missile launcher. The remaining tonnage went towards a medium laser and additional armor.
yangsThoughts: Commander, where do you find these things, how do you consistently manage to find working examples of five hundred year old designs? The double heat sinks at least improve the heat management, but I'm not sure the Artemis is worth the downgraded PPCs.

details: Developed in response to a Star League Defense Force request for a long-range infantry support BattleMech, the first Jackrabbit prototype was completed by 2763, but was only approved for combat testing a mere two weeks before the Amaris Coup. Stefan Amaris took advantage of this windfall and ordered the Jackrabbit mass produced for his troops. Due to fundamental flaws in the design being revealed in the hard fighting of Amaris Civil War and the taint of being used against the SLDF, no Jackrabbits were taken on Operation Exodus. The surviving examples of the 500 Jackrabbits produced and the Skobel factory were carefully mothballed by ComStar. Intended to provide long-range fire support to fellow light 'Mechs and infantry, the Jackrabbit is armed with an Armstrong JJ-Lite AC/2, backed up at short range by a single Holly Streak SRM-2 rack. The main weapon is also the Jackrabbit's main drawback; while class 2 autocannons have longer effective ranges than most other weapons, they inflict the same damage as a machine gun at the cost of considerable weight which could be devoted to a larger engine or more armor, a tradeoff that ultimately doomed the Jackrabbit.
yangsThoughts: I really can't say that the criticisms of this thing are wrong, that AC/2 is just not worth it for how heavy it is relative to power. Now, if you replaced that with an ultra autocannon, then that would really shake things up.

details: Developed in response to a Star League Defense Force request for a long-range infantry support BattleMech, the first Jackrabbit prototype was completed by 2763, but was only approved for combat testing a mere two weeks before the Amaris Coup. Stefan Amaris took advantage of this windfall and ordered the Jackrabbit mass produced for his troops. Due to fundamental flaws in the design being revealed in the hard fighting of Amaris Civil War and the taint of being used against the SLDF, no Jackrabbits were taken on Operation Exodus. The surviving examples of the 500 Jackrabbits produced and the Skobel factory were carefully mothballed by ComStar. Nicknamed 'the Joker' by SLDF troops, JKR-9R was developed by the Rim Worlds Republic in 2768; technicians attempted to boost the flawed Jackrabbits effectiveness by replacing the autocannon with a large laser, an extra single heat sink, and a ton of armor. Half the surviving examples are of this type.
yangsThoughts: I guess a large laser kind of helps the poor performance of the AC/2, but I think cramming a PPC onto the thing would work better, really.

details: The Jackrabbit II (also known as the Nexus) was developed by Comstar after the devastation of the fighting on Tukayyid. Since Comstar had controlled the old Jackrabbit factory since the time of the Amaris civil war, they reactivated it and reworked the Jackrabbit into being a fast striker in 3054. As a recon 'Mech intended to rarely engage an enemy in a pitched battle, the Nexus is armed with a Blankenburg Technologies medium laser backed up by two highly-accurate Blankenburg Technologies medium pulse lasers. As a weapon of last resort, the Nexus has two Intek small lasers. For added protection against missile attacks, the Nexus had an McArthur anti-missile system.
yangsThoughts: A lot more firepower than the older model with those lasers, but it's extremely fragile with that amount of armor, so you've really gotta watch out for anything even looking at it funny.

details: The Excalibur is designed to engage an enemy at long range and use its mobility to dictate the ranges of the engagement. The 'Mech is named for the appearance of the muzzle and dummy hand on the right arm, which reminded the design team of the look of a medieval Terran sword. The EXC-B2 first saw service in 2620 and served with SLDF forces well in Operation Smother and the Amaris Civil War. However, the Excalibur has proven to be most potent when supported by other types of BattleMechs, primarily due to insufficient armor. Excalibur MechWarriors were often derisively called 'Streakers' during the Amaris Coup due to their hit-and-run attacks and refusals of stand-up fights. By the time of Operation EXODUS, most surviving examples were found in the 328th Royal BattleMech Division and left with General Kerensky. The 'Mech has an impressive top speed of 86.4 km/h, which is made possible through the use of a Ford 350 XL engine. The Excalibur carries a relatively light seven and a half tons of armor, with CASE in both side torsos to protect against an ammunition explosion. With its mission of long-range combat, the Excalibur does not need a very high level of armor protection. The Excalibur dissipates its waste heat through the use of eleven heat sinks. For added protection against missile attacks, the Excalibur had a McArthur anti-missile system. As its primary weapon, the Excalibur carries a powerful Grizzard Model 200 Gauss rifle that has an effective range of up to six hundred and sixty meters. This is backed up by an Ingrid Systems LRM-20 with an Artemis IV fire control computer for increased missile accuracy.
yangsThoughts: Powerful guns, and long range, but that armor is incredibly fragile. You're gonna need to really stay away from counter fire if you wanna keep this thing intact.

As its primary weapon, the Excalibur carries a powerful Grizzard Model 200 Gauss rifle that has an effective range of up to six hundred and sixty meters. This is backed up by an Ingrid Systems LRM-20 with an Artemis IV fire control computer for increased missile accuracy. The B2b is very similar to the B2, except that the use of an Endo Steel structure allows for an additional three and a half tons of armor. This variant was utilized by the SLDF Royals.
yangsThoughts: The improved heatsinks and improved armor help a lot with letting this version actually get in brawling fights, but you still wanna try to snipe unless you change out that weaponry.

details: The Excalibur is designed to engage an enemy at long range and use its mobility to dictate the ranges of the engagement. The 'Mech is named for the appearance of the muzzle and dummy hand on the right arm, which reminded the design team of the look of a medieval Terran sword. The EXC-B2 first saw service in 2620 and served with SLDF forces well in Operation Smother and the Amaris Civil War. However, the Excalibur has proven to be most potent when supported by other types of BattleMechs, primarily due to insufficient armor. Excalibur MechWarriors were often derisively called 'Streakers' during the Amaris Coup due to their hit-and-run attacks and refusals of stand-up fights. By the time of Operation EXODUS, most surviving examples were found in the 328th Royal BattleMech Division and left with General Kerensky. The 'Mech has an impressive top speed of 86.4 km/h, which is made possible through the use of a Ford 350 XL engine. The Excalibur carries a relatively light seven and a half tons of armor, with CASE in both side torsos to protect against an ammunition explosion. With its mission of long-range combat, the Excalibur does not need a very high level of armor protection. The Excalibur dissipates its waste heat through the use of eleven heat sinks. For added protection against missile attacks, the Excalibur had a McArthur anti-missile system. As its primary weapon, the Excalibur carries a powerful Grizzard Model 200 Gauss rifle that has an effective range of up to six hundred and sixty meters. This is backed up by an Ingrid Systems LRM-20 with an Artemis IV fire control computer for increased missile accuracy. Following the fall of Terra in 3058, ComStar no longer had any factories, let alone ones that could produce the Excalibur. They solved the latter problem in mid-3067 by contracting with Independence Weaponry on Quentin to produce a variant that has been well-received in both the Com Guards and DCMS. The CS removes the Gauss Rifle to make way for a Lord's Light 2 ER PPC and twelve double heat sinks. Four ER medium lasers and an ER Small Laser ensure that the Excalibur CS is a threat at close range, as well. Thirteen and a half tons of armor protect this variant better than any other configuration of the Excalibur. A targeting computer helps guide the direct-fire weapons. Finally, triple strength myomer harnesses the heat buildup of the CS to make it faster.
yangsThoughts: Lots of effective brawling weaponry, but it definitely has issues with running hot, you might want to reduce the lasers a bit and put more heat sinks on to compensate.

details: The Excalibur is designed to engage an enemy at long range and use its mobility to dictate the ranges of the engagement. The 'Mech is named for the appearance of the muzzle and dummy hand on the right arm, which reminded the design team of the look of a medieval Terran sword. However, the Excalibur has proven to be most potent when supported by other types of BattleMechs, primarily due to insufficient armor. Excalibur MechWarriors were often derisively called 'Streakers' during the Amaris Coup due to their hit-and-run attacks and refusals of stand-up fights. By the time of Operation EXODUS, most surviving examples were found in the 328th Royal BattleMech Division and left with General Kerensky. The 'Mech has an impressive top speed of 86.4 km/h, which is made possible through the use of a Ford 350 XL engine. The Excalibur carries a relatively light seven and a half tons of armor, with CASE in both side torsos to protect against an ammunition explosion. With its mission of long-range combat, the Excalibur does not need a very high level of armor protection. The variant is a radical reworking of the Excalibur by Word of Blake that was first reported to have been seen in 3067 on Outreach. All the weapons are removed to make way for eleven and a half tons of armor and enough jump jets to vault the 'Mech up to one hundred and fifty meters at a time. A short-range arsenal is in place for the remaining tonnage, anchored by a rotary autocannon/5. It, along with two ER medium lasers, are made more accurate through an advanced Targeting Computer. A Streak SRM-6 enables highly-accurate missile fire. An improved C3 computer aids in targeting with the other five members of the Level II.
yangsThoughts: Another brawling variant, this one focused on an autocannons instead of energy weapons, this variant has lots of ammunition but has issues with heat management like the CS.

details: Although only a prototype at the outbreak of the Amaris Coup in 2766, the Sling still managed to make its way into the hands of the Star League Defense Forces in the fight to retake Terra - and in turn, several of the machines accompanied Aleksandr Kerensky on the Exodus, finding a place among the nascent forces of Clan Smoke Jaguar during Operation KLONDIKE. Meant mostly as a high-speed fire support unit, the minimally-armored Sling was meant to remain out of the way of enemy fire utilizing its GM 175 XL engine and provide fire-support by saturating the target area with its three LRM-5s. The standard SLDF procedure was for Slings to only carry Thunder munitions, but pilots assigned to the machines frequently ignored this standard in order to carry at least one ton of standard munitions. The Sling also carried a TAG spotting laser, meaning the 'Mech could serve double duty as a harasser and an artillery spotter.
yangsThoughts: Fast and evasive, this thing can easily keep out of sight, but doesn't really have enough firepower for anything other than fighting other light mechs.

details: Although only a prototype at the outbreak of the Amaris Coup in 2766, the Sling still managed to make its way into the hands of the Star League Defense Forces in the fight to retake Terra - and in turn, several of the machines accompanied Aleksandr Kerensky on the Exodus, finding a place among the nascent forces of Clan Smoke Jaguar during Operation KLONDIKE. Meant as a more traditional skirmish unit, the SL-1H replaced all three LRM-5 launchers with a pair of standard SunGlow medium lasers and a single medium pulse laser, which allowed for an increase in both the number of single heat sinks to 12 and the armor protection to five tons due to removed ammunition supplies. The 1H still kept the TAG laser, allowing it to still serve as an artillery spotter.
yangsThoughts: Fast with a decent laser array, this thing can get laser damage into areas fast, but is really fragile so you're gonna need to keep moving to stay alive.

details: The Mercury debuted for the first time in 2742, intended as a replacement for the aging Wasps and Stingers in use by the Star League Defense Force. Hailed as the beginning of a new age in 'Mech design, the Mercury was faster and better-armed than its competition without sacrificing armor protection. It also featured an advancement in weapons installation, making use of a modular replacement system to allow for easier repairs. The ideal scout and raider, sales of the Mercury were limited exclusively to the SLDF. After the Amaris Civil War and the dissolution of the Star League, those SLDF units which still fielded the Mercury were either stationed on Terra or at the Graham IV factory, all of whom left with General Kerensky in the Exodus. The Mercury carries an assortment of energy weapons which helps to extend its endurance on long campaigns. The primary weapons are two Martell medium lasers in the right arm and left torso. The right arm laser's power supply is spread across the length of the whole arm with multiple redundant systems, while the torso-mounted laser is clustered tightly in one area to decrease the chance of a penetrative hit damaging the system. These are supported by two Hessen IX small lasers in the center torso and head and feature the revolutionary modular replacement system.
yangsThoughts: A classic mech, fast, modular and easy to maintain. This thing inspired the omnimech, though I'm not sure where you found an original model in this good of a condition.

details: The Mercury debuted for the first time in 2742, intended as a replacement for the aging Wasps and Stingers in use by the Star League Defense Force. Hailed as the beginning of a new age in 'Mech design, the Mercury was faster and better-armed than its competition without sacrificing armor protection.
