After the Amaris Civil War and the dissolution of the Star League, those SLDF units which still fielded the Mercury were either stationed on Terra or at the Graham IV factory, all of whom left with General Kerensky in the Exodus. This Mercury variant has been reworked by an unnamed clan to mount a bigger engine instead of MASC, and with improved energy weaponry.
yangsThoughts: I don't know who built this thing, commander, It looks to be using Clan-spec weaponry, but I don't recognize the markings and I don't know who would be building these things instead of more modern designs.

details: Introduced in 2735, the Crockett was originally designed and built for the Star League as a training BattleMech with combat capability in response to needs during the Hidden Wars. Ten years after its introduction, however, Star League Defense Force commanders began to realize that Blankenburg Technologies had taken the 'combat capability' requirement almost too seriously, and began to field the 'Mech on the front lines. Beyond its heavy weapons loadout, large number of heat sinks and use of jump jets, part of what made the Crockett successful was the enemy's unfamiliarity with this design, giving it an added advantage. Its cockpit systems, simplified for use as a trainer, allowed anyone to pilot the Crockett with only a minimal amount of familiarization training. In service to the SLDF the hallmark tactic of the Crockett was to engage an enemy 'Mech at range and, before the enemy could accurately target it, jump behind them to attack their weaker rear. The 5003-0 Crockett is a downgraded version of the 'Mech first spotted in 2881. The 'Mech retains the same overall feel of the 5003-1 but uses only technology available to the Great Houses during the Succession Wars. The ER Large Lasers have been replaced with Magna Mark III Large Lasers models and the LB-X autocannon has been downgraded to a standard Blankenburg AC/10. In addition, the double heat sinks that made the 'Mech capable of a very high rate of fire have been downgraded to single versions that can only dissipate half as much heat as those carried by the 5003-1 Crockett.
yangsThoughts: Kind of a big downgrade compared to the original, but it does at least still have decent armor, you're gonna want to improve that heat management though.

details: Introduced in 2735, the Crockett was originally designed and built for the Star League as a training BattleMech with combat capability in response to needs during the Hidden Wars. Ten years after its introduction, however, Star League Defense Force commanders began to realize that Blankenburg Technologies had taken the 'combat capability' requirement almost too seriously, and began to field the 'Mech on the front lines. Beyond its heavy weapons loadout, large number of heat sinks and use of jump jets, part of what made the Crockett successful was the enemy's unfamiliarity with this design, giving it an added advantage. Its cockpit systems, simplified for use as a trainer, allowed anyone to pilot the Crockett with only a minimal amount of familiarization training. In service to the SLDF the hallmark tactic of the Crockett was to engage an enemy 'Mech at range and, before the enemy could accurately target it, jump behind them to attack their weaker rear. Due to the fact that the Crockett was originally a training 'Mech, it carries a variety of weapons. The primary weapon is a Blankenburg LB 10-X AC that can use either standard High Explosive Armor Penetrating (HEAP) ammunition or a shotgun-like cluster round. It is fed by two magazines, a 20-rounder in the right torso and a 10-rounder in the left. The autocannon is backed up by two Blankenburg 25 Extended Range Large Lasers one in each arm for long range combat. When the Crockett closes with its enemies, it can bring into play its twin Holly-6 SRM-6 launchers, one mounted in each side torso with a 180-round reserve in the right, and one Dodd Small Laser mounted in each arm. The Holly launchers were chosen in particular for their reliability, especially when the 'Mech lands after jumping through the air; less-proven launchers were shown to have one or more missiles knocked out of alignment after just a single jump.
yangsThoughts: Now this is a proper Crockett, decent speed for an assault mech, good weapons layout and efficient heat dissipation, just watch out for the heat output from the jumpjets and lasers at the same time.

details: Introduced in 2735, the Crockett was originally designed and built for the Star League as a training BattleMech with combat capability in response to needs during the Hidden Wars. Ten years after its introduction, however, Star League Defense Force commanders began to realize that Blankenburg Technologies had taken the 'combat capability' requirement almost too seriously, and began to field the 'Mech on the front lines. Beyond its heavy weapons loadout, large number of heat sinks and use of jump jets, part of what made the Crockett successful was the enemy's unfamiliarity with this design, giving it an added advantage. Its cockpit systems, simplified for use as a trainer, allowed anyone to pilot the Crockett with only a minimal amount of familiarization training. In service to the SLDF the hallmark tactic of the Crockett was to engage an enemy 'Mech at range and, before the enemy could accurately target it, jump behind them to attack their weaker rear. This SLDF Royal variant introduced in 2752 replaces the LB 10-X autocannon and SRM launchers with a suite of medium lasers and Medium Pulse Lasers. Each arm mounts an ER Large Laser with a medium laser and MPL mounted alongside. The head and center torso each mount an MPL as well. For long range firepower, a Gauss rifle with two tons of ammo is mounted in the left torso.
yangsThoughts: A nice improvement over the original, the much larger energy loadout allow for much more damage output at distance, and it has nothing to explode except the one Gauss Rifle. Just watch your heat levels.

details: The Guillotine was originally designed in 2499 as a front-line BattleMech for the Terran Hegemony and was the Star League Defense Force's standard heavy 'Mech for generations. While its weaponry was ample for its era, as time went by the march of technology allowed for the development of larger 'Mechs which were significantly better armed. However few of these new 'Mechs could match the Guillotine in maneuverability or staying power, and so the 'Mech simply transitioned from the front-line shock role to that of a heavy raider - a task it excels at thanks to its quick speed and use of jump jets. Its mobility also makes the 'Mech adept at fighting in difficult terrain such as jungle or mountainous regions. The only serious issue with the 'Mech ironically are its weapons, which have been plagued with various problems over the centuries. The Guillotine carries a single Large Laser which had buggy cabling in some early version, along with four medium lasers. Backing up the energy weapons is a single SRM-6 launcher with one ton of ammo. The nimbleness of the Guillotine is what's responsible for its longevity, a factor which can catch new pilots off-guard. Four Anderson 398 jump jets, one in either leg and either side torso, gives the 'Mech a jumping distance of 120 meters.
yangsThoughts: A versatile skirmisher, this mech is decently powerful, especially when you can backstab enemies, but it suffers in terrain that doesn't let it make use of its evasion.

details: The Guillotine was originally designed in 2499 as a front-line BattleMech for the Terran Hegemony and was the Star League Defense Force's standard heavy 'Mech for generations. While its weaponry was ample for its era, as time went by the march of technology allowed for the development of larger 'Mechs which were significantly better armed. However few of these new 'Mechs could match the Guillotine in maneuverability or staying power, and so the 'Mech simply transitioned from the front-line shock role to that of a heavy raider - a task it excels at thanks to its quick speed and use of jump jets. Its mobility also makes the 'Mech adept at fighting in difficult terrain such as jungle or mountainous regions. The only serious issue with the 'Mech ironically are its weapons, which have been plagued with various problems over the centuries. Suspected to have been delivered in error to Kapten Harald Estriđsen of the Free Rasalhague Republic's 1st Drakøns as a gift from ComStar in 3049, this Guillotine variant was assumed to be a prototype designed by ComStar for its Com Guards. Removing ten heat sinks but upgrading the remaining fifteen to doubles, the left arm Large Laser was replaced by an ER PPC while the number of Medium Lasers was increased from four to twelve; one in the head, three in the right arm, two in the left torso and a whopping six in the right torso. Its heat load now quite excessive, the SRM-6 rack and its troublesome CASE ammo bay also remains.
yangsThoughts: Are you joking with me right now? Why does this thing mount twelve medium lasers, who greenlit this thing?

details: The Guillotine was originally designed in 2499 as a front-line BattleMech for the Terran Hegemony and was the Star League Defense Force's standard heavy 'Mech for generations. While its weaponry was ample for its era, as time went by the march of technology allowed for the development of larger 'Mechs which were significantly better armed. However few of these new 'Mechs could match the Guillotine in maneuverability or staying power, and so the 'Mech simply transitioned from the front-line shock role to that of a heavy raider - a task it excels at thanks to its quick speed and use of jump jets. Its mobility also makes the 'Mech adept at fighting in difficult terrain such as jungle or mountainous regions. The only serious issue with the 'Mech ironically are its weapons, which have been plagued with various problems over the centuries. Based on the original Estridsen variant that was suspected to have been delivered in error to Kapten Harald Estriđsen of the Free Rasalhague Republic's 1st Drakøns as a gift from ComStar in 3049, the precursor to this Guillotine variant was assumed to be a prototype designed by ComStar for its Com Guards. Removing ten heat sinks but upgrading the remaining fifteen to doubles, the left arm Large Laser was replaced by an ER PPC while the number of Medium Lasers was increased from four to twelve; one in the head, three in the right arm, two in the left torso and a whopping six in the right torso. Its heat load now quite excessive, the SRM-6 rack and its troublesome CASE ammo bay also remains. The production version of this mach was produced in 3070 and it made use of some Clan-Spec weaponry and heat sinking technology to allow for even greater heat management than the original prototype.
yangsThoughts: Ok, now I know someone is messing with me, not only did they make a prototype but they actually made a production version of this thing?

details: The Guillotine was originally designed in 2499 as a front-line BattleMech for the Terran Hegemony and was the Star League Defense Force's standard heavy 'Mech for generations. While its weaponry was ample for its era, as time went by the march of technology allowed for the development of larger 'Mechs which were significantly better armed. However few of these new 'Mechs could match the Guillotine in maneuverability or staying power, and so the 'Mech simply transitioned from the front-line shock role to that of a heavy raider - a task it excels at thanks to its quick speed and use of jump jets. Its mobility also makes the 'Mech adept at fighting in difficult terrain such as jungle or mountainous regions. The only serious issue with the 'Mech ironically are its weapons, which have been plagued with various problems over the centuries. The 7M variant is an improvement of the older Guillotine replacing the Large Laser with an ER PPC with capacitor, the two medium lasers in the right arm with twin light PPCs, and upgrading the other two medium lasers to ER models. The older SRM-6 is upgraded to a Streak enhanced model, which also fixed the original 'Mech's odd SRM arming bug.
yangsThoughts: A very nice upgrade overall, it does suffer from not being able to fire all of its weapons without overheating, so you're gonna need to adjust your tactics a bit.

details: The Shootist first saw service in 2621 as a heavily armored, electronic warfare-capable command 'Mech for SLDF Royal BattleMech Regiments. However, the Shootist's upgraded electronic suites proved no match for the cutting edge of electronic warfare of its own time. When the BattleMaster eclipsed the Shootist in its intended role as a command vehicle merely ten years after its introduction, the SLDF changed the Shootist's mission parameters. The Shootist's balanced mix of weapons and speed along with its heavy armor allowed it a new role: juggernaut for close-combat heavy lances. Later it found further use as a bodyguard unit for command lances. However, this role did not prove favorable to the longevity of the Shootist. Since the Succession Wars, this BattleMech is rarely seen outside of the Com Guards, Word of Blake Militia, and ancient Clan Brian Caches. The Shootist has a top speed of 64.8 km/h, giving it an average level of mobility for a heavy 'Mech. The 'Mech has thirteen double heat sinks that allow the Shootist to dissipate the waste heat that is produced by its heavy weapons payload. The Shootist is armed to engage an enemy at various ranges. The only long-range weapon on the 'Mech is a Blankenburg ER Large Laser that has an effective range of five hundred and seventy meters. For medium to close range combat, the Shootist carries a devastating Deathgiver Autocannon/20 which is capable of downing most light and even some medium 'Mechs in a single salvo. Finally, for point-blank defense, the 'Mech has two Blankenburg Medium Pulse Lasers and a Dinatech Mark III Small Laser.
yangsThoughts: A decent close in brawler, that heavy autocannon packs a serious punch, but if you can fit the ammo you might want to upgrade it to an ultra class for maximum damage.

details: The Shootist first saw service in 2621 as a heavily armored, electronic warfare-capable command 'Mech for SLDF Royal BattleMech Regiments. However, the Shootist's upgraded electronic suites proved no match for the cutting edge of electronic warfare of its own time. When the BattleMaster eclipsed the Shootist in its intended role as a command vehicle merely ten years after its introduction, the SLDF changed the Shootist's mission parameters. The Shootist's balanced mix of weapons and speed along with its heavy armor allowed it a new role: juggernaut for close-combat heavy lances. Later it found further use as a bodyguard unit for command lances. However, this role did not prove favorable to the longevity of the Shootist. Since the Succession Wars, this BattleMech is rarely seen outside of the Com Guards, Word of Blake Militia, and ancient Clan Brian Caches. The Shootist has a top speed of 64.8 km/h, giving it an average level of mobility for a heavy 'Mech. The 'Mech has thirteen double heat sinks that allow the Shootist to dissipate the waste heat that is produced by its heavy weapons payload. The 9C further modifies the 8C by removing the autocannon and a heat sink to make room for a Gauss rifle, turning the Shootist into a support 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: A long range sniper version of this venerable mech, this setup could really use a clan gauss to get the most out of its tonnage.

details: Conceived by Martinson Armaments of Terra in 2578 as an infantry support 'Mech, the Hoplite first saw service with the newly formed Star League Defense Force during the Reunification War before being relegated to Terran Hegemony militia units in the twenty-seventh century. While every Hoplite in the Inner Sphere was destroyed during the Amaris Crisis and the early Succession Wars, Aleksandr Kerensky's SLDF exiles found the simple and rugged design ideal for bolstering their forces in the months prior to the Exodus Civil War. Selected by Wolf's Dragoons in the belief that the ancient design wouldn't arouse undue attention, it had the complete opposite effect, and only raised more questions as to the Dragoons' origins when they arrived in 3005. After the mercenaries cut ties with the Clan Homeworlds in 3020, they also lost access to replacement Hoplites, with attrition bringing the design close to extinction by the Jihad. Intended for infantry support, the Hoplite is slow for a medium 'Mech, with a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. However its 11.5 tons of armor and heavy arsenal, both impressive for a 'Mech of its size, makes the Hoplite a rugged machine able to fight its way out of most situations using superior firepower. Refitted by the Dragoons after the Inner Sphere's recovery of the Helm Memory Core, the HOP-4D Hoplite is armed with an impressive Mydron Excel LB-X AC/10, which allows it to fire either standard rounds or cluster rounds, which are similar to the blast from a shotgun, only on a 'Mech scale. As a secondary weapon, the Hoplite carries a Conan/5 LRM-5 launcher, which also allows it to keep the enemy at long ranges. Carrying 20 rounds of autocannon ammunition and 24 LRM reloads forces the design to remain close to supply lines.
yangsThoughts: Good firepower and armor for its size, but its dependance on ammo means you really gotta watch your supplies, and it could really use some clan grade tech to get enough firepower.

details: Conceived by Martinson Armaments of Terra in 2578 as an infantry support 'Mech, the Hoplite first saw service with the newly formed Star League Defense Force during the Reunification War before being relegated to Terran Hegemony militia units in the twenty-seventh century. While every Hoplite in the Inner Sphere was destroyed during the Amaris Crisis and the early Succession Wars, Aleksandr Kerensky's SLDF exiles found the simple and rugged design ideal for bolstering their forces in the months prior to the Exodus Civil War. Intended for infantry support, the Hoplite is slow for a medium 'Mech, with a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. However its 11.5 tons of armor and heavy arsenal, both impressive for a 'Mech of its size, makes the Hoplite a rugged machine able to fight its way out of most situations using superior firepower. Developed after the Battle of Luthien, the Hoplite C is a variant exclusively used by Wolf's Dragoons that uses all Clan technology. Clad in 10 tons of ferro-fibrous and fitted with double heat sinks, the 'Mech carries a UAC/10 as its primary weapon, supported by an LRM-15 launcher for long-range engagements, and two medium pulse lasers and an ER medium laser for close range combat.
yangsThoughts: One of the many examples of just how much better clan weaponry is, the half weight LRMs and lighter ultra autocannon give it room for some pulse lasers and a medium laser

details: Conceived by Martinson Armaments of Terra in 2578 as an infantry support 'Mech, the Hoplite first saw service with the newly formed Star League Defense Force during the Reunification War before being relegated to Terran Hegemony militia units in the twenty-seventh century. While every Hoplite in the Inner Sphere was destroyed during the Amaris Crisis and the early Succession Wars, Aleksandr Kerensky's SLDF exiles found the simple and rugged design ideal for bolstering their forces in the months prior to the Exodus Civil War. After the mercenaries cut ties with the Clan Homeworlds in 3020, they also lost access to replacement Hoplites, with attrition bringing the design close to extinction by the Jihad. Intended for infantry support, the Hoplite is slow for a medium 'Mech, with a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. However its 11.5 tons of armor and heavy arsenal, both impressive for a 'Mech of its size, makes the Hoplite a rugged machine able to fight its way out of most situations using superior firepower. The 4B variant of the Hoplite is an early variant of the 'Mech that attempts to both alleviate the ammunition dependence of its primary weapon and increase its long-range damage potential. This is done by mounting a Magna Hellstar PPC as the primary weapon that is backed up by a massive Mitchell Systems LRM-15 launcher with two tons of reloads, giving it excellent long-range and indirect fire support capabilities. Largely extinct by the end of the Succession Wars, a single example remained on the field by the Fourth Succession War, fielded by Wolf's Dragoons.
yangsThoughts: Basically just replacing the autocannon with a PPC and a bigger missile launcher, I think they should have used a smaller missile launcher to get some more heat sinks on here.

details: Conceived by Martinson Armaments of Terra in 2578 as an infantry support 'Mech, the Hoplite first saw service with the newly formed Star League Defense Force during the Reunification War before being relegated to Terran Hegemony militia units in the twenty-seventh century. While every Hoplite in the Inner Sphere was destroyed during the Amaris Crisis and the early Succession Wars, Aleksandr Kerensky's SLDF exiles found the simple and rugged design ideal for bolstering their forces in the months prior to the Exodus Civil War. Selected by Wolf's Dragoons in the belief that the ancient design wouldn't arouse undue attention, it had the complete opposite effect, and only raised more questions as to the Dragoons' origins when they arrived in 3005. After the mercenaries cut ties with the Clan Homeworlds in 3020, they also lost access to replacement Hoplites, with attrition bringing the design close to extinction by the Jihad. Intended for infantry support, the Hoplite is slow for a medium 'Mech, with a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. However its 11.5 tons of armor and heavy arsenal, both impressive for a 'Mech of its size, makes the Hoplite a rugged machine able to fight its way out of most situations using superior firepower. Essentially a 'Royal' variant in everything but name, the 'Double B' Hoplite was put into production at Strana Mechty MechWorks and Eden Weaponry Consolidated just before the death of Aleksandr Kerensky and found its way both into the forces of the Pentagon Powers and the infant toumans of the Clans. The 'Double B' upgrades the LRM-15 to a Holly manufactured version, enhanced with an Artemis IV system, swaps the PPC for an Kinslaughter H-Class ER PPC, and increases the 'Mech's survivability with a Guardian ECM suite, CASE in both torsos, double heat sinks, and an anti-missile system. However, the design's speed and armor were not upgraded, enforcing the need to deploy the 4Bb as a fire-support unit.
yangsThoughts: The double heat sinks mean this thing can easily stay cool even when firing both weapons, so it's a very nice upgrade, I just wish they had sprung for a bigger engine.

After the mercenaries cut ties with the Clan Homeworlds in 3020, they also lost access to replacement Hoplites, with attrition bringing the design close to extinction by the Jihad. Intended for infantry support, the Hoplite is slow for a medium 'Mech, with a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. However its 11.5 tons of armor and heavy arsenal, both impressive for a 'Mech of its size, makes the Hoplite a rugged machine able to fight its way out of most situations using superior firepower. Effectively identical to the original Hoplite, the 4C carries a Luxor HV AC/10 as its primary weapon, which allows it to put a good amount of damage on a target, while generating a low amount of heat, and is backed up by a Conan/5 LRM-5 launcher. Arriving with Wolf's Dragoons, these were the first Hoplites seen in the Inner Sphere since their extinction during the Succession Wars.
yangsThoughts: I remember when these things first arrived with the Dragoons, everyone was wondering where the hell they came from with this thing. It's not the best firepower overall, but it's pretty cheap as a mech to throw it on.

details: Conceived by Martinson Armaments of Terra in 2578 as an infantry support 'Mech, the Hoplite first saw service with the newly formed Star League Defense Force during the Reunification War before being relegated to Terran Hegemony militia units in the twenty-seventh century. While every Hoplite in the Inner Sphere was destroyed during the Amaris Crisis and the early Succession Wars, Aleksandr Kerensky's SLDF exiles found the simple and rugged design ideal for bolstering their forces in the months prior to the Exodus Civil War. Selected by Wolf's Dragoons in the belief that the ancient design wouldn't arouse undue attention, it had the complete opposite effect, and only raised more questions as to the Dragoons' origins when they arrived in 3005. After the mercenaries cut ties with the Clan Homeworlds in 3020, they also lost access to replacement Hoplites, with attrition bringing the design close to extinction by the Jihad. Intended for infantry support, the Hoplite is slow for a medium 'Mech, with a top speed of only 64.8 km/h. However its 11.5 tons of armor and heavy arsenal, both impressive for a 'Mech of its size, makes the Hoplite a rugged machine able to fight its way out of most situations using superior firepower. A Hell's Horses field refit from Operation KLONDIKE, the 4Cb removes the weapons, electronics suite, and a heat sink in favor of a Gauss rifle, SRM-6, and ferro-fibrous armor.
yangsThoughts: A redesign that focuses more on sniping than close range fighting, but it's still capable of some decent short range punch with those missiles.

details: Initially created for the newly formed Star League Defense Force's use in the Reunification War, the popular design remained in production until the destruction of Stormvanger's Hesperus II Falcon line during the Third Battle of Hesperus II in 2789, becoming all but extinct until the arrival of Wolf's Dragoons in 3005. Well regarded by the mercenaries, the Dragoons upgraded their dwindling number of Falcons in the early 3050s before eventually commissioning Blackwell Industries to restart production of the design in early 3060 to produce advanced Clan-tech versions for their exclusive use, as well as more mundane and affordable versions for the general mercenary market. Originally developed as a field upgrade before becoming Blackwell's standard production model, the 4P Falcon carries a light arsenal that is primarily based around energy weapons. While the two Omnicron 1000 small lasers were retained, the original standard medium laser was swapped for a shorter-ranged but highly accurate Magna 400P medium pulse laser. Finally, for protection from missiles, the rear-facing machine guns were dropped for a forward-facing SureFire 444 anti-missile system and a single ton of ammunition.
yangsThoughts: Nice to see that some of Wolf's Dragoons mechs are spreading around to us here the periphery. This thing is fast and well armored, though not super heavily armed. You might want to upgrade the small lasers to mediums if you don't need to defend against missiles.

details: Initially created for the newly formed Star League Defense Force's use in the Reunification War, the popular design remained in production until the destruction of Stormvanger's Hesperus II Falcon line during the Third Battle of Hesperus II in 2789, becoming all but extinct until the arrival of Wolf's Dragoons in 3005. Well regarded by the mercenaries, the Dragoons upgraded their dwindling number of Falcons in the early 3050s before eventually commissioning Blackwell Industries to restart production of the design in early 3060 to produce advanced Clan-tech versions for their exclusive use, as well as more mundane and affordable versions for the general mercenary market. Built for the Royal SLDF Divisions at the captured Roe Weapon Systems plant on Apollo during the Amaris Civil War, the so-called Royal Falcon carried an experimental Parti-Kill snub-nose PPC backed up by two medium lasers. It was also equipped with double heat sinks, and was built on a Duralyte 300 endo steel chassis. All known examples and design specs were taken with the Exodus Fleet.
yangsThoughts: I didn't realize any of these things had survived till now, not sure who built them, but who ever it was its a nice upgrade of tonnage with that endosteel.

details: The Lynx was first produced in 2761 by Blue Shot Weapons of Solaris VII for use by the SLDF. One of the first BattleMechs to be designed around an ER PPC, the Lynx possesses a stunning combination of speed, mobility, armor, and firepower for any 'Mech of any weight class. However, Blue Shot Weapons never was able to produce this striker BattleMech in significant numbers. With the decline of technology of the Succession Wars, Blue Shot's production dwindled still, until the factory producing the Lynx was destroyed in 2928 by Marik raiders. The few remaining Lynxes were ultimately cannibalized for whatever could be salvaged. Beyond the Inner Sphere, a number of Lynx would accompany the Exodus fleet, serving as an operating partner with the Sling during Clan Smoke Jaguar's involvement in Operation KLONDIKE. The Lynx would remain popular with Jaguar warriors and would even be rebuilt with Clan-technology before being overshadowed by the introduction of the OmniMech. Relegated to second-line garrison troops, the so-called Lynx C would serve in the Jaguars doomed defense of Huntress against the Inner Sphere during Operation SERPENT. The Lynx is powered by a Nissan 275 XL extralight engine that allows the 'Mech to reach speeds of up to 86.4 km/h. Adding to its mobility, five Luxor Load Lifters jump jets provide the Lynx with a jumping distance of up to 150 meters. The Lynx is protected by 12 tons of Kallon Unity Weave armor, which almost provides the maximum protection that a 55 ton 'Mech can carry. Finally, to dissipate the waste heat produced by all of its energy weapons, the Lynx has 15 double heat sinks. The Lynx is intended for long term independent operations as well as protracted combat. To carry out the 'Mech's intended combat roles, it carries a Defiance 1001 ER PPC in its right arm, giving it an effective range of 690 meters. Backing this up is a Defiance B3L large laser mounted in the right torso that gives the Lynx even more firepower in the medium to long range envelope, and for close range combat the Lynx has a quartet of Defiance B3M medium lasers split between the center, left and a pair fitted in the left arm that can eviscerate any enemy that gets too close.
yangsThoughts: Decently fast, powerful jumpjets and a massive amount of energy firepower make this mech powerful enough to deal with enemies significantly larger than itself. Watch out for the heat level though, it can easily leave you shut down at the wrong time. Interestingly, we found a couple of missile hardpoints on the chassis, let the techs know if you wanna do something with em.

details: The Lynx was first produced in 2761 by Blue Shot Weapons of Solaris VII for use by the SLDF. One of the first BattleMechs to be designed around an ER PPC, the Lynx possesses a stunning combination of speed, mobility, armor, and firepower for any 'Mech of any weight class. However, Blue Shot Weapons never was able to produce this striker BattleMech in significant numbers. With the decline of technology of the Succession Wars, Blue Shot's production dwindled still, until the factory producing the Lynx was destroyed in 2928 by Marik raiders. The few remaining Lynxes were ultimately cannibalized for whatever could be salvaged. Beyond the Inner Sphere, a number of Lynx would accompany the Exodus fleet, serving as an operating partner with the Sling during Clan Smoke Jaguar's involvement in Operation KLONDIKE. The Lynx would remain popular with Jaguar warriors and would even be rebuilt with Clan-technology before being overshadowed by the introduction of the OmniMech. Relegated to second-line garrison troops, the so-called Lynx C would serve in the Jaguars doomed defense of Huntress against the Inner Sphere during Operation SERPENT. The Lynx is powered by a Nissan 275 XL extralight engine that allows the 'Mech to reach speeds of up to 86.4 km/h. Adding to its mobility, five Luxor Load Lifters jump jets provide the Lynx with a jumping distance of up to 150 meters. The Lynx is protected by 12 tons of Kallon Unity Weave armor, which almost provides the maximum protection that a 55 ton 'Mech can carry. Finally, to dissipate the waste heat produced by all of its energy weapons, the Lynx has 15 double heat sinks. Clan Smoke Jaguar's Clan technology rebuild retaining the same ground speed and jump radius as its forebear, but the weapon suite is now an Type 22 ER PPC, Series 7J ER large laser, Kolibri Delta large pulse laser, Series 2a ER medium laser, and Series 1b ER small laser. To offset the increased heat, engineers replaced two tons of armor with double heat sinks, bringing the total to 17.
yangsThoughts: Pretty much the same basic mech as the regular Lynx, its already so good the Clans couldn't do that much to improve it other than put on Endo Steel and upgrade the guns to clan-spec. The problem is that the upgrade to clan spec energy weapons makes it generate so much more heat that it overheats in half the time.

details: The Balar was concepted by Luthien Armor Works after the Clan Invasion and was based on Smoke Jaguar Ebon Jaguars, captured during the Battle of Luthien. Built around a similarly fast 350-rated XL Engine and constructed on a similar, but heavier, Endo-Steel frame than its progenitor, the Balar entered production right at the end of the 3050s and was fairly rapidly sold to other manufacturers throughout the Inner Sphere in order to recoup manufacturing costs. The Balar's Prime configuration uses a LB-10X Autocannon for primary punch while an ER PPC and LRM-10 cover longer ranges. A Streak SRM-2 helps to ward away pesky light mechs that get too close for comfort.
yangsThoughts: It weighs more than the original Clan version it's based on, but it's got less firepower because of the heavier Inner Sphere weaponry. It's a fine mech, but you might want to put Clan weaponry on it to maximize the firepower.

details: The Balar was concepted by Luthien Armor Works after the Clan Invasion and was based on Smoke Jaguar Ebon Jaguars, captured during the Battle of Luthien. Built around a similarly fast 350-rated XL Engine and constructed on a similar, but heavier, Endo-Steel frame than its progenitor, the Balar entered production right at the end of the 3050s and was fairly rapidly sold to other manufacturers throughout the Inner Sphere in order to recoup manufacturing costs. The Balar's Prime configuration uses a LB-10X Autocannon for primary punch while an ER PPC and LRM-10 cover longer ranges. A Streak SRM-2 helps to ward away pesky light mechs that get too close for comfort.
yangsThoughts: A little more focused punch than the prime layout, but I think the machine guns could just be dropped to reduce explosion risk.

details: The Balar was concepted by Luthien Armor Works after the Clan Invasion and was based on Smoke Jaguar Ebon Jaguars, captured during the Battle of Luthien. Built around a similarly fast 350-rated XL Engine and constructed on a similar, but heavier, Endo-Steel frame than its progenitor, the Balar entered production right at the end of the 3050s and was fairly rapidly sold to other manufacturers throughout the Inner Sphere in order to recoup manufacturing costs. The Balar's B configuration is an all-energy config, centered around two Large Pulse Lasers and a PPC. For backup firepower it carries an ER Medium Laser, Medium Pulse Laser, and two Small Lasers. For extra battlefield utility it carries a rare Beagle Active Probe and to keep it cold it carries 15 double heat sinks
yangsThoughts: All energy based, so it has nothing to explode and can just keep fighting forever as long as you don't overheat, but it does generate a lot of heat.

details: The Balar was concepted by Luthien Armor Works after the Clan Invasion and was based on Smoke Jaguar Ebon Jaguars, captured during the Battle of Luthien. Built around a similarly fast 350-rated XL Engine and constructed on a similar, but heavier, Endo-Steel frame than its progenitor, the Balar entered production right at the end of the 3050s and was fairly rapidly sold to other manufacturers throughout the Inner Sphere in order to recoup manufacturing costs.  The Balar's C is a mixed attacker, using a LB-2X Autocannon and twin LRM-15s for long range firepower while making use of twin SRM-4s at close range. Five total tons of ammunition keep the guns firing in any situation.
yangsThoughts: The exact opposite of the B layout, this thing is all ammo based, which means it basically never overheats, but it has a lot of stuff that can blow up inside it.

details: Designed by Hollis Incorporated as a platform to sell Magna Metals engines, the Cyclone A1 began mass-manufacture in 2631 and fell out of production when the Star League fell, only returning in the 3060s. Sporting a Magna 210XL Fusion engine, the Cyclone A1 can reach and maintain speeds up to 118kph. Sold as a Scout Mech, the A1 features both a Guardian ECM Suite and a Beagle Active Probe which leaves just enough space for its light weapons payload consisting of a pair of Longfire V LRM 5s in the arms and a pair of Magna Mk II Medium Lasers in the side torsos.
yangsThoughts: Nice to see that some of the older designs have made a comeback, this thing is extremely fast, but very lightly armed, so you'll need to keep it moving if you want it to survive.

details: After the lack-luster reaction to the Cyclone B2 in most markets, Magna Metals Inc., in collaboration with Hollis Incorporated, developed the C1 variant. The Cyclone C1 is built around the same Magna 210XL engine of the A1 variant. Using the same Endo-Steel chassis of the B2, the C1 replaces the weapons payload of the B2 with a Magna Firestar ER PPC, a pair of Magna Mk VI ER Medium Lasers, a Guardian ECM Suite, and an additional Double Heat Sink.
yangsThoughts: An all energy version of the classic, the extra weight savings from that chassis mean you can mount a surprisingly heavy amount of energy weaponry for how light the mech is.

details: With their immediate neighbors working on superheavy battlemechs, House Marik found itself behind the technological 8-ball. In order to ensure the FWLM could compete on a new, heavier battlefield, Thomas Marik commissioned Project Olympian, House Marik's superheavy construction project. The resultant mech, the AL1-G8R Olympian, proved effective albeit expensive. Armed with twin capacitor fed Heavy PPCs as well as four each MML-5s and ER Small Lasers. The Olympian proved successful though its cost and grueling maintenance requirements kept it from wider production.
yangsThoughts: A nice mix of energy and missile support, it can hold its own in most situations, but I can't help but wonder how much better it might perform with Clan grade equipment.

details: With their immediate neighbors working on superheavy battlemechs, House Marik found itself behind the technological 8-ball. In order to ensure the FWLM could compete on a new, heavier battlefield, Thomas Marik commissioned Project Olympian, House Marik's superheavy construction project. The resultant mech, the AL1-G8R Olympian, proved effective albeit expensive. Some 'Mechs are hammers and some are scalpels. At least, that's the philosophy that this 'Mech's designer had. Where most other superheavy BattleMechs are hammers, winning encounter with brute force and raw power, this Olympian-class superheavy BattleMech wins encounters via surgical precision of its six Large Pulse Lasers. While less firepower than might be expected from a superheavy, the extremely high accuracy means that the Scalpel is always scoring hits and slagging away armor.
yangsThoughts: High accuracy, and decent range, you can't really go wrong with that. Just keep in mind it only has the one weapon range, so it can be outranged if you get overconfident.

details: During the Golden Century, Clan Cloud Cobra, just like most of the clans, enjoyed considerable growth and relative peace which allowed them to pour time and resources into laboratories and research and development projects. One such result of these efforts was the Medusa, a 50-ton brain-child of Josian Cloister scientists. The 300XL Engine, Endo-Steel chassis, and Ferro-Fibrous armor makes the Medusa a considerably expensive 'Mech yet an effective one. First and foremost, the Medusa was designed around a trio of Clan SRM-6s spread across the torso and around the 'mech's distinctive cockpit cowling. As a longer-ranged complement to the array of SRMs, the design mounts a Large Pulse Laser and two ER Medium Lasers.
yangsThoughts: A nicely balanced medium 'Mech, not much for long range firepower other than the pulse laser, but those SRMs will rip through anything it can get close to.

details: During the Golden Century, Clan Cloud Cobra, just like most of the clans, enjoyed considerable growth and relative peace which allowed them to pour time and resources into laboratories and research and development projects. One such result of these efforts was the Medusa, a 50-ton brain-child of Josian Cloister scientists. The 300XL Engine, Endo-Steel chassis, and Ferro-Fibrous armor makes the Medusa a considerably expensive 'Mech yet an effective one. The MED-3 takes an unusual tack with the mech, concentrating the firepower into a massive Ultra AC/20, backed up by a SRM-6 and a pair of ER Medium Lasers.
yangsThoughts: Pretty much just a transport for the UAC 20 with some extra guns strapped to the side, great up close but a bit fragile when getting in range.

details: During the Golden Century, Clan Cloud Cobra, just like most of the clans, enjoyed considerable growth and relative peace which allowed them to pour time and resources into laboratories and research and development projects. One such result of these efforts was the Medusa, a 50-ton brain-child of Josian Cloister scientists. The 300XL Engine, Endo-Steel chassis, and Ferro-Fibrous armor makes the Medusa a considerably expensive 'Mech yet an effective one. The MED-4 focuses on being a missile cloud, mounting a whopping seven ATM-3 launchers as well as a single ER Large Laser. 6 tons of ammo keeps the ATMs firing on all cylinders.
yangsThoughts: A weird design overall, it really goes all in on the missiles but at the same time it spreads them out strangely across the chassis.

details: StarCorps Industries's Project: SORCERER was shrouded in such secrecy that the first Sorcerer Battlemechs just seemed to appear in Star League Defense Force ranks where they were met with varying degrees of suspicion. The 95-ton Sorcerer 1A is built around a PlasmaStar 380XL engine to propel it to a respectable 64.8km/h which enables the Sorcerer to bring its arsenal and Guardian ECM Suite to the enemy. The design carries a pair of Ceres Arms Smasher PPCs, a pair of Donal Snub-Nose PPCs, a pair of Magna MkII Medium Lasers, and a Magna MkI Small Laser. A staggering twenty Double Heat Sinks is barely enough to keep the weapons' heat generation in check.
yangsThoughts: A big PPC carrying brawler, this thing is good at both long and short ranges with those snubbies, but it definitely has issues with overheating.

details: Shortly after the Sorcerer 1A began walking off of the StarCorps Industries production lines on Fletcher, the Sorcerer 1B began production in the satellite facility on Crofton. The 1B variant trades in the array of PPCs and Lasers for a Blankenburg Technologies ER PPC, a pair of Artemis IV-enhanced Holly LRM15s, and a trio of Defiance P5M Medium Pulse Lasers. The reduced Heat generation of this payload allowed designers to remove the five external Double Heat Sinks and freed up room for CASE in the right torso.
yangsThoughts: An interesting layout change, swapping the PPCs for LRMs allows it to do indirect fire, but it could probably use updating to either Clan spec or MMLs these days.

details: Despite being designed for Urban and close-quarters warfare, the Sorcerer 1C variant drops the pair Snub-Nosed PPCs of the original Sorcerer 1A in favor of the easier-to-obtain Blankenburg Large Pulse Lasers. Less surprisingly, the 1A's PPC and laser complement is replaced by a quartet of Magna 400P Medium Pulse Lasers and a pair of Holly Streak SRM2s. The Sorcerer 1C dedicates eight tons to mount four Pitban LFT-50 jump jets to propel it 120 meters in short bursts and utilizes only sixteen Double Heat Sinks.
yangsThoughts: Honestly, I have to say that the designers of this were a bit crazy to swap out the snubbies for pulse lasers when making something for urban combat, even if they are cheaper.

details: Developed by Iroquois Machinery Limited as their first entry into true BattleMech production, the Broadside has been met with heavy skepticism by Davion MechWarriors. However, after an impressive showing from a cadet in the 1st Conroe Training Battalion against a Kuritan Atlas, the Broadside is coming to be more broadly accepted. The design is built around a 300-rated fusion engine with Endo-Steel bones for extra weight saving. True to its Davion roots, the Broadside uses a mixture of autocannons for primary firepower (a LB-10X, UAC/10, and RAC/2 specifically). For backup, the Broadside carries a brace of lasers and a flamer. The dense armor provides more than enough protection while CASE II protects the substantial ammunition bins.
yangsThoughts: A lot of power in this thing, not very fast, but a nice heir to the design of the Annihilator, it even has the same ballistic compensators.

details: Developed by Iroquois Machinery Limited as their first entry into true BattleMech production, the Broadside has been met with heavy skepticism by Davion MechWarriors. However, after an impressive showing from a cadet in the 1st Conroe Training Battalion against a Kuritan Atlas, the Broadside is coming to be more broadly accepted. The design is built around a 300-rated fusion engine with Endo-Steel bones for extra weight saving. True to its Davion roots, the Broadside uses a mixture of autocannons for primary firepower, a pair of Ultra Autocannon/10s and an Ultra Autocannon/5 in this case. For backup, the Broadside carries a small number of ER Medium Lasers. The dense armor provides more than enough protection while CASE II protects the substantial ammunition bins.
yangsThoughts: More consistency in the autocannons but shorter ranger brackets, you'll have to be careful to avoid getting flanked while bringing the bigger 10's to bear.

details: The Broadside is a relatively new chassis to the battlefields of the Inner Sphere but Solaris VII never misses an opportunity for innovation. In that vein comes Blunderbuss, a Broadside with a knock-out punch. Built on the same Endo-Steel skeleton as the regular Broadside, the Blunderbuss swaps the rugged standard engine for an advanced Clan-grade 300-rated XL. It uses this extra free weight to mount a dizzying array of firepower, with a Silver Bullet Gauss Rifle and a Hyper-Assault Gauss Rifle/30 forming the core of the array. An Clan-spec Gauss Rifle and ER PPC round out the guns, ensuring that the Blunderbuss can one-tap just about anything it can catch. The heat is decently managed by the 12 double heat sinks mounted to it.
yangsThoughts: An odd layout, with mismatched Gauss Rifles and I'm not honestly sure why, but it seems to work ok so I guess if it works it works.

details: A lumbering mountain of armor and weaponry, Solaris Arms' massive assault 'Mech definitely lives up to the name of Juggernaut in the Solaris Games. While not a flashy design, Solaris Arms kept up steady production of the design since the early 3050s, the company addressed complaints of the 'Mech's bulky chassis and outboard sensor arrays with a sleeker refreshed model in the mid-3060s. The original Juggernaut model carries regular Large and Medium Pulse Lasers in place of the advanced X-Pulse lasers. Its ammunition bins are protected with CASE. It carries four Small Pulse Lasers in place of the coolant pods, as well as four additional Machine Guns, though this comes at the cost of four Double Heat Sinks. The original design had poorly placed sensor units that were heavily criticized by both technicians and MechWarriors.
yangsThoughts: Oh man, I've always liked this design overall, though I had the mech techs reposition those sensor units before I even let you see the thing in the bay. It mounts a huge number of machine guns and lasers, which makes it work well in the arenas, but you may want to put on some more ranged equipment for use on the battlefield.

details: A lumbering mountain of armor and weaponry, Solaris Arms' massive assault 'Mech definitely lives up to the name of Juggernaut in the Solaris Games. While not a flashy design, Solaris Arms kept up steady production of the design since the early 3050s, the company addressed complaints of the 'Mech's bulky chassis and outboard sensor arrays with a sleeker refreshed model in the mid-3060s. Sixteen and half tons of Valiant Chainmail armor protect a GM 270 fusion engine, propelling the 'Mech at fairly standard assault 'Mech speed of 54 km/h. A whopping sixteen Double Heat Sinks are still not enough to vent the massive volume of waste heat the Juggernaut's advanced lasers produce, with the 'Mech also carrying four Coolant Pods in an desperate attempt to quickly disperse the large amount of heat that it can generate. The modern R9T2 Juggernaut's main armament is a trio of Sutel Precision Line Large Pulse Lasers with Bethel Labs X-Pulse, one in each arm with one in its center torso, backed up by three Sutel Precision Line Medium Pulse Lasers with Bethel Labs X-Pulse divided between each arm and the head. Due to the 'Mech's lack of hand actuators, at knife-fighting range the Juggernaut is protected by a staggering twelve LFN Lindblad Machine Guns fed from two tons of reloads.
yangsThoughts: The big problem with this one has got to be the x-pulse laser's massive heat output, 16 doubles is just not up to the task of keeping it cold, and you'll probably want to just pull most of the machine guns off in favor of more heat sinks.

details: A lumbering mountain of armor and weaponry, Solaris Arms' massive assault 'Mech definitely lives up to the name of Juggernaut in the Solaris Games. While not a flashy design, Solaris Arms kept up steady production of the design since the early 3050s, the company addressed complaints of the 'Mech's bulky chassis and outboard sensor arrays with a sleeker refreshed model in the mid-3060s. Sixteen and half tons of Valiant Chainmail armor protect a GM 270 fusion engine, propelling the 'Mech at fairly standard assault 'Mech speed of 54 km/h. A whopping sixteen Double Heat Sinks are still not enough to vent the massive volume of waste heat the Juggernaut's advanced lasers produce, with the 'Mech also carrying four Coolant Pods in an desperate attempt to quickly disperse the large amount of heat that it can generate. The modern R9T2 Juggernaut's main armament is a trio of Sutel Precision Line Large Pulse Lasers with Bethel Labs X-Pulse, one in each arm with one in its center torso, backed up by three Sutel Precision Line Medium Pulse Lasers with Bethel Labs X-Pulse divided between each arm and the head. This variant replaces the Machine Guns with the Magshot gauss weapons. It also carries two more coolant pods compared to the JG-R9T2.
yangsThoughts: Now, Magshots are these new gauss type things out of Fed Sun space, basically a super tiny Gauss Rifle that has the longest range of any support weapon, I just wish they weren't so bulky. They're way better range than the machine guns they replaced, so this is a nice upgrade overall.

details: A lumbering mountain of armor and weaponry, Solaris Arms' massive assault 'Mech definitely lives up to the name of Juggernaut in the Solaris Games. In the mid-3080's Clan Sea Fox approached a Solaris Arms struggling to deal with the devastation of the Jihad with an offer to sponsor construction of new Juggernauts as long as they used a generous helping of Clan-Spec equipment provided by the Clan. The advertising deal was highly successful and the newly reworked 'Mech taking advantage of both a Clan-spec XL engine and Clan-spec EndoSteel was able to mount a much more lethal array of Clan-spec pulse lasers and AP Gauss Rifles. While it didn't fare quite as well in the arena as Solaris Arms had hoped, it definitely accomplished Clan Sea Fox's goal of driving more business for their merchant fleet.
yangsThoughts: I'm pretty sure this is as powerful as this basic design setup can go at this point, that Clan-spec stuff lets it get an astonishing amount of firepower, and it even has a bit of room left over for some missiles or ballistics if you want em.

details: The BattleHammer was the launching point for Pafftek Industries. It is a production derivative of a frankenmech made early in the Clan Invasion by a merc company. The 'Arena Flagship' utilizes Clan Tech
yangsThoughts: This is similar in concept to the Templar's D configuration, except it uses the much more effective Clan-spec Gauss Rifles instead, if you want to upgrade it, consider putting some Hyper Assault Gauss rifles on instead.

details: Pafftek Industries, trying to reduce the cost of the exceptional BattleHammer, made a series utilizing only inner-sphere tech. This, the most basic version of the BattleHammer, utilizes technology readily available in the post-Helm memory core era. It is available to anyone except the Clans and the Word of Blake.
yangsThoughts: A sizable downgrade overall, but the techs can fix that up with some weapon swaps. The fundamental design is pretty solid if a bit limited on weapon space.

details: Pafftek Industries, attempting to build on the proven performance of the Battlehammer 2D, replaced the arm autocannons with Rotary Autocannon 5's for improved firepower backed up by a quartet of medium pulse lasers in the torsos, and a single Clan-spec ER PPC in the center torso. This required switching to an endosteel chassis to free up enough tonnage.
yangsThoughts: A very high firepower variant, it doesn't have the range of the flagship, but the raw firepower is much higher.

details: The 70 ton Black Heart is a rare Word of Blake Mech; deployed exclusively among their Manei Domini elite. Powered by a 280-rated XL Engine and built on an Endo Steel chassis, the Black Heart BH-1 is armed with a pair of Light Gauss Rifles. A pair of Medium Lasers and a trio of SRMs make up the Mechs secondary armament while a combination of Guardian ECM and an AMS boost it’s defensive capabilities.
yangsThoughts: Good speed, decent armament for all ranges and decent armor, this mech has basically everything you could want out of a heavy mech, except possibly some Clan-spec upgrades.

details: In the 3060s, ComStar and the Republic of the Sphere found themselves in need of a new line combat heavy BattleMech. Drawing on theoretical mock-ups from ComStar's Star League archives, they developed a heavy version of the venerable Crab BattleMech. To respect the lineage between the Crab and the assault-weight King Crab, this new chassis was named the Prince Crab. The Prince Crab is built on an Endo-Composite frame with a standard 300-rated fusion engine for power. For firepower the PRC-4B carries twin LB-10X Autocannons, backed up by four torso-mounted ER Medium Lasers and a solitary ER Small Laser. 10 double heat sinks don't quite manage the heat while four tons of CASE II protected ammunition ensure the guns never stop firing.
yangsThoughts: An interesting midpoint between the regular Crab and the King, this thing is well armored and durable, with a good mix of weaponry.

details: In the 3060s, ComStar and the Republic of the Sphere found themselves in need of a new line combat heavy BattleMech. Drawing on theoretical mock-ups from ComStar's Star League archives, they developed a heavy version of the venerable Crab BattleMech. To respect the lineage between the Crab and the assault-weight King Crab, this new chassis was named the Prince Crab. The Prince Crab is built on an Endo-Composite frame with a standard 300-rated fusion engine for power. For firepower the PRC-5S is equipped with a quartet of SRM-6s supplied with three tons of CASE II protected ammunition. For ammo-free firepower, the -5S uses twin Snub-Nose PPCs and a lone ER Small Laser. 16 double heat sinks struggle to keep it all cooled.
yangsThoughts: An interesting midpoint between the regular Crab and the King, this thing really goes in for the close range battling with the heavy SRM loadout, just watch out for getting outranged.

details: After the Prince Crab's debut, the Republic Institute of Strategic Combat decided to make use of the chassis for a weapon testing platform, specifically to test the newly-developed Hyper Laser. Armed with a RISC Hyper Laser, as well as two standard SRM-6s for close-range backup, the PRC-5X proved a deadly combatant. Plentiful heat management gear combined with a Guardian ECM and sturdy armor keeps the -5X functional in all environments while CASE II ensures the design is durable even against ammunition explosions.
yangsThoughts: An interesting midpoint between the regular Crab and the King, that Hyper Laser thing is high damage, but that heat output is way too high to sustain for too long.

details: As the Inner Sphere developed Omnimech technology in the 3050s, the Taurian Concordat watched with interest. Finally, in the early 3060s, thanks to some good old-fashioned corporate espionage, the Concordat managed to steal basic Omnimech technology plans from the Federated Suns and developed their own Omnimech, the Liberty. To keep costs low, the Liberty is made with simple off-the-shelf weapons technology. The Liberty Prime is armed with twin PPCs, a LRM-15, two Medium Lasers, and 4 Small Lasers. Endo-Steel and an XL Engine provide the weight for the payload.
yangsThoughts: A simple enough omnimech design, easy to manufacture by omnimech standards, and with a decent but not astonishing weapons loadout. The stock setup is able to work for most uses, but you'll probably want to rework it a bit.

details: As the Inner Sphere developed Omnimech technology in the 3050s, the Taurian Concordat watched with interest. Finally, in the early 3060s, thanks to some good old-fashioned corporate espionage, the Concordat managed to steal basic Omnimech technology plans from the Federated Suns and developed their own Omnimech, the Liberty. To keep costs low, the Liberty is made with simple off-the-shelf weapons technology. The Liberty A is armed for range, with a pair of LRM-20s providing core firepower. For up close, it can call upon a quartet of Rocket Launcher/10s.
yangsThoughts: I'm not sold on this setup really, the rocket launchers are cheap to run, sure, but if you need more than 4 volleys, then you're sunk. I'd much rather trust my life to an SRM or something else that won't run out of ammo right when I need it the most.

details: As the Inner Sphere developed Omnimech technology in the 3050s, the Taurian Concordat watched with interest. Finally, in the early 3060s, thanks to some good old-fashioned corporate espionage, the Concordat managed to steal basic Omnimech technology plans from the Federated Suns and developed their own Omnimech, the Liberty. To keep costs low, the Liberty is made with simple off-the-shelf weapons technology. The Liberty C is equipped with an AC/5 for punch and a Large Laser for range. Up close it can call on four SRM-4s.
yangsThoughts: Probably the cheapest weapons loadout I've ever seen on an omnimech, I feel like if you're cheaping out this much on the guns for a mech that you're putting an expensive XL engine and Endo Steel in, you've gotta rethink your priorities.

details: As the Inner Sphere developed Omnimech technology in the 3050s, the Taurian Concordat watched with interest. Finally, in the early 3060s, thanks to some good old-fashioned corporate espionage, the Concordat managed to steal basic Omnimech technology plans from the Federated Suns and developed their own Omnimech, the Liberty. To keep costs low, the Liberty is made with simple off-the-shelf weapons technology. The Liberty D replicates the premier Taurian assault mech, the Mackie. Armed with an AC/20, twin Large Lasers, and twin CT-mounted Small Lasers, the Liberty D is quite versatile for the price.
yangsThoughts: It's at least not quite as cheap as the C loadout, but it's still bizarrely cheap for the weapons on a chassis this pricy to build.

details: Defiance Industries has used the Mackenzie as a dedicated testing platform for ballistic weaponry for years, most recently as part of development testing for the Hyper Gauss Rifle in conjunction with Clan Jade Falcon. While the weapon did eventually get manufactured, development took longer than initially expected and so the company wound up with an excess of testbed models which they then sold off after replacing the testing weaponry with production versions. The Mackensie is built around a torso cockpit and a 280-rated Extra-Light Fusion Engine for power. For backup weaponry, the Mackensie mounts an ER PPC and a quartet of Magshots. Four jump jets give it extra positioning ability.
yangsThoughts: A pretty good heavy gauss platform overall, the big hit from the gauss is effective against most things though the support weaponry is a bit weak.

details: After launching a series of retaliatory raids against the Lyran Alliance as punishment for stealing the Light Engine plans from them, Wolfs Dragoons wound up finding a sizable cache of intact King Crab arms but without the corresponding torsos and legs. Unwilling to waste such a windfall, the Dragoon's kept them in storage in hopes of finding a use for them later. After the destruction of the Jihad, Wolfs Dragoons contracted Irian BattleMechs Unlimited to make use of those arms by attaching them to reworked Marauder II torsos and legs. The resulting mech was then fitted with extensive Clan-spec equipment to produce this unusual design. The 1A configuration mounts twin Clan-spec Large Pulse Lasers and Ultra Autocannon 20s in the arms. These heavy arm weapons are supported by twin LRM 15 launchers on the shoulders for indirect fire support while the mech lumbers into range of its main guns.
yangsThoughts: When you brought this into my mech bay I initially thought you were having a laugh at my expense, but for all it's ugly appearance, this thing is a solid heavy brawler with decent fire support abilities, just keep an eye on that heat guage.

details: After launching a series of retaliatory raids against the Lyran Alliance as punishment for stealing the Light Engine plans from them, Wolfs Dragoons wound up finding a sizable cache of intact King Crab arms but without the corresponding torsos and legs. Unwilling to waste such a windfall, the Dragoon's kept them in storage in hopes of finding a use for them later. After the destruction of the Jihad, Wolfs Dragoons contracted Irian BattleMechs Unlimited to make use of those arms by attaching them to reworked Marauder II torsos and legs. The resulting mech was then fitted with extensive Clan-spec equipment to produce this unusual design. The 1B configuration is designed more for long range fire support, mounting twin Clan-spec Large Pulse Lasers and Inner Sphere grade Rotary Autocannon 5s in the arms. These heavy arm weapons are supported by twin LRM 15 launchers on the shoulders for additional fire support.
yangsThoughts: More heavy fire support on a very weird looking mech, but for all the strange appearance I can't fault the actual performance.

details: After launching a series of retaliatory raids against the Lyran Alliance as punishment for stealing the Light Engine plans from them, Wolfs Dragoons wound up finding a sizable cache of intact King Crab arms but without the corresponding torsos and legs. Unwilling to waste such a windfall, the Dragoon's kept them in storage in hopes of finding a use for them later. After the destruction of the Jihad, Wolfs Dragoons contracted Irian BattleMechs Unlimited to make use of those arms by attaching them to reworked Marauder II torsos and legs. The resulting mech was then fitted with extensive Clan-spec equipment to produce this unusual design. The 1C configuration is based on the old 'Clanbuster' configuration of the King Crab; mounting twin Clan-spec Gauss Rifles in the arms backed by twin ER PPCs, while keeping the ubiquitous twin LRM 15s that all the variants share.
yangsThoughts: I like this design a lot, way better heat profile, and much better ammo handling, you might even be able to squeeze on some upgraded missile launchers if you don't mind it running a bit hot or lighter on ammo.

details: After launching a series of retaliatory raids against the Lyran Alliance as punishment for stealing the Light Engine plans from them, Wolfs Dragoons wound up finding a sizable cache of intact King Crab arms but without the corresponding torsos and legs. Unwilling to waste such a windfall, the Dragoon's kept them in storage in hopes of finding a use for them later. After the destruction of the Jihad, Wolfs Dragoons contracted Irian BattleMechs Unlimited to make use of those arms by attaching them to reworked Marauder II torsos and legs. The resulting mech was then fitted with extensive Clan-spec equipment to produce this unusual design. The 1D configuration builds on the 1C by replacing the Gauss Rifles with HAG30s for maximum firepower while keeping the twin ER PPCs and LRM 15s.
yangsThoughts: Really going for the super heavy firewpower with this one, it might be slightly overkill for some situations but the raw firewpower is undeniable.

details: A home-grown design from the Aurigan Reach, it was designed fairly soon after the civil war situation was resolved. Built to bring a light Gauss Rifle into combat with some defensive armament around it, it mounts the rifle in the torso for stability and has an extensive set of small and medium lasers for protection against close opponents.
yangsThoughts: Nice to see Lady Arano's techs keeping busy, the Light Gauss has a great range so it's easily capable of outranging almost anything else on the field, but it's definitely lacking a bit in punch compared to things like the Hollander, though the better support weaponry obviously helps keep it alive much better.

details: A home-grown design from the Aurigan Reach, it was designed fairly soon after the civil war situation was resolved. Built to bring a light Gauss Rifle into combat with some defensive armament around it, it mounts the rifle in the torso for stability and has an extensive set of small and medium lasers for protection against close opponents. For handling close in firepower needs, designers replaced the light Gauss Rifle with an LBX 10 while leaving the rest of the mech the same.
yangsThoughts: I'm not sold super well on this one, the lack of a good long range weapon on this version is kind of a weakness.

details: A home-grown design from the Aurigan Reach, it was designed fairly soon after the civil war situation was resolved. Built to bring a light Gauss Rifle into combat with some defensive armament around it, it mounts the rifle in the torso for stability and has an extensive set of small and medium lasers for protection against close opponents. To remove ammunition dependence, the light Gauss was replaed with a Heavy PPC.
yangsThoughts: This is a nicer design, the heavy impact of that PPC is very good at busting through armor and disrupting targets, but it would be better if replaced with a Clan-spec one, as always with energy weapons.

details: This arena brawler is called the Ion Raptor, named for its inventive gladiator creator, The Raptor (real name unknown). Designed in concert with Zellbringen Stables (who later went on to sell a more reasonable version named the Ion on the market), the Ion Raptor is a Clantech frankenmech in the veins of the Prometheus. Armed with a battery of six Light PPCs and four Clan-spec Streak SRM-4s and powered by a Clan XL Engine, the real innovation on the Ion Raptor is its use of Laser Heat Sinks, a rare and temperamental technology. The mech's arena record speaks for itself though, with a plethora of wins backing its weird loadout.
yangsThoughts: A kind of weird design, but it works pretty well overall, you'd probably get better performance if you got rid of the light PPCs and put on the proper Clan-spec ER PPCs.

details: Designed by Zellbringen Stables and patterned on a more experimental Solaris VII arena design, the Ion has been designed for extended battlefield endurance. Originally based on a Clan-tech frankenmech arena design called the Ion Raptor, the Ion is a more sober, all Inner Sphere technology, version meant for sale on the market. Powered by a 280-rated Extra Light Fusion Engine and built on a lightweight Endo-Steel frame, the Ion carries a powerful punch: 6 Light PPCs provide ranged firepower while 4 SRM-4s give short-ranged punch. 15 Double Heat Sinks strain to keep the mech cool. The Ion was a moderate success with the Great Houses and appears in all five militaries.
yangsThoughts: Basically the same as the -1R but with worse tech weapons, the obvious upgrades to clan spec equipment will help this out, whether you stick with the SRMs or upgrade to Streaks.

details: Designed by Zellbringen Stables and patterned on a more experimental Solaris VII arena design, the Ion has been designed for extended battlefield endurance. Originally based on a Clan-tech frankenmech arena design called the Ion Raptor, the Ion is a more sober, all Inner Sphere technology, version meant for sale on the market. Powered by a 280-rated Extra Light Fusion Engine and built on a lightweight Endo-Steel frame, the Ion carries a powerful punch: dual Heavy PPCs give grit and dual MRM-10s give crit potential while a battery of heat sinks strain to keep it all controlled.
yangsThoughts: A heavy close in brawler with decent heat management, you could swap in some MMLs if you want to get some indirect fire ability for actual battlefield use instead

details: Designed by Zellbringen Stables and patterned on a more experimental Solaris VII arena design, the Ion has been designed for extended battlefield endurance. Originally based on a Clan-tech frankenmech arena design called the Ion Raptor, the Ion is a more sober, all Inner Sphere technology, version meant for sale on the market. Powered by a 280-rated XL Fusion Engine and built on a lightweight Endo-Steel frame, the Ion carries a powerful punch: two each of HVAC/5s, LRM-10s, and ER Medium Lasers keep the mech shooting at all ranges while six tons of ammo keeps the guns live for a long time.
yangsThoughts: A long range striker, I might want some Ultra Autocannons instead of the HVACs for the raw damage, but you'll have to watch the ammo supply if you make that change.

details: Constructed by Earthwerks Incorporated to appeal to the Periphery market, the base model of the Thunderstone is built to use very little expensive or sophisticated construction materials, it uses all standard double heat sinks, a standard 270 fusion engine and standard armor, the only expensive construction material being the relatively inexpensive Endo-Composite structure and the CASE II systems protecting the buyer's investment. Marketed as being cheap to maintain and upgrade, Periphery states were quick to buy models of the mech to counter the proliferation of super heavy mechs across the Inner Sphere. Since buyers were expected to not have sufficient resources to equip multiple pilots in a squad with 'Mechs of this size, the 'Mech was built to have weapons for any situation, twin extended range medium and large lasers, twin ER PPCs, an LRM 20 and two SRM 6s. Unfortunately, the mech does not mount sufficient heat sinks to allow for firing all of these weapons simultaneously, so it was fitted with several coolant bottles to handle the load in emergencies.
yangsThoughts: This thing is overall a bit light on firepower for a super heavy, but that comes with the territory of sticking with extremely low price weapons. That said, it's a solid mech design and easy to work on, so we can easily rework it to support whatever you want it to mount.

details: Constructed by Earthwerks Incorporated to appeal to the Periphery market, the 2A model of the Thunderstone is built to use very little expensive or sophisticated construction materials, it uses all standard double heat sinks, a standard 270 fusion engine and standard armor, the only expensive construction material being the relatively inexpensive Endo-Composite structure and the CASE II systems protecting the buyer's investment. Marketed as being cheap to maintain and upgrade, Periphery states were quick to buy models of the mech to counter the proliferation of super heavy mechs across the Inner Sphere. This version mounts 4 Plasma Rifles and twin MRM-30 launchers with two Small Pulse Lasers acting as defense against infantry attack.
yangsThoughts: Very specialized for heat warfare, this thing can quickly shut down most opponents, so it's great for salvage and disabling missions but it's not great at direct damage.

details: After the initial success of the comparatively inexpensive 1A and 2A models, Earthwerks Incorporated introduced the 3C model to appeal to buyers who wanted performance and power while still being able to reuse the same mech bay facilities as the earlier models; the 3C model of the Thunderstone is built using all Clan-Spec construction materials, and XL engine and Endo-Steel chassis, as well as Clan-spec double heat sinks, CASE II systems still protect the buyer's investment. While not as prolific as the cheaper models, the ability to share bay space and maintenance crews between the various models has earned a place among the Periphery nations with deeper pockets.  For weaponry, it mounts a similar loadout in concept to the 1A, effective at all ranges, but it accomplishes this by using all Clan-spec weaponry, which, due to the lack of minimum range on a lot of them, allows it to operate at all ranges by just specing for the longest one. It thus mounts twin ER PPCs, twin Large Pulse Lasers, a single ER Medium Laser in the head, twin LRM 20 racks, a single HAG 30 and a quartet of AP Gauss Rifles for anti infantry and battle armor purposes. All sections are protected by Clan-spec CASE II to protect the investment of the buyers.
yangsThoughts: I like this design a lot, it has a lot of spread out firepower, great range, good indirect and direct fire capability and plenty of armor, the only thing it lacks is speed, but for a super heavy that's par for the course.

details: Originally developed by the first Star League, the Tolson was designed to serve as the ultimate command and control and electronic warfare platform of its era. The original designer, Dr. Transient, elected to give it a sleek appearance to help it better resist enemy sensors and equipped it with the best technology the Star League could muster. The Tolson is built on a 300-rated XL Engine and clad in 10 tons of Ferro-Fibrous armor. For weaponry it carries two Large Lasers and a LRM-10 but the real prize of the mech is the in-built Command Console as well as the Beagle Active Probe, Guardian ECM, Narc Launcher, and TAG system, all of which give it unparalleled battlefield control.
yangsThoughts: Great at getting command gear to the front line, but not really good for much else in terms of actually fighting.

details: Originally developed by the first Star League, the Tolson was designed to serve as the ultimate command and control and electronic warfare platform of its era. The Tolson TOL-5Nb is the so-called 'Royal' version of the design. Although the Tolson in general was quite high-tech by Star League standards, the -5Nb uses more advanced weaponry to up the ante in combat. Built on the same 300-rated XL engine with DHS and Ferro-Fibrous armor, the 'Royal' Tolson uses a LB-10X Autocannon and 4 Medium Pulse Lasers for firepower. It carries the same Command Console and Guardian ECM of its progenitor but drops the Narc, and TAG to instead carry a MASC system for extra speed.
yangsThoughts: I dunno about this, getting this thing to the battle faster isn't really that helpful since it's not great when it gets shot but the improved weaponry is at least better in a fight.

details: The Hyperion was developed in response to the Omega's debut by the Word of Blake during the Jihad. Using Clan tech to make the structure and engine allowed for a massive 72.5 tons of available pod space for the Omnimech. Propelled by a 375 XL engine and built on an Endo-Steel framework the designers did have to cut some slight corners on the actual armor to allow for such extensive firepower, the mech is protected by 18.5 tons of ferro fibrous armor, which is one and a half tons below the maximum for the frame.  The PRIME configuration mounts twin Gauss Rifles, twin ER PPCs, twin Improved Heavy Large Lasers, twin ER Medium Lasers and a seemingly superfluous ER Small Laser in the head.
yangsThoughts: This thing is efficient, extremely heavily armed and surprisingly fast for how big it is, it's a marvel of engineering. You might want to consider replacing those small lasers with some more armor or heatsinks though, something this big will attract a lot of fire.

details: Rumored to exist and only recently proven to exist, the Yazata is the second Word of Blake superheavy design and seems to follow in the tracks of the Celestial OmniMechs, serving as a sort of 'master' for that family of designs. Powered by a 375-rated extra light fusion engine, the Yazata has tremendous free tonnage to use for firepower. The Yazata puts even assaults to shame with its weapon load, using a Silver Bullet Gauss Rifle and Heavy PPC for direct firepower while twin Thunderbolt-15s provide indirect support.
yangsThoughts: A Word of Blake superheavy that isn't the Omega, interesting. The stock weapons loadout is pretty good, but a bit weird, but at least we can easily reconfigure it for other usages.

details: Rumored to exist and only recently proven to exist, the Yazata is the second Word of Blake superheavy design and seems to follow in the tracks of the Celestial OmniMechs, serving as a sort of 'master' for that family of designs. Powered by a 375-rated extra light fusion engine, the Yazata has tremendous free tonnage to use for firepower. The Yazata puts even assaults to shame with its weapon load, using twin Silver bullet Gauss Rifles and a quartet of snub nosed PPCs.  Angel ECM makes it hard to target the unit.
yangsThoughts: A Word of Blake superheavy that isn't the Omega, interesting. Even more gausses and ppcs for this one, no indirect fire.

details: The Assassin II is the result of a Federated Suns specification calling for a 'quick strike harasser unit'. The Maltex Corporation proposed a major redesign of their older Assassin chassis, the resulting BattleMech exceeded all expectations and has started to see use as a counter to the stealth technology employed by the Capellan Confederation. This Omnimech is designed with integrated Triple Strength Myomer to allow it to carry a self contained handheld Plasma Rifle, is equipped with retractable blades like Word of Blake units and is fitted with a Null Signature System and Chameleon Light Polarization Shield for stealth. For pod mounted equipment, the base layout mounts 6 jumpjets, two ER Medium Lasers and twin MML-5s, along with two tons of ammo.
