yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting mech design, I haven't seen handheld weapons in production mechs, but I guess there's a first time for everything, unfortunately this one is not compatible with other units as it has a non-standard control connection instead of the expected trigger mount. The pod mounted mmls and lasers are a bit underpowered for my tastes, but they do the job.

details: The Assassin II is the result of a Federated Suns specification calling for a 'quick strike harasser unit'. The Maltex Corporation proposed a major redesign of their older Assassin chassis, the resulting BattleMech exceeded all expectations and has started to see use as a counter to the stealth technology employed by the Capellan Confederation. This Omnimech is designed with integrated Triple Strength Myomer to allow it to carry a self contained handheld Plasma Rifle, is equipped with retractable blades like Word of Blake units and is fitted with a Null Signature System and Chameleon Light Polarization Shield for stealth. For pod mounted equipment, the OA layout mounts 6 jumpjets, two ER Medium Lasers one RAC2, along with one ton of ammo.
yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting mech design, I haven't seen handheld weapons in production mechs, but I guess there's a first time for everything, unfortunately this one is not compatible with other units as it has a non-standard control connection instead of the expected trigger mount. The pod mounted RAC2 is better at direct firepower than the missiles, so it's better for how this mech likes to operate in stealth.

details: The Assassin II is the result of a Federated Suns specification calling for a 'quick strike harasser unit'. The Maltex Corporation proposed a major redesign of their older Assassin chassis, the resulting BattleMech exceeded all expectations and has started to see use as a counter to the stealth technology employed by the Capellan Confederation. This Omnimech is designed with integrated Triple Strength Myomer to allow it to carry a self contained handheld Plasma Rifle, is equipped with retractable blades like Word of Blake units and is fitted with a Null Signature System and Chameleon Light Polarization Shield for stealth. For pod mounted equipment, the OB layout mounts 6 jumpjets, 3 Medium X-Pulse Lasers and an MRM-10, along with one ton of ammo.
yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting mech design, I haven't seen handheld weapons in production mechs, but I guess there's a first time for everything, unfortunately this one is not compatible with other units as it has a non-standard control connection instead of the expected trigger mount. An all medium range loadout, it's got efficient damage output, but it might work better with all energy weapons.

details: The Assassin II is the result of a Federated Suns specification calling for a 'quick strike harasser unit'. The Maltex Corporation proposed a major redesign of their older Assassin chassis, the resulting BattleMech exceeded all expectations and has started to see use as a counter to the stealth technology employed by the Capellan Confederation. This Omnimech is designed with integrated Triple Strength Myomer to allow it to carry a self contained handheld Plasma Rifle, is equipped with retractable blades like Word of Blake units and is fitted with a Null Signature System and Chameleon Light Polarization Shield for stealth. For pod mounted equipment, the OC layout mounts 6 jumpjets, one ER Medium Laser, one Medium X-Pulse Laser and twin MRM-10s, along with one ton of ammo.
yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting mech design, I haven't seen handheld weapons in production mechs, but I guess there's a first time for everything, unfortunately this one is not compatible with other units as it has a non-standard control connection instead of the expected trigger mount. Twin MRMs are quite a bit of firepower for a small mech like this, but you'll have to watch that ammo supply.

details: The Assassin II is the result of a Federated Suns specification calling for a 'quick strike harasser unit'. The Maltex Corporation proposed a major redesign of their older Assassin chassis, the resulting BattleMech exceeded all expectations and has started to see use as a counter to the stealth technology employed by the Capellan Confederation. This Omnimech is designed with integrated Triple Strength Myomer to allow it to carry a self contained handheld Plasma Rifle, is equipped with retractable blades like Word of Blake units and is fitted with a Null Signature System and Chameleon Light Polarization Shield for stealth. For pod mounted equipment, the OD layout mounts 6 jumpjets, one ER Medium Laser, a Snub-Nose PPC and an SRM-4, along with half a ton of ammo and an AMS to protect against enemy missiles.
yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting mech design, I haven't seen handheld weapons in production mechs, but I guess there's a first time for everything, unfortunately this one is not compatible with other units as it has a non-standard control connection instead of the expected trigger mount. This is a very close range layout, probably too close ranged for how fragile this thing is.

details: With a noticeable lack of OmniMechs designed in the Federated Suns, Field Marshal Duke George Hasek went over the heads of the Department of the Quartermaster and commissioned Kallon Industries to design an assault OmniMech and backed the project with his own considerable financial resources. The resulting 'Mech is a force to be reckoned with on the modern battlefield. The Templar is built on a lightweight Kallon Type XIX Endo Steel chassis and is powered by a KalTek 340 XL engine that gives the 'Mech a top speed of 64.8 km/h without sacrificing durability. A workhorse configuration, the Templar B can engage an enemy at either short or long ranges. For long range combat, the Templar B carries an ER PPC. For intermediate ranges, the 'Mech has three Rotary Autocannon/2s and also has two ER medium lasers for close combat. To handle the 'Mech's heat load, the Templar B has four additional double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Lots of autocannon fire makes this thing a monster against anything it can hit, but it can quickly run out of ammo if you don't keep an eye on it.

details: With a noticeable lack of OmniMechs designed in the Federated Suns, Field Marshal Duke George Hasek went over the heads of the Department of the Quartermaster and commissioned Kallon Industries to design an assault OmniMech and backed the project with his own considerable financial resources. The resulting 'Mech is a force to be reckoned with on the modern battlefield. The Templar is built on a lightweight Kallon Type XIX Endo Steel chassis and is powered by a KalTek 340 XL engine that gives the 'Mech a top speed of 64.8 km/h without sacrificing durability. A close combat configuration, the C configuration of the Templar carries a devastating LB 20-X Autocannon as its primary weapon, which can fire both solid rounds and shotgun-like cluster rounds. Also for close range, the Templar C has five Medium Pulse Lasers and an ER medium laser, which can be used to devastating effect at short range. For long-range defense, the Templar carries a single ER PPC. To add mobility to the 'Mech, it has four pod-mounted jump jets that let the Templar C jump up to one hundred and twenty meters. An additional heat sink is added to help handle the 'Mech's heat.
yangsThoughts: Lots of energy weapons with an LBX as the big punch, though with this many lasers you're gonna do more damage with the energy weapons than you will with the autocannon.

details: The 10S is armed with a mammoth Heavy Gauss Rifle, split between the right and center torsos, supported by a standard Gauss Rifle in the right arm, SRM-4 in the left torso and twin ER medium lasers in the left arm. Carrying the usual Victor weapons loadout in addition to the Heavy Gauss Rifle is made possible through the use of an Endo-Steel frame and fragile 320-rated Extralight engine. Lacking the jump jets for which most Victors are renowned, the -10S mounts an anti-missile system and is clad in thirteen tons of standard plating. The 10S carries three tons of Heavy Gauss reloads, two of standard Gauss rounds and a single ton of SRM and AMS ammo each.
yangsThoughts: This is probably one of the highest firepower version of this mech I've seen, it may be a bit fragile on the inside, but a Heavy Gauss plus a Gauss Rifle is such a big hitting combo that it usually won't take enough damage to need to worry.

details: After the chaos of the Jihad and the Wars of Reaving, Grumium Creations saw an opportunity to revitalize the venerable Cyclops design with a ground up redesign that would make use of Clan-spec tech throughout, while still retaining the command equipment the Cyclops was known for. This redesign, dubbed the Cyclops II, was increased to 95 tons, used a Clan-spec endosteel chassis and a Clan-spec 380-XL engine and XL gyro to maintain the same speed profile of the original Cyclops, and the head was still fitted with the originals highly effective Tacticon computer. Each general variant of this 'Mech was equipped with some type of C3 system, with the B sub-variant (this one) having a C3i Computer for use with small lance level combat forces. The 1 general variant is built primarily for long range ballistic fire support mounting twin Clan-spec Gauss rifles in the arms, and an additional Gauss type weapon in the torso depending on which C3 system is installed. the 1-B sub-variant mounts a newly developed Hyper Gauss Rifle for maximum focused firepower.
yangsThoughts: Nice to see that the classic form of the Cyclops is getting a much needed rework to meet modern battlefield needs. That Hyper Gauss rifle is bad news for anyone on the receiving end of it, and the combination of C3i and Tacticon is powerful. I just wish it had a bit more ammo.

details: After the chaos of the Jihad and the Wars of Reaving, Grumium Creations saw an opportunity to revitalize the venerable Cyclops design with a ground up redesign that would make use of Clan-spec tech throughout, while still retaining the command equipment the Cyclops was known for. This redesign, dubbed the Cyclops II, was increased to 95 tons, used a Clan-spec endosteel chassis and a Clan-spec 380-XL engine and XL gyro to maintain the same speed profile of the original Cyclops, and the head was still fitted with the originals highly effective Tacticon computer. Each general variant of this 'Mech was equipped with some type of C3 system, with the B sub-variant (this one) having a C3i Computer for use with small lance level combat forces. The 2 general variant is built primarily for extensive energy weapon fire power, a trio of Clan-spec ER PPCs give it powerful long range fire support, while a massive array of 3 Medium Pulse Lasers and up to 6 ER Medium Lasers depending on which C3 system is installed give it enough close in firepower to liquify any light or medium mechs foolish enough to try and get in close. The heat generated by the all energy weapons loadout is managed by a massive 21 heat sinks and a radical heat sink system.
yangsThoughts: Well, no ammo issues with this version, but you're gonna have to really watch that heat output with all those energy weapons. It really can do a number on anything that gets close though, which will allow it to keep safe even in the middle of heavy combat.

details: After the chaos of the Jihad and the Wars of Reaving, Grumium Creations saw an opportunity to revitalize the venerable Cyclops design with a ground up redesign that would make use of Clan-spec tech throughout, while still retaining the command equipment the Cyclops was known for. This redesign, dubbed the Cyclops II, was increased to 95 tons, used a Clan-spec endosteel chassis and a Clan-spec 380-XL engine and XL gyro to maintain the same speed profile of the original Cyclops, and the head was still fitted with the originals highly effective Tacticon computer. Each general variant of this 'Mech was equipped with some type of C3 system, with the B sub-variant (this one) having a C3i Computer for use with small lance level combat forces. The 3 general variant is built for massive missile volleys, a trio of IATM 9s with 5 tons of ammo provide the primary closer in firepower, while the LRM 20 and up to 4 LRM 5 (depending on which C3 system is fitted) provide secondary fire support with another 5 tons of ammo.
yangsThoughts: Wow, this mech really goes in for the missile volleys, this thing can absolutely crush most targets under withering missile fire and still have enough ammo to keep going.

details: After the chaos of the Jihad and the Wars of Reaving, Grumium Creations saw an opportunity to revitalize the venerable Cyclops design with a ground up redesign that would make use of Clan-spec tech throughout, while still retaining the command equipment the Cyclops was known for. This redesign, dubbed the Cyclops II, was increased to 95 tons, used a Clan-spec endosteel chassis and a Clan-spec 380-XL engine and XL gyro to maintain the same speed profile of the original Cyclops, and the head was still fitted with the originals highly effective Tacticon computer. Each general variant of this 'Mech was equipped with some type of C3 system, with the B sub-variant (this one) having a C3i Computer for use with small lance level combat forces. The 4 general variant is resemble the classic layout of the traditional Cyclops, with a HAG30 taking the place of the heavy autocannon (and moving to the left arm to protect against explosions), a Clan-spec LRM-15 replacing the LRM-10 in the left torso, a set of up to 4 ER Medium Lasers and an ER PPC replacing the medium lasers, and an IATM 6 replacing the SRM.
yangsThoughts: Basically a massive upgrade of the classic Cyclops loadout while keeping the originals ability to operate at all range bands when necessary to support its allies, this loadout doesn't match the raw firepower of the 2-B, but is much better at avoiding heat issues.

details: An early post-Pentagon Civil War variant developed by both Clans Coyote and Star Adder, the 1G had an ER PPC in the right torso, and three Gauss rifles (one in each arm, and one in the left torso) to provide hard hitting firepower. Each Gauss rifle had two tons of ammunition. Ten double heat sinks were more than adequate to keep the machine cool.
yangsThoughts: I'd heard about these but didn't expect them to make their way to the Inner Sphere from clan space. They've got some serious ranged firepower and way more armor than the normal version, just don't expect it to get anywhere in a hurry.

details: The original Star League-in-Exile model created in earliest 2779 was equipped with a head-mounted ExoStar small laser, four Blackenburg LB 10-X autocannons, a pair of Starflash Plus small pulse lasers, and four Magna Mk. II medium lasers. It carried six tons of LB 10-X ammunition, and the torsos were protected with CASE. Ten double heat sinks kept heat manageable.
yangsThoughts: Another old clan design, this one turning the classic Annihilator 1A into a way stronger brawler with more armor.

details: The Annihilator is armed with an arsenal that is built to instill fear in an enemy as much as damage them. The 'Mech is armed with four Mydron Excel LB 10-X autocannons as its primary weapons. These can all fire either standard rounds or cluster rounds which act like 'Mech sized shotguns spreading damage out all over an enemy 'Mech. The 'Mech is also armed with four Magna 400P medium pulse lasers, which allow the Annihilator to decimate any target which is able to make it through the devastating hail of fire and into close range combat.
yangsThoughts: Very similar to the 1X but the single heat sinks mean it doesn't have anywhere near as much armor.

details: A configuration devised by Wolf's Dragoons in cooperation with Arc-Royal MechWorks, the ANH-5W carries four Gauss rifles and four medium pulse lasers. The 5W also uses advanced components such as an endo steel internal structure. Ten double heat sinks can provide adequate heat management.
yangsThoughts: This is basically the long range version of the standard Annihilator, and with that much focused firepower on the table, you can really see why they built it. Still very slow, but at least it has enough range to compensate.

details: The first of two variants seeking to take advantage of newly developed Clan technology, the Annihilator C was rebuilt using only Clan technology during the Golden Century and later replicated by the Wolf's Dragoons in the post-Clan Invasion era. An endo steel frame and three tons less armor than the ANH-1X allowed the Annihilator C to mount a larger engine to increased its maximum speed to 54 km/h. The 'Mech was armed with four Ultra autocannon/10s and four ER medium lasers. The Ultra autocannon/10s allowed the 'Mech to fire at twice the rate of a normal autocannon, allowing it to put as much fire out as if it was carrying eight regular autocannons.
yangsThoughts: Now this is a true Clan version of this thing, full loadout of Clan grade ballistics and actually faster to boot.

details: Apparently based on the ANH-1G, this variant only uses Clan technology. The 'Mech's top speed was 32.4 km/h and the armor protection was reduced, but four Gauss rifles backed up by an ER PPC in the center torso and a head-mounted ER small laser provided withering firepower for defensive engagements. Ten tons of ammunition kept the Gauss rifles fed through the longest engagements. To make room for all these weapons, the C2 used an endo steel internal structure. Integrated CASE protected the arms and torsos from Gauss rifle explosions.
yangsThoughts: A crazy amount of long range firepower on this one, that ER PPC is basically as good as a Gauss Rifle.

details: The 5E is more focused on missile and ballistic weapons, the arms are affixed with a pair of LRM-5s and LRM-10s while each torso carries an Ultra autocannon/20, making this 'Mech a deadly adversary at both short and long range. Lacking any energy weaponry, the 5E carries four tons of Ultra autocannon rounds and three tons of LRM missiles.
yangsThoughts: An interesting idea, going with the Ultras instead of the Heavy Gausses saves a lot of weight for missile launchers for use while this thing is lumbering into range, but if anything is fast enough to keep away you're gonna have a problem.

details: This early version of the Imp, introduced in 2793, carries a Defiance 1001 ER PPC and Defiance P5M Medium Pulse Laser in the right and left torso. Supporting these weapons is a pair of Medium Pulse Lasers mounted in the center torso and a ExoStar Small Laser in the head. The left arm mounts another Medium Pulse Laser, while the right arm carries an Holly LRM-20 slaved to an Artemis IV fire control system with 2 tons of ammo. For heat dissipation, it carries 19 Double Heat Sinks. The engine is a standard fusion model, and the missile ammunition is protected by CASE. This version was the original Imp design specified by Nicholas Kerensky and was intended for battlefield commanders.
yangsThoughts: Not sure how this old design wound up here, but I'm not gonna look a gift horse in the mouth too hard. It's well armed and well armored with a nice loadout of double heat sinks to ensure it doesn't overheat.

details: Similar to the Imp 1A variant, this model trades the Medium Pulse Lasers for medium lasers. It also trades the head-mounted Small Laser for a Medium Pulse Laser. It keeps the ER PPCs and LRM-20 and Artemis IV system. The biggest change is the addition of a Guardian ECM suite in the 'Mech's right torso.
yangsThoughts: Not a huge change from the regular Imp, but those pulse lasers really improve the accuracy to make picking your targets apart much easier.

details: Another early version based on the Imp 1B, this variant mounts a Gauss rifle in the right arm. The Imp 1C uses the head-mounted Small Laser of the Imp 1A in place of the Medium Pulse Laser. The Guardian ECM suite is still in the right torso. One heat sink is removed, giving the Imp 1C a total of seventeen double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Entirely focused on direct fire, but otherwise unchanged in concept from the other early versions of this design. This one is well suited to be turned into a brutal close combat specialist if you put some nice autocannons on it.

details: This upgrade of the Imp is built exclusively for Wolf's Dragoons using only Clan technology. The 'Mech is armed with two ER PPCs and an LRM-20 launcher with an Artemis IV Fire Control System for long range combat. For close ranges, the 'Mech carries five Medium Pulse Lasers. The Imp C is also armored with nineteen tons of armor.
yangsThoughts: Exactly as devastating as you would expect a machine mounting a full loadout of Clan-spec energy weapons and missiles to be. This is probably the highest damage output variant of the chassis and also the most well armored.

details: The 000b variant is an upgrade of the classic KGC-000 built exclusively for the Star League Defense Force Royal units. Introduced at the same time as the original model its upgrade to double heat sink technology allowed the removal of three heat sinks as well as the addition of an extra ton of ammunition for each autocannon and an Artemis IV FCS for the missile launcher.
yangsThoughts: The 000b variant is an upgrade of the classic KGC-000 built exclusively for the Star League Defense Force Royal units. Introduced at the same time as the original model its upgrade to double heat sink technology allowed the removal of three heat sinks as well as the addition of an extra ton of ammunition for each autocannon and an Artemis IV FCS for the missile launcher.

details: With three more heat sinks and nine ER medium lasers, the Kodiak 5 is a fearsome close range brawler. It is no slouch at range, able to bring two ER large lasers and a Gauss rifle to bear.
yangsThoughts: A nice versatile brawling platform that can snipe at range to prevent its enemies from just kiting it around.

details: Multiple variants emerged from factories with an 'MSK-7#' designation. Dating to the late 2400s, these were the first variants to utilize what would become standard components. The most common 7A variant replaces the Autocannon/5 with a colossal Autocannon/20, a second large laser is mounted in the torso, and the original PPC remains. The total heat sink count is brought up to 21 and the switch to standard armor allows for a significant boost to survivability.
yangsThoughts: A genuine antique again, but this one is using standard equipment, the techs and I can definitely work with this.

details: Though most nations got rid of their cloned Mackies in favor of their own 'Mechs, the Hegemony was proud of their achievement. Moreover, many Hegemony militia units made extensive use of the first BattleMech, resulting in a continuous production right up until the Amaris Coup. Many of the various militia upgrades coalesced into the -9H, helped by the fact that Skobel continued to produce small quantities of this design. It mounts two Donal PPCs and an Imperator Autocannon/20, backed up by the pair of Starflash medium lasers and an additional 5 heat sinks to try to deal with the added heat for a total of 20 heat sinks. Nineteen tons of armor and a CASE system for the 4 ton Autocannon/20 ammunition bin in the right torso gave it full protection, allowing the 9Hs battlefield longevity. The sensor systems were augmented with a Beagle Active Probe to make it an excellent anti-insurgency unit. Nonetheless, Aleksandr Kerensky could only locate around 100 of the 'Mechs to take with him on the Exodus of the SLDF due to heavy losses against Stefan Amaris who personally detested the ancient machines and their stubborn frontline defense among the militia in 2766. The 9H is only a rough standard for Hegemony militia units, with the chassis being very easy to customize.
yangsThoughts: A clone of something this famous is still an amazing antique to find in my mechbay, so I'm still happy with this find. It's using standard equipment too, so it can be made fully combat capable.

details: Weighing in at a full hundred tons, the Titan was designed around a Vlar 300 standard fusion engine that limits its top speed to fifty-four kilometers per hour. While slow even by assault 'Mech standards, the use of a standard engine provides exceptional survivability, as the 'Mech can continue operating even after catastrophic torso damage. Nineteen tons of standard armor give the Titan formidable protection across its frame. The original Titan never reached full-scale production, though rumors abound that the Federated Suns' New Avalon Institute of Science produced prototypes, some with upgraded technology. The 1A called for two PPCs, six medium lasers, four SRM-4s, and an SRM-6. It only had twenty-two heat sinks and a somewhat different armor configuration from the later production versions.
yangsThoughts: I didn't realize any of these had survived honestly, maybe it's a variant from the reintroduced version they came out with.

details: Weighing in at a full hundred tons, the Titan was designed around a Vlar 300 standard fusion engine that limits its top speed to fifty-four kilometers per hour. While slow even by assault 'Mech standards, the use of a standard engine provides exceptional survivability, as the 'Mech can continue operating even after catastrophic torso damage. Nineteen tons of standard armor give the Titan formidable protection across its frame. Based off the FedSuns TI-1A derived specs, the Titan was reintroduced into production by Kressly during the Dark Age era for the Republic of the Sphere. It replaced the SRM-6 with an SRM-4 and the twenty-two heat sinks were replaced by sixteen double heat sinks. It added CASE to the ammunition bays, added three Jump Jets, and upgraded the PPCs to extended range models.
yangsThoughts: A bit more maneuverability with the jumpjets, and it's got better heat management, but maybe downgrading those PPCs to improve heat handling would be better to allow constant fire better.

details: Weighing in at a full hundred tons, the Titan was designed around a Vlar 300 standard fusion engine that limits its top speed to fifty-four kilometers per hour. While slow even by assault 'Mech standards, the use of a standard engine provides exceptional survivability, as the 'Mech can continue operating even after catastrophic torso damage. Nineteen tons of standard armor give the Titan formidable protection across its frame. This Titan variant was introduced at the same time as the TI-1Aj. It uses the same SRM-4 and double heat sink count, but drops the jump jets to carry a third PPC, all three standard models to reduce the 'Mech's heat load.
yangsThoughts: More firepower instead of jump jets, I can get behind this. And they even did my favorite trick of replacing ER PPCs with regular ones for the better heat handling.

details: Intended for close combat, the Hauptmann A is armed with four ER medium lasers as its primary short-ranged weapons with a pair of SRM-6 launchers linked to an Artemis IV fire control system for increased missile accuracy. For increased mobility, the Hauptmann A has three jump jets that allow the 'Mech to jump up to ninety meters. Finally, to defend itself if it ends up in a long-range firefight, the A configuration mounts a Gauss rifle. To handle the massive heat load, it carries an additional six double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Most of it's firepower is close range, so it's best to keep it there, but it can snipe with the Gauss if things keep trying to run.

details: Configured for both long- and short-range engagements, the B configuration of the Hauptmann has a pair of ER PPCs which give it the capability to engage an enemy at long ranges. For close combat, the 'Mech has three medium pulse lasers and a devastatingly powerful Ultra autocannon/20. To handle the high heat generated from its weapons, the B configuration has five additional double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Decent at all ranges, but not specialized for anything, might want to change out something to get the weapons ranged to match better.

details: This Nightstar variant was introduced after the Marauder-9D and uses some of the same components. Each arm mounts a Light Gauss Rifle and a Clan-spec ER Large Laser. A Clan-grade ER PPC is mounted in the right torso. A head mount carries a Medium X-Pulse Laser. To deal with the heat, sixteen double heat sinks are present, but two Coolant Pods can provide emergency cooling.
yangsThoughts: A pretty good ranged bombardment build, but I just can't figure out why they didn't just put Clan-spec Gauss Rifles on the arms, they'd still fit and would provide a hell of a lot more bang for the same weight.

details: Another variant that seems to be based on combating the Inner Sphere, the Turkina C is designed for urban combat. An LB 20-X autocannon and twin large pulse lasers can open up holes in enemy 'Mechs, while the Streak SRM-6 and paired medium pulse lasers exploit them. Paired anti-personnel gauss, machine guns, and flamers discourage conventional forces from closing. An active probe finds most hidden units, ensuring the MechWarrior is rarely surprised; two extra heat sinks make a significant difference.
yangsThoughts: A nice close range build, but it still has at least some ranged weapons in there, try to avoid letting this get flanked as it doesn't really have the speed to run.

details: This variant uses two pairs of ATM 12s. Ten tons of ammunition allow it to make use of the flexibility of advanced tactical missile technology. A pair of ER medium lasers provide backup and two extra heat sinks keep the load manageable.
yangsThoughts: Super heavy fire support layout, great for long range support, though you might want to pull a couple bits of ammo off and put a couple more lasers on for more direct fire damage.

details: This powerful close range variant is equipped with six Streak SRM-6s, and a HAG 40.
yangsThoughts: Really going all in on the close in firepower with this one, but a HAG/40 is still decent at the long range if needed.

details: This ammunition-dependent version uses a pair of Ultra autocannon/10s and an LB 20-X AC for close-range duels, which its dearth of ammunition further reinforces. It mounts a lone ER large laser for long-range support.
yangsThoughts: A nice build for brawling but it really needs more ammo or more energy weapons for endurance.

details: The fifth configuration is similar to the primary, though it is more geared to long-range support. It uses a Gauss rifle, two ER large lasers, and three Ultra autocannon/2s. A pair of heavy medium lasers enable the 'Mech to remain useful after it has run out of ammo.
yangsThoughts: The UAC/2s allow it to do more controlled damage than just firing the Gauss all the time while having a similar range bracket, but it really does burn through the ammo incredibly fast.

details: This configuration makes use of ATMs in the form of four ATM 3s. A single ton of ammunition for each pair does not allow the weapons to make the most of their flexibility. A targeting computer guides paired ER PPCs and medium pulse lasers. Four jump jets enable the Blood Asp E to vault one hundred twenty meters into the air, while additional heat sinks keep heat levels within reasonable levels.
yangsThoughts: Not sure why they went with such small launchers and then didn't give them enough ammo to really be useful. The techs think they can squeeze more launchers on those arms to let you really lay down the hurt.

details: Developed during the Jihad, this configuration is designed for close-quarters combat. The Blood Asp has been given four Streak SRM-6 launchers with three tons of ammunition, and a pair of medium pulse lasers and AP Gauss rifles for short range combat. For medium and long range, it mounts two ER PPCs and a single plasma cannon with a ton ammo. To cope with the heat build up, the 'Mech has been given an additional five double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Dunno abut how useful that plasma cannon is gonna be really, sure it can overheat things great, but you really need more heat power to do that well.

details: The Federated Suns variant replaces the autocannons with twin rotary autocannon/5s. All the lasers were upgraded to extended range models, and the weight savings went into an extra pair of double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Very high damage output, but you're gonna have to deal with heavy heat generation from all that firepower.

details: This new variant was introduced during the onslaught of fighting of the Jihad in 3072[3]. The variant is fitted out as an all-range combatant with enhanced anti-infantry fighting. It has retained its Gauss Rifle and Streak SRM 6 while exchanging the LRMs for three ATM 6 launchers with four tons of ammunition. The medium pulse lasers have been exchanged for trio of ER medium lasers. For its anti-infantry role, it has six AP Gauss rifles fitted into its left arm, with a ton of ammunition for them.[1] An endo steel chassis has been added to the design, allowing for a slight increase in its armor protection.
yangsThoughts: The ATMs allow for better firepower than the old LRMs and those AP Gauss Rifles are pretty nice, but you do have to watch out for their wildly different range bracket from the main Gauss

details: An evolution from the early 3060s of the -2R variant that upgraded the autocannons to Rotary AC/2s. To make room for this, the missile launchers were changed into MRM 10 launchers. This variant was associated with House Davion.
yangsThoughts: Really can see the Davion love of autocannons here, massive raw firepower available here, but it does run out of ammo pretty fast.

details: An evolution from the early 3060s of the -2R variant that upgraded the autocannons to Rotary AC/2s. To make room for this, the missile launchers were changed into MRM 10 launchers. This variant was associated with House Davion.
yangsThoughts: Really can see the Davion love of autocannons here, massive raw firepower available here, but it does run out of ammo pretty fast.

details: The -2R variant of the Mauler uses relatively new weapons. The standard AC/2s are replaced with Ultra versions, doubling their potential damage. Paired ER medium lasers are fitted in arms formerly housing the ER large lasers. The missile launchers are reduced to smaller LRM-10s. A heavier standard fusion engine is utilized instead of the expensive XL.
yangsThoughts: More of the Davion love of autocannons, this thing has a ton of long range volley power, but you gotta keep on top of the ranges in case someone wants to come close and brawl.

details: An experimental subvariant suggested by Professor Wheeler from the Sun Zhang Academy and implemented on the flawed -1KX chassis, it swaps the Gyro for a Compact Gyro and adds half a ton of armor. Instead of Snub-Nose PPCs it features Large Variable Speed Pulse Lasers, and the autocannons are exchanged for a quartet of Thunderbolt 5 launchers.
yangsThoughts: Weird overall design, but it's got some good bombardment firepower in there, just gotta watch out for ammo explosions.

details: In order to humiliate Clan Blood Spirit, Clan Star Adder upgraded their second-line forces and used them to retaliate against that Clan's interference in their Absorption of Clan Burrock. Part of this upgrade was the Marauder IIC 2. Adder scientists retained the medium pulse laser, but exchanged the PPCs and small lasers for two Series 4D-2 heavy large lasers and a dorsal mounted Series XIV ER large laser. The result is a hard-hitting design that is most effective at close range. In order to help the 'Mech get to close range, it includes four Guld Mk. III jump jets that are capable of vaulting the 'Mech up to 120 meters in a single bound. Three extra heat sinks enable the firing of all the close-range weapons. The weight left over was used in the form of an additional ton of armor, bringing it to twelve-and-a-half tons, equivalent to fifteen tons of standard armor.
yangsThoughts: Long range and hard hitting, but a bit light on the armor for a mech this big.

details: The IIC 3 is another variant created by Clan Star Adder, but they did not hoard this one all for themselves. Similar to the original with an ER PPC in each arm and two ER small lasers in the center torso, it also includes a left-torso-mounted ATM 12. Three tons of ammunition allow flexibility in ammo selection and an additional double heat sink makes it perfectly heat-efficient.
yangsThoughts: Similar to the original classic Marauder really, but this time it's got a big missile launcher instead. Good if you want to blast over terrain.

details: Developed by Gorton, Kingsley & Thorpe Enterprises in 3070 to fill the Ghost Bear touman's appetite for the Marauder IIC after a shadow trade war with the Diamond Sharks cut off supply, this variant mounts two of the largest weapon now available to the Clans, the HAG 40, in its arms. Ten tons of ammunition supply these mammoth guns, while fifteen tons of armor protect the 'Mech. Even with an XL engine installed, the inclusion of the HAGs only allowed for three jump jets to be added, meaning that the IIC 4 can leap up to 90 meters. Even mounting only 10 double heat sinks, the IIC 4 still has no heat concerns.
yangsThoughts: Well, if you like Gauss weaponry, this variant is for you, but it really doesn't have room for anything else weight wise with how ammo hungry the weapon are. Maybe downgrading one of them to a 30 would help by having at least some secondary lasers.

details: The 11Eb is an upgrade of the Thug used by the SLDF Royal Brigades in 2760. It upgrades the PPCs to ER PPCs.
yangsThoughts: A very simple change, longer range, but worse heat management.

details: Take an extremely powerful long range 'Mech, extend its range and accuracy, and put it in the hands of the Inner Sphere's two most secretive organizations, and you have the 12E Thug. Built for both ComStar and the Word of Blake in 3067 the PPCs have been upgraded to ER PPCs and an C3i computer has been added. To make the change to a C3i computer, a double heat sink, one ton of ammunition, and the corresponding CASE were removed.
yangsThoughts: A nice upgrade, the C3i computer helps it fight better, and the armament is still decently effective, though it is a bit underarmed compared to other assault 'Mechs.

details: The 13K is a variant of the 12E (Introduced in 3068) that removes many of the traditional strengths of the Thug to increase its damage potential. Introduced in 3068 the missiles have been removed and the 'Mech is now powered by an XL engine. This, combined with the loss of a half-ton of armor and one of the heat sinks, reduces the much-vaunted survivability of the Thug to make room for three heavy PPCs. Though this gives the 13K great damage potential, it can quickly overwhelm its heat dissipation system. Two medium pulse lasers provide close range defense in case an enemy gets within the minimum range of the PPCs and an C3i CPU enables it to share targeting telemetry with other units equipped with the same.
yangsThoughts: Huge firepower upgrade compared to the older designs, but it overheats extremely fast.

details: This variant removes all of the weapons, six heat sinks, and a half ton of armor. In their place is a pair of arm-mounted Type 0 hyper-assault Gauss rifle 20s. A pair of Series 2D-2 heavy medium lasers are mounted on each side of the body, while a Streak SRM-4 is in the head.
yangsThoughts: Efficient long range firepower, but will run out of ammo very fast. Maybe change out one of the HAGs to mount a single bigger HAG with more ammo?

details: Another of the so-called Clanbuster refits developed by ComStar in the lead-up to the Battle of Tukayyid, the 10-KNT is a modified version of the 9-KNT with more close range energy weapons instead of a hatchet. Compared to the older 7-KNT, the PPC was upgraded to a Magna Sunspot ER PPC, the medium lasers switched to Aberdovey medium pulse lasers, an Aberdovey large pulse laser added to the center torso, and both the small laser and Beagle active probe were removed. In addition to these weapons, the 10-KNT adds another medium pulse laser and an ER Medium Laser to the left arm, and also adds one more double heat sink to allow more sustained firepower.
yangsThoughts: I can't believe you found one of these, Comstar has always kept a tight grip on these things, but I guess even they aren't infallible. Even more firepower than the fabled 9-KNT, but abysmal heat handling even with the double heat sinks.

details: This variant was introduced by Clan Steel Viper. It replaces the traditional weaponry with a pair of ER PPCs, a Gauss rifle, and two machine guns.
yangsThoughts: Very good long range firepower, just watch your heat and you can handle most things with this.

details: Introduced in the Jihad, this version of the Lao Hu turns the enemy's equipment against him. It carries a Rotary AC/5 in the right arm, while the left carries a plasma rifle. An advanced Targeting Computer ties into these weapons, making them more accurate. The LRM-10 has been enhanced with an Artemis IV fire control system.
yangsThoughts: Closer range firepower than the 3C but with a lot more punch, just keep in mind that it's very ammo dependent.

details: Introduced in the Jihad, this version of the Lao Hu turns the enemy's equipment against him. It carries a Rotary AC/5 in the right arm, while the left carries a plasma rifle. An advanced Targeting Computer ties into these weapons, making them more accurate. The LRM-10 has been enhanced with an Artemis IV fire control system.
yangsThoughts: Closer range firepower than the 3C but with a lot more punch, just keep in mind that it's very ammo dependent.

details: This first variant is a short-range powerhouse. It mounts an Ultra autocannon/20 to breach opponent's armor and a pair of Streak SRM-6s to exploit those holes. A large pulse laser and a trio of medium pulse lasers are highly accurate weapons that can help to minimize collateral damage in an urban environment. Even with four additional double heat sinks, this variant can be quickly overwhelmed by heat.
yangsThoughts: Great close range brawling power, but not much in the long range department, you may need to put some more ranged weapons on if you have a problem with getting outranged.

details: This variant uses massed pulse lasers, two large and six medium, in conjunction with an ATM 6 and an SRM-6, and carries six extra double heat sinks. The ATM only mounts two tons of ammunition, limiting its flexibility.
yangsThoughts: A perfect example of massed small weapons fire being stronger than bigger weapons, the huge set of pulse lasers here allows it to rip appart anything it can catch.

details: Like the original Orion IIC the B retains the right torso Gauss rifle and left torso SRM-4 rack, but swaps the LRM-20 rack for an SRM-6 launcher and mounts a pair of ER large lasers in each arm. Mounting seventeen double heat sinks to deal with such high-heat weapons, made possible by the use of a 300-rated XL engine, the Orion IIC carries four tons of SRM reloads and two of Gauss rounds.
yangsThoughts: I prefer the indirect fire capability of the regular IIC but the extra free tonnage is nice to get some better weaponry on for the techs.

details: A Jihad-era refit turned 3080s full production variant, the 6T shifts towards brawling. It replaces the 4S's MRM 20 and ER large laser with an MML-9 pack, a snub-nose PPC, and CASE II protecting the ammo-laden right arm.
yangsThoughts: An interesting change to be even more of a close in brawler, I would prefer a pulse laser instead of the snubbie personally.

details: A 3146 refit designed by the Republic of the Sphere for Julian Davion's personal honor guard, turned into a full production variant for the AFFS by StarCorps, the 7D replaces the 4S's weapons with an all-ClanTech arsenal. ExoStar Pinnacle lasers — dual ER medium lasers, a single medium pulse laser, and a centerpiece large pulse laser — are accompanied by a Type XX 'Great Bow' LRM-20 pack. The 4S's electronics suite is preserved, and CASE II is added to the ammo-laden right arm.
yangsThoughts: As usual, it's always a huge upgrade to get Clan spec weaponry on a 'Mech, so this is a massive improvement, the LRM has no minimum range so it works just as well as an MRM rack.

details:
The first heavy OmniMech developed by Clan Wolf, the Woodsman was one of the most heavily tested weapons ever produced by the Clan. Every last detail was considered and problems were eliminated before production began. This resulted in a chassis that would not only function well in the Clan's front line forces for nearly a century, but also inspire the Timber Wolf, Gargoyle, and Naga OmniMechs.
The most common alternate configuration in the Woodsman's long career mounted two ER PPCs, two medium pulse lasers, and a large pulse laser and ER small laser for backup. It keeps the same number of double heat sinks as the primary configuration, but relocates them across the torso.
yangsThoughts: This thing is an antique design from Clan Wolf, but I hear they are bringing it back into production. It's got a wide variety of hardpoints and is very suitable to almost any weapons loadout. This loadout here is very energy heavy, which works well with its relatively high engine weight and lower pod space than some other Omnimechs.

details:
The first heavy OmniMech developed by Clan Wolf, the Woodsman was one of the most heavily tested weapons ever produced by the Clan. Every last detail was considered and problems were eliminated before production began. This resulted in a chassis that would not only function well in the Clan's front line forces for nearly a century, but also inspire the Timber Wolf, Gargoyle, and Naga OmniMechs.
This configuration is a low-heat high damage option for pilots. A Gauss rifle is mounted in the right arm while the left arm has an ER large laser and ER medium laser. The right torso has an SRM-4 while the left torso carries an LRM-10. Both of these launchers have an Artemis IV FCS. For anti-infantry work, a small pulse laser is carried in the center torso.
yangsThoughts: This thing is an antique design from Clan Wolf, but I hear they are bringing it back into production. It's got a wide variety of hardpoints and is very suitable to almost any weapons loadout. This loadout is very heat efficient, but more ammo dependant than most of the Woodsman setups.

details: A Dark Ages refinement of the Prime, the T configuration mounts an ER PPC in each arm supported by a shoulder mounted ATM-6, a trio of ER medium lasers and twin anti-personnel Gauss rifles. The T is protected by an anti-missile system and has CASE II for the two tons of ATM missiles and single ton of AMS ammo. The remaining weight is devoted to double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Interesting reworking of the concept, the AP Gauss will help by being longer range than some other support weapons, but keep an eye on them cause they can explode.

details: The Hellbringer H utilizes new heavy laser technology for its short-range arsenal. The long-range weapons of the 'Mech resemble those of the A configuration: the Ultra AC/5 is retained, though now paired to an ER PPC and an LRM-15. For close range combat, the 'Mech has a powerful heavy large laser and an array of four heavy small lasers. The defensive equipment is the same as the A configuration, two machine guns, two AP Gauss Rifles and an active probe.
yangsThoughts: As always for these things, it's an eclectic loadout with weird range bracketing but it overall works out ok thanks to the short minimum ranges of most Clan weaponry.

details: The Hellbringer A, though apparently a long-range configuration, does have some unusual equipment. A set of two ER large lasers and an Ultra AC/5 serve as primary weapons, and are supported by an LRM-20 rack. The 'Mech also carries a Narc missile beacon to increase missile accuracy, intended for nearby fire-support units. At close ranges, the 'Mech can defend itself with an ER medium laser and also has two machine guns and an active probe.
yangsThoughts: This is extremely unusual for a Clan mech, they very rarely go for NARC beacon support, because they prize single combat. Well, we don't follow that sort of thinking around here, so this loadout is great for us.

details: The Argus is first and foremost a close combat 'Mech. For this reason, the Argus carries a Mydron Model RC rotary autocannon/5 which is capable of firing at up to six times the normal rate of a standard autocannon, making it highly destructive at close range. This is backed up by two Bright-Bloom ER medium lasers for close combat. The Argus has a single Mydron Minigun machine gun to defend itself against unarmored infantry attacks, and also has a Federated 10-shot LRM-10 launcher which provides the Argus with some long range striking capabilities. The rotary autocannon/5 and the ER medium lasers are linked to an advanced targeting computer which makes them extremely accurate.
yangsThoughts: I like this design's heavy brawling firepower, but it's very ammo dependant for a lot of it's firepower, keep an eye on those bins.

details: The Hellfire A is built on an endo steel and mounts an 240-rated XL engine, clad in standard armor but saving weight for a Gauss rifle and Streak SRM-4 in the right torso, paired Streak SRM-6s in the left torso, supported by a single heavy medium laser in each arm. Fitted with MASC like the standard Hellfire to make up for lackluster ground speed, the A mounts thirteen double heat sinks and carries three tons of Gauss reloads and four tons of Streak missiles.
yangsThoughts: Massive amounts of spare weight on this one let it mount massive weapons loadouts. Not fast for a heavy mech, but able to rival many lighter assault mechs in raw firepower.

details: The B mounts the 300 XL engine of the Hellfire 2, but swaps the ferro-fibrous armor for an endo steel chassis, using the weight saved to mount a heavy large laser and LRM-15 in the left torso, twin Streak SRM-6s in the right torso, supported by a heavy medium and ER medium laser in each arm. Carrying MASC to boost its mobility, the Hellfire B is fitted with eighteen double heat sinks and carries two tons of Streak and one ton of LRM missiles.
yangsThoughts: Much faster than the A but less heavily armed overall, it can easily overheat if you're not careful.

details: This variant uses a pair of ATM 12s that are powerful at all ranges. It also uses paired ER medium lasers, medium pulse lasers, and ER small lasers. An extra heat sink helps to keep heat levels under some control.
yangsThoughts: Pretty much the same as the Prime in concept, just with more ammo and heavier launchers, if you don't mind less ammo then you can swap out some of it for more lasers or something.

details: The Double D is built for long ranged combat on the left side and short range combat on the right. The right arm carries a trio of medium pulse lasers. A Streak SRM-6 and one ton of ammo are carried in the right torso. The left torso carries an Artemis IV enhanced LRM-20 with a meager one ton of ammunition. A pair of LB 2-X ACs and their shared ton of ammo are mounted in the left arm.
yangsThoughts: Kind of a deranged combo, the missmatched range brackets mean that it's not really good at anything, but with a little work, we can probably improve that layout for you.

details: The Mad Dog F has a pair of HAG 20s. Though they don't have the sheer power of the larger guns of the E variant, the four tons of ammunition provide substantially better staying power. To back the HAGs up, the F mounts four ER medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: A complete change of pace for this mech, really, no missiles at all and focused on twin HAGs.

details: From the same lineage of 'WalkerPods' as the Ostroc and Ostsol, the Ostwar was Ostmann Industries' first attempt at creating a BattleMech. One of the many ancient designs revived thanks to the Hegemony Memory Core, the new independent Principality of Regulus's desperate need for BattleMechs during the Jihad saw them convert the Harmony MetalWorks IndustrialMech factory to production of the primitive 2M, with Ronin Incorporated building a new assembly line for the modern 3M after that conflict concluded. The original 2M Ostwar was armed with a twenty-tube Doombud Long Range Missile launcher for its long-range attacks. In close range, the 'Mech was armed with two four-tubed Shannon SH-40 SRM-4 launchers in each of its paddle shaped forearms. It also mounted two Martell medium lasers mounted on the left torso. The 'Mech carried three tons of ammunition for the LRM rack, while carrying a ton of SRM ammunition in its left arm. This modernized rebuild of the original design with lighter standard components uses the saved weight to add a second LRM-20 and one additional ton of ammo.
yangsThoughts: This seems to be a mix of old parts from an original antique mixed with some new construction parts, which is good because it means it's not stuck with primitive armor or engine. It's still a bit weak and undergunned by modern standards but it's perfectly serviceable if slow.

details: From the same lineage of 'WalkerPods' as the Ostroc and Ostsol, the Ostwar was Ostmann Industries' first attempt at creating a BattleMech. One of the many ancient designs revived thanks to the Hegemony Memory Core, the new independent Principality of Regulus's desperate need for BattleMechs during the Jihad saw them convert the Harmony MetalWorks IndustrialMech factory to production of the primitive 2M, with Ronin Incorporated building a new assembly line for the modern 3M after that conflict concluded. The modern 3M version, built at Ronin Incorporated's facility on Wallis in 3075,[12] replaces all the original's components with advanced technology. Powered by a standard Hermes 260 fusion engine, which increases the 'Mech's top speed by 10 km/h, the chassis is protected by eleven tons of light ferro-fibrous armor and CASE mounted in both side torsos. Swapping the standard SRM racks for more advanced and ammo-efficient Shannon SH-47 Streak SRM-4 launchers and standard medium lasers for modern Diverse Optics ER medium lasers, with the Doombud LRM-20 launcher enhanced with Artemis IV FCS. The heat sinks are upgraded to double-strength models.
yangsThoughts: A newly made version of the classic mech, it's not as advanced in tech as the 2Mb but it's cheaper to maintain.

details: The 7M steps outside the Ostsol's design philosophy of independent operations. Introduced in 3065 the 'Mech is built around an Endo Steel chassis, and uses an extralight engine for power, though it only carries ten double heat sinks. The primary weapons are two Light Gauss Rifles and four ER medium lasers. Finally, CASE has been added to protect against a catastrophic failure of either of the Light Gauss Rifles.
yangsThoughts: Much longer range than the typical Ostsol design, and actually using ballistic weapons for better heat handling, though it does make it more dependant on supplies.

details: This Star League era variant uses an XL engine, twelve single heat sinks, and upgrades the LRM-10s to LRM-15s. Three tons of ammunition are in a CASE protected torso bin.
yangsThoughts: A minor upgrade but a nice increase in fire support ability with the bigger LRM racks.

details: The 8D Dervish utilizes some of the newest technologies available at the time of the variant's introduction in 3062. The 'Mech is built with an endo steel chassis and is powered by an extralight engine, and also carries two more tons of armor than the 6M model. The weapons carried by the 8D are two LRM-15 launchers with an Artemis IV fire control system, while the SRM-2 launchers and medium lasers were removed for four ER medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: A pretty decent upgrade, though I'd love to put some Clan-spec launchers on this thing and really push that firepower.

details: Devoid of the more experimental technology utilized by the Cephalus and Osteon to allow it to be built in higher volumes, the Septicemia saves weight with a 275 Type II XL extralight engine, 55T Battle Epsilon-5 Profile endo steel chassis, and nine tons of Compound SJ8CW ferro-fibrous armor. While the location of its weaponry in its torsos hampers its ability to carry battle armor, this was intentional to ensure hand actuators were retained for maximization of physical combat abilities. The Prime configuration of the Septicemia is a long-range combatant. Its largest weapon is a HAG 40, though it only has enough ammunition to fire twelve payloads. An ER large laser provides effective backup and gives the 'Mech staying power. Both guns are aided by a targeting computer.
yangsThoughts: Not a lot of weapon variety but a solid mix for heavy hits at long range.

details: Devoid of the more experimental technology utilized by the Cephalus and Osteon to allow it to be built in higher volumes, the Septicemia saves weight with a 275 Type II XL extralight engine, 55T Battle Epsilon-5 Profile endo steel chassis, and nine tons of Compound SJ8CW ferro-fibrous armor. While the location of its weaponry in its torsos hampers its ability to carry battle armor, this was intentional to ensure hand actuators were retained for maximization of physical combat abilities. Based on configuration C, this Septicemia is also intended for battle against conventional forces. Two plasma cannons, six AP Gauss rifles, and eight machine guns in two machine gun arrays provide devastating firepower against conventional infantry and vehicles. Two medium pulse lasers in each arm deal with more heavily armored foes, while still remaining effective against infantry. Finally, the Society's engineers equipped this model with a Nova CEWS.
yangsThoughts: A very interesting loadout with those big MG arrays and AP gausses, just watch out for getting outranged by enemies.

details: A more radical redesign, this variant actually drops the targeting computer and jump jets to mount three ER small lasers and four ER medium lasers. A pair of ATM 6s with four tons of ammunition provided it with a credible threat at all ranges. The high amounts of heat generated by this variant led designers to mount seventeen double heat sinks on the chassis.
yangsThoughts: A lot of firepower here, but also a lot of heat output, watch out for losing any heatsinks cause you will really easily wind up overheating.

details: Another Crab variant used by the Star League, the 27sl uses an XL engine with fourteen double heat sinks. The large lasers and medium laser are traded out for two large pulse lasers and a medium pulse laser, and the small laser replaced by a medium laser. To improve mobility, five jump jets were added for a 150 meter jumping range.
yangsThoughts: Big improvement over the standard and even the 27b variant in terms of firepower, and that jump ability lets it get to places the regular crabs can't.

details: The CRB-30 is a complete rework of the CRB-27 Crab introduced in 3061. To start, the standard engine has been replaced with an extralight model. The CRB-30 puts this free weight to good use by replacing the twin large lasers with a pair of ER PPCs and upgrading the medium laser and small laser to an ER medium laser and an ER small laser. The standard single heat sinks have all been replaced with double heat sinks, though two have been removed, so the PPCs must use volley fire. The last part of this upgrade is the addition of a Guardian ECM suite and an improved C3 computer.
yangsThoughts: A lot more firepower than older crabs, this is probably the best you can get on this chassis for firepower without using Clan-spec stuff.

details: Upgraded with Star League technology, the 7M variant is built with on an Endo Steel chassis and uses a 250 Magna XL engine, giving it the same performance but allowing the design to mount ten double heat sinks and five Rawlings 50 jump jets for a jumping distance of one hundred and fifty meters. It also allows for the Trebuchet's LRM-15s' ammo to be protected by CASE and for a Octagon Missile Magnet Narc Missile Beacon with two tons of ammo, improving not only the Trebuchet's accuracy but allowing it to share targeting information with other similarly equipped 'Mechs.
yangsThoughts: Basically everything about this version is an upgrade from the standard, jumpjets, more free tonnage, double heat sinks, the works. It even comes with a NARC beacon to allow all of our units to do extra missile damage as well.

details: A refit geared towards long-range fire-support, the -3P mounts an Ultra AC/2 in each arm which are fed by 3 tons of ammo, the ER small lasers remain, while the SRM is downgraded to a 4 tube launcher.
yangsThoughts: Pretty decent at sniping, you're gonna need to watch out for things getting close to you with those minimum ranges.

details: A smaller XL 250 engine and the removal of a jump jet and the SRM-6 allows the -6P to upgrade its ER small lasers to medium pulse lasers and add a medium laser to each arm alongside the PPCs. An extra half ton of armor is added and 3 extra double heat sinks are needed to handle the increased heat from the -6P's weapons.
yangsThoughts: All energy with this one, so it's got the typical energy build heat issues, but that firepower is very useful.

details: The Hatchetman 3X further indexes on the stock design's close combat role, adding more armor and replacing its AC10 for an array of SRMs, Lasers, and Anti-Personnel weapons. This is the ultimate melee brawler.
yangsThoughts: The Hatchetman 3X further indexes on the stock design's close combat role, adding more armor and replacing its AC10 for an array of SRMs, Lasers, and Anti-Personnel weapons. This is the ultimate melee brawler.

details: Also developed in 3068, the 9F variant is designed for close combat. The 9F sacrifices both of the LRM-10 launchers, one medium pulse laser, the Guardian ECM suite, and a half-ton of armor for two MRM-20 launchers, giving the 'Mech more punch at the cost of ranged combat capabilities and defenses.
yangsThoughts: A lot of close range firepower, but you'll have to be careful of getting outranged, also be careful of the ammo volatility.

details: This variant, introduced in 3076, is equipped with a pair of MML-7s and medium pulse lasers, supplemented with a light PPC. This variant loses a jump jet, restricting its jump range to 150 meters.
yangsThoughts: Much more versatile than the 9F, but it loses a lot in terms of raw firepower. You might wanna consider replacing the ppc for another pulse laser to raise the armor and jump ability a bit.

details: Developed by the Clans during the Golden Century, this rebuilt version of the Phoenix Hawk uses an endo steel chassis and XL engine. Nine tons of armor and CASE protect the 'Mech. Each arm carries a medium pulse laser while each side torso carries a UAC/2 with one ton of ammunition for each autocannon. The left torso carries a half ton of machine gun ammunition to feed the machine guns mounted in the head and center torso.
yangsThoughts: A powerful upgrade, like all Clan variants tend to be, it has much more free tonnage and a lot of useable hardpoints for weaponry, and it still retains the vectored thrust kits.

details: A more recent configuration, the Shadow Cat C marries a pair of ATM 6s to three ER medium lasers, and mounts enough ammunition to make full use of the flexibility of the advanced tactical missile system. A Narc missile beacon helps colleagues' missiles target an enemy unit.
yangsThoughts: It's pretty unusual for the Clans to go for NARC tech, they tend to prefer one on one duels, but when they do go for it, they do it well, this thing is fast enough to get a pod on almost anyone and then survive long enough to get back out again.

details: A model of advanced Gauss technology, this Jihad-era Shadow Cat configuration mounts a total of 6 AP Gauss rifles and a HAG/20. It uses a Beagle active probe to scout for enemy infantry forces.
yangsThoughts: Someone really likes their Gauss tech, this mech is covered with nothing but Gauss Rifles. It's got great heat handling and great range, but that heavy ammo dependance for the HAG means it has low endurance in combat.

details: The -3F Cicada is one of the most advanced versions of this 'Mech, with upgrades based on combat reports from the front lines of the Clan Invasion, and was introduced in 3052. The chassis has been constructed using endo steel and is protected with six and a half tons of ferro-fibrous armor. The -3F is powered by an extralight engine and carries a weapons loadout that appears to be a cross between the 2A and 3C models, with the primary weapon being an ER PPC, backed up by two medium lasers. What's more, the Cicada can now jump up to 240 meters thanks to the inclusion of eight jump jets.
yangsThoughts: This thing is fast and actually has enough free tonnage to put reasonable amounts of armor on, which solves the biggest problem of these things being fragile. I just wish the XL engine wasn't so expensive.

details: The 3M upgrade of this older BattleMech uses some of the newest technology available as of its introduction in 3049. It uses an extralight version of the same Pitban 320 engine, freeing up extra space to upgrade the small laser to a small pulse laser and adding an UAC/5 to the left torso. The new ballistic weapon is fed by one ton of ammunition stored in the right torso and protected by CASE in the event of an ammunition explosion. The 3/Star Slab armor has also been replaced with Durallex Light, although the amount of protection is the same as in the -2A.
yangsThoughts: A lot of firepower for a Cicada with that Ultra AC5 but the limited armor is going right back to the old Cicada problems.

details: The 3M upgrade of this older BattleMech uses some of the newest technology available as of its introduction in 3049. It uses an extralight version of the same Pitban 320 engine, freeing up extra space to upgrade the small laser to a small pulse laser and adding an UAC/5 to the left torso. The new ballistic weapon is fed by one ton of ammunition stored in the right torso and protected by CASE in the event of an ammunition explosion. The 3/Star Slab armor has also been replaced with Durallex Light, although the amount of protection is the same as in the -2A.
yangsThoughts: A lot of firepower for a Cicada with that Ultra AC5 but the limited armor is going right back to the old Cicada problems.

details: The 4W is based on the WLF-2 model and is intended as a midrange harasser. Introduced by Arc-Royal MechWorks in 3069 the 4W has an endo steel chassis and carries three light PPCs mounted in the torsos, as well as an ER medium laser and an ER small laser in the right arm.
yangsThoughts: A nice amount of firepower for a thing this size, and quite durable, just has issues with heat buildup.

details: Clan Hell's Horses Incubus 4 completely changes the weapon loadout with an eye to lower cost, but keeps the core purpose of the 'Mech intact. It mounts a heavy large laser, two medium pulse lasers, two micro pulse lasers, and two ER micro lasers. It can quickly build up heat in normal combat situations, but most of the Trials it is involved in necessitate disabling multiple weapons, so this is rarely a problem.
yangsThoughts: The Clan honor duels this thing is usually used in are definitely not similar to battlefield conditions, so this thing really needs more heat management to operate properly.

details: Clan Ice Hellion, always prolific users of fast light 'Mechs, copied the original redesign of the Incubus, but decided to create their own variant. This fifth version mounts an ATM-6 with two tons of ammunition, two ER medium lasers, and four light machine guns.
yangsThoughts: An interesting loadout, but I'm not sure if the ATM's power is worth taking over a more direct fire weapon.

details: This 3085 variant is armed with an MML-7 and ER medium laser, carries light ferro-fibrous armor, and uses double heat sinks. Improved jump jets maximize battlefield mobility, while a Full Head Ejection System protects Davion MechWarriors who pilot this variant.
yangsThoughts: More versatile range wise than the older models, but otherwise the same basic design you are already familiar with.

details: Developed by Clan Wolf after they took Stewart in 3138, this mixed tech variant is used by solahma troops. It uses an Inner Sphere XL engine, XL gyro, and improved jump jets which allows the Valkyrie C to move quickly and cover 240 meters at a time. Armament is a Clan built medium pulse laser and a Clan LRM-10 with two tons of ammunition. Clan CASE II protects the ammunition, and Clan ferro-fibrous armor protects the rest of the 'Mech.
yangsThoughts: A typical C type refit, this mech can both do more damage and has more ammo and no minimum range on the LRMs, I'd consider reducing the jump distance to put on even more firepower personally though.

details: Effectively a Totem 'Mech for Clan Mongoose, the Mongoose C refit was introduced early during the Clans Golden Century. Produced by the Cudahy Assembly Plant Delta on Circe, the Mongoose C was built around a Clan-grade Kell/F endo steel skeleton, 200-rated XL engine, and four and half tons of Compound H17 ferro-fibrous armor, freeing up weight to mount a Series 2b ER medium laser in each arm in support of a center-torso mounted Series 6b ER large laser and a head-mounted Series 1b ER small laser. Still carrying an active probe, the Mongoose C mounted two extra double heat sinks. Following their Trial of Absorption of Clan Mongoose, Clan Smoke Jaguar would consign every last Mongoose C chassis to the scrapheap and history.
yangsThoughts: I thought these had all been thrown out when Clan Mongoose was absorbed, but apparently some of these things have been dug back out put back in the field, it's pretty much identical in setup to the 66b but with massively better weapons from the Clans.

details: This version is more properly adapted to a scouting role by the addition of an active probe, and also uses double heat sinks to allow for an all-energy configuration. Eight ER micro lasers and two micro pulse lasers replace the machine guns, and are all tied into a targeting computer that improves the accuracy of all the weapons.
yangsThoughts: Pretty similar to the standard layout in terms of firepower, but no chance of ammo explosions and no running out of ammo.

details: This Royal variant of the Stalker was introduced in 2705. It carries a Guardian ECM Suite in the center torso, upgrades the large lasers to extended range versions, and adds Artemis IV fire control systems to the LRM-15s that replace the Jackson LRM-10 launchers. The SRM-6 racks are gone, and the standard heat sinks were replaced with seventeen double heat sinks. Surviving examples of these variants left with Aleksandr Kerensky during Operation Exodus.
yangsThoughts: All these left with kerensky, so some of the Clanners must have brought them back, it's a nice solid upgrade to the base design, like all Royal mechs and it is way less likely to overheat.

details: Outfitted with the relatively recently rediscovered Star League technology of circa 3050, the FedCom 5S can be characterized primarily by its tendency to overheat. The designers behind this variant took the simple expedient of upgrading its engine and firepower without taking heat buildup into consideration. The 'Mech has had its engine replaced with an XL engine and the large lasers have been removed and replaced with two Thunderbolt-12 Large Pulse Lasers. For protection against ammunition explosions, CASE has been added to both side torsos and an Deprus Swarmshot anti-missile system has been added for protection against incoming missiles.
yangsThoughts: And I thought the regular Stalker was prone to overheating, for some reason these guys upgraded the base engine to an XL without upgrading the heatsinks.

details: This variant is the stanardized variant based on the the personal 'Mech of J. Elliot Jamison, commander of Wolf's Dragoons' Zeta Battalion, which was built in 3054 as a Clan-tech version of the venerable STK-3F. The Large Lasers were replaced with Large Pulse Lasers, the medium lasers upgraded to ER medium lasers, and a pair of LRM-20s are used instead of the original's ten-tube launchers. Rounding out the weapons array, two ammo-efficient Streak SRM-6s are used. Eighteen Clan-spec double heat sinks are mounted to help control the heat load.
yangsThoughts: As you would expect, the Clan spec tech version of this venerable mech design that is covered in lasers and missiles (the two things that the Clan improved the most) is a veritable monster compared to the base model. Better heat management, massively improved firepower and just as well armored.

details: A simple refit of the 27T using ER PPCs and Double Heat Sinks to massively improve the range ability and cooling, and allow upgrading the SRMs to Streak SRM models instead.
yangsThoughts: A simple upgrade, but very effective. More range, better cooling and better missiles, no real downside at all other than price.

details: The 27U, also known as the Hatamoto-Hi, was almost identical to the 27T. The differences in this 3047 design were the removal of the two SRM-6 launchers and the addition of four medium pulse lasers and another heat sink to help with the additional heat burden.
yangsThoughts: Significantly more overall firepower with those pulse lasers, though it does run way hotter so keep an eye on the heat guage.

details: A planetary forces variant of the UrbanMech fitted for handling police actions on worlds with heavy pirate and bandit presence. Complete with a light bar, the K-9 is refitted for somewhat longer range combat. The standard variant's AC/10 is swapped for a UAC/5 and the left arm small laser is updated to a large laser, with two small lasers mounted in the center torso. While its ground speed remains unchanged, the K-9's heavier weapons loadout is made possible through the use of an endo steel chassis and mounting 10 double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Still painfully slow, still very fragile, but at least it has some extra firepower to bring to bear and better combat endurance with that laser.
A lot of missiles for a mech this small, it can hit really hard for its size, and is about the same at ammo supply as the base model.

details: A variant proposed during the Clan Invasion but ultimately never put into production because of high cost and the Clan Invasion, this SuburbanMech would have used a 120-rated engine to reach a running speed of 64 km/h and power four jump jets. 10 double heat sinks were to be installed, and the PPC would be swapped for an extended-range model but the rest of the weaponry matched the UM-R90 variant. Six and a half tons of armor would be carried.
yangsThoughts: I thought they never built any of these, but apparently I was wrong, because here it is in the bay. It's the same as the regular R90 in concept, but with more hardpoints and actually able to move at a vaguely reasonable speed.

details: The first homegrown BattleMech produced by the Draconis Combine was the Gladiator. The 55-ton 'Mech took seven years to design, as Coordinator Kozo Von Rohrs refused to open up the process to anybody but a select group at New Samarkand Armor Works, the predecessor of Luthien Armor Works, who were under constant surveillance by DEST. 2468 finally saw the GLD-1R Gladiator hit mass-production. Following the Battle of Nox in 2475, the dishonored 'Mech was retired from official production, with NSAW closing down the production lines by 2488. Merryweather Industries was the first private company to attempt BattleMech production without governmental support. Despite their location in the Terran Hegemony, they acquired the plans to the Gladiator and produced a new run in 2490. They were unable to raise the capital to finish their second run, the 4R, and nearly went bankrupt before changing over to IndustrialMechs. The GLD-4R production lines were closed down in 2495. During the Jihad, StarCorps Industries produced a small yearly run of a new variant, the 5R, which over decades became the foundation of a line of Gladiator variants produced by the Son Hoa branch. The 55-ton Gladiator is built on an MW240 chassis, carrying a Core Tek 275 fusion engine that gives it a top speed of 86 km/h, comparable to many other 'Mechs of the same weight. Nine and a half tons of standard armor provides a respectable amount of protection, while five jump jets gives it greater agility and enables the 'Mech to jump up to 150 meters at a time. A Lord's Light P1 PPC provides long range firepower. Two Argra 1L medium lasers are just as powerful up close and a Thunderstroke SRM-6 exploits any holes in an enemy's armor.
yangsThoughts: A classic old medium mech, still viable today though not particularly good or notable by itself. I'm surprised this survived the five centuries since they were last made though.

details: The first homegrown BattleMech produced by the Draconis Combine was the Gladiator. The 55-ton 'Mech took seven years to design, as Coordinator Kozo Von Rohrs refused to open up the process to anybody but a select group at New Samarkand Armor Works, the predecessor of Luthien Armor Works, who were under constant surveillance by DEST. 2468 finally saw the GLD-1R Gladiator hit mass-production. Following the Battle of Nox in 2475, the dishonored 'Mech was retired from official production, with NSAW closing down the production lines by 2488. Merryweather Industries was the first private company to attempt BattleMech production without governmental support. Despite their location in the Terran Hegemony, they acquired the plans to the Gladiator and produced a new run in 2490. They were unable to raise the capital to finish their second run, the 4R, and nearly went bankrupt before changing over to IndustrialMechs. The GLD-4R production lines were closed down in 2495. During the Jihad, StarCorps Industries produced a small yearly run of a new variant, the 5R, which over decades became the foundation of a line of Gladiator variants produced by the Son Hoa branch. The 55-ton Gladiator is built on an MW240 chassis, carrying a Core Tek 275 fusion engine that gives it a top speed of 86 km/h, comparable to many other 'Mechs of the same weight. Nine and a half tons of standard armor provides a respectable amount of protection, while five jump jets gives it greater agility and enables the 'Mech to jump up to 150 meters at a time. The first test platform for Merryweather, the 3R uses two LRM-10s backed by twin Argra 1L medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: I'd swear you are like a magnet for weird old mechs that noone has seen operational in centuries, Commander. Pretty much just a standard firesupport loadout, and the tech is mundane by current standards, but it's still a perfectly serviceable medium mech.

details: The first homegrown BattleMech produced by the Draconis Combine was the Gladiator. The 55-ton 'Mech took seven years to design, as Coordinator Kozo Von Rohrs refused to open up the process to anybody but a select group at New Samarkand Armor Works, the predecessor of Luthien Armor Works, who were under constant surveillance by DEST. 2468 finally saw the GLD-1R Gladiator hit mass-production. Following the Battle of Nox in 2475, the dishonored 'Mech was retired from official production, with NSAW closing down the production lines by 2488. Merryweather Industries was the first private company to attempt BattleMech production without governmental support. Despite their location in the Terran Hegemony, they acquired the plans to the Gladiator and produced a new run in 2490.
