They were unable to raise the capital to finish their second run, the 4R, and nearly went bankrupt before changing over to IndustrialMechs. The GLD-4R production lines were closed down in 2495. During the Jihad, StarCorps Industries produced a small yearly run of a new variant, the 5R, which over decades became the foundation of a line of Gladiator variants produced by the Son Hoa branch. The 5R is an upgraded variant by StarCorps Industries. It was fashioned after a customized Gladiator fielded by Major Alex Keller of the Devil's Brigade that utilized cutting-edge and experimental technology, though the production model designated 5R does not exactly copy Keller's customized 'Mech. It is armed with a snub-nose PPC, two ER medium lasers, and an MML-5 with Artemis IV FCS to improve accuracy. Altogether thirteen double heat sinks help to alleviate heat problems. This version features a Guardian ECM suite and a C3 slave unit, and has twelve tons of standard armor, which gives it maximum protection for a 'Mech of its size.
yangsThoughts: A modern rebuild of the classic design, a pretty standard medium mech with good armor and weaponry. Nothing groundbreaking but able to hit at all ranges.

details: The Jackdaw is a post-Jihad ground up redesign of the Raven. Built using a combination of Clan and Inner Sphere equipment, it serves primarily as powerful electronics warfare and lance coordination unit for light lances. Its most notable equipment is the Angel ECM Suite and Bloodhound Active Probe it mounts ensuring that it has the electronics power to overwhelm any opposition using less advanced systems. Built using a 245 XL engine and an EndoSteel chassis, it has enough free tonnage to mount a decent array of weaponry for a scout mech. The 2RK version mounts a Clan-spec ER PPC and an Improved ATM-9 launcher although it can only manage to carry one ton of ammunition for it.
yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting machine, that ECM suite is seriously powerful, and nothing can hide from that probe either. Not particularly well armed, but that's not really it's primary focus.

details: The Jackdaw is a post-Jihad ground up redesign of the Raven. Built using a combination of Clan and Inner Sphere equipment, it serves primarily as powerful electronics warfare and lance coordination unit for light lances. Its most notable equipment is the Angel ECM Suite and Bloodhound Active Probe it mounts ensuring that it has the electronics power to overwhelm any opposition using less advanced systems. Built using a 245 XL engine and an EndoSteel chassis, it has enough free tonnage to mount a decent array of weaponry for a scout mech. The 2EK version mounts 4 Clan-spec ER Medium Lasers and a trio of Clan-Spec LRM-5 launchers although it can only manage to carry one ton of ammunition for them. Four extra double heat sinks are needed to keep this variant stable.
yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting machine, that ECM suite is seriously powerful, and nothing can hide from that probe either. A bit more close range firepower at the cost of some of that long range bombardment power, good for rougher terrain where you can't get a good shot in at distance.'.

details: The Jackdaw is a post-Jihad ground up redesign of the Raven. Built using a combination of Clan and Inner Sphere equipment, it serves primarily as powerful electronics warfare and lance coordination unit for light lances. Its most notable equipment is the Angel ECM Suite and Bloodhound Active Probe it mounts ensuring that it has the electronics power to overwhelm any opposition using less advanced systems. Built using a 245 XL engine and an EndoSteel chassis, it has enough free tonnage to mount a decent array of weaponry for a scout mech. The 2BK version mounts a Rotary AC2 with one ton of ammo along with a trio of Clan-spec ER Medium Lasers.
yangsThoughts: Now this is an interesting machine, that ECM suite is seriously powerful, and nothing can hide from that probe either. Focused a bit more of direct fire sniping of smaller units, that Rotary AC is powerful armament for a mech this light.

details: The Alpha is a mysterious new super heavy chassis design. The fact that it is fully built by clan level tech is unmistakeable, however the name of the clan responsible for constructing it is unknown. Unknown design origin aside, the ability of the mech to actually function is clearly based on the Omega superheavy as designed by the Word of Blake. This mech, however, is built with a wide variety of weapons hardpoints in both arms and both torsos, to allow for more weapons customization than most mechs superheavy or otherwise. Designed for a more firesupport role, the ALP-2 is fitted with twin HAG 20's for sniping, a pair of ATM 9s and ATM 12s for more precise fire support and a massive array of 8 medium pulse lasers to neutralize anything foolish enough to get into close range. Its primary flaw is a lack of ammunition to sustain bombardment for long periods.
yangsThoughts: A brand new superheavy design, of Clan-spec origin and the name of the clan is unknown? The mysteries abound with this one. This version is good at long range fire, which is good cause you're gonna be waiting a while for it to get close enough for anything else.

details: The Alpha is a mysterious new super heavy chassis design. The fact that it is fully built by clan level tech is unmistakeable, however the name of the clan responsible for constructing it is unknown. Unknown design origin aside, the ability of the mech to actually function is clearly based on the Omega superheavy as designed by the Word of Blake. This mech, however, is built with a wide variety of weapons hardpoints in both arms and both torsos, to allow for more weapons customization than most mechs superheavy or otherwise. Focusing entirely on direct fire, the ALP-3 is fitted with twin RAC5s, a quartet of ER PPCs and a quartet of Streak SRM6's. Like other variants however, it still suffers from relatively limited ammunition supplies in the case of long battles.
yangsThoughts: A brand new superheavy design, of Clan-spec origin and the name of the clan is unknown? The mysteries abound with this one. A kind of strange layout vaguely resembling some sort of mutant Awesome, but the fact that it can mount a quartet of ER PPCs casually really drives home how absurd superheavies can be.

details: Clan Jade Falcon's brand new super heavy mech design, this mech is heavily armed but with a somewhat eclectic hardpoint mount setup for a superheavy, with no ballistic hardpoints in the torso. Designed with heavy combat in mind, the mech has plenty of free tonnage to use for heavy weaponry. The HBL-2 layout mounts a quartet of firelinked pulse laser arrays nicknamed Trasers and pairs of Ultra Autocannon 20s and 2s in the arms for both long and short range direct fire.
yangsThoughts: This thing is big, mean and heavy, Commander. It's got plenty of hardpoints and plenty of armor so it can handle whatever it has to. Comes with these weird linked pulse laser things, they somehow linked 3 lasers together with a variable speed pulse controller attached to the middle, good range and power but a bit heavy.

details: Clan Jade Falcon's brand new super heavy mech design, this mech is heavily armed but with a somewhat eclectic hardpoint mount setup for a superheavy, with no ballistic hardpoints in the torso. Designed with heavy combat in mind, the mech has plenty of free tonnage to use for heavy weaponry. The HBL-2 layout a pair of HAG 40s in the arms for massive damage, and matches them with twin Hyper Lasers sourced from the Republic Institute of Strategic Combat. The twin ER PPCs and SRM 6 launchers feel like afterthoughts compared to all the unusual weaponry.
yangsThoughts: This thing is big, mean and heavy, Commander. It's got plenty of hardpoints and plenty of armor so it can handle whatever it has to. Those Hyper Laser things are unusual, they can match Clan-spec large lasers in range while being like Heavy Lasers in damage, they just generate so much heat that you're lucky to be able to fire em.

details: Although the concept of a superheavy 'Mech had already been explored by the Terran Hegemony centuries before, and later by Amaris forces, with the Matar as example, and the Clans polished that concept launching the Stone Rhino, it wasn't until the Word of Blake overcame the problems associated with such a massive design that the Omega became viable. Featuring an Endo Steel skeleton larger and bulkier than any seen before, oversized actuators and a new myomer system that involved both larger fiber bundles than standard and the use of hybrid myomers, the Blakists were able to overcome a number of flaws discovered by those who had explored the superheavy 'Mech concept in the past. The resulting internal structure was far larger than would be viable for any conventional BattleMech, but in the case of the Omega, resulted in a working superheavy design that stood half again as tall as the 100-ton King Crab, from which the designers had evidently taken a number of cues. The X3 mounts 3 Gauss Rifles spread over the arms and left torso and pairs them with two LBX 10 autocannons in the right torso. The only non-ballistic weapons are a couple of ER Medium Lasers.
yangsThoughts: I can't believe those crazy Blakists managed to make Amaris's idea into an actual functional unit design at this size. It's got a lot of heavy firepower, but it's slow and easily outmaneuvered, so keep an eye on your back.

details: Although the concept of a superheavy 'Mech had already been explored by the Terran Hegemony centuries before, and later by Amaris forces, with the Matar as example, and the Clans polished that concept launching the Stone Rhino, it wasn't until the Word of Blake overcame the problems associated with such a massive design that the Omega became viable. Featuring an Endo Steel skeleton larger and bulkier than any seen before, oversized actuators and a new myomer system that involved both larger fiber bundles than standard and the use of hybrid myomers, the Blakists were able to overcome a number of flaws discovered by those who had explored the superheavy 'Mech concept in the past. The resulting internal structure was far larger than would be viable for any conventional BattleMech, but in the case of the Omega, resulted in a working superheavy design that stood half again as tall as the 100-ton King Crab, from which the designers had evidently taken a number of cues. The X6 is a reworked design to try to address some of the strange range brackets of the original build. It mounts a pair of Ultra AC20s alongside a pair of Ultra AC10s giving it heavy direct firepower. It also adds an angel ECM system to protect it from counter battery fire alongside the ubiquitous Blakist C3I system.
yangsThoughts: Nothing but autocannons here, which makes it relatively straightforward to use, but very ammunition heavy. Keep a close watch to make sure you don't run out.

details: The Gigant was created by House Steiner as an emergency response to House Marik's Longbow II superheavy 'Mech. Just as the Longbow II is an upsized Longbow, the Gigant is an upsized Crusader, with the designers taking advantage of Lyran resources to more than double the tonnage of the original chassis. The hasty process by which the Gigant was conceived meant that it suffered from numerous teething problems; most of these were eventually solved, but the design retains a dubious reputation. Regardless, as a superheavy it can lay down tremendous firepower with multiple missile launchers and energy weapons. The -08 model replaces the LRM-20 launchers (all 4 of them) with MRM-30s increasing overall damage output at the cost of range.
yangsThoughts: A simple weapon swap, but it definitely makes this mech a very nasty variant for anything that gets too close. Just watch out for things outranging you.

details: The Gigant was created by House Steiner as an emergency response to House Marik's Longbow II superheavy 'Mech. Just as the Longbow II is an upsized Longbow, the Gigant is an upsized Crusader, with the designers taking advantage of Lyran resources to more than double the tonnage of the original chassis. The hasty process by which the Gigant was conceived meant that it suffered from numerous teething problems; most of these were eventually solved, but the design retains a dubious reputation. Regardless, as a superheavy it can lay down tremendous firepower with multiple missile launchers and energy weapons. The -09 model completely reworks the weaponry, twin arm mounted Large Variable Speed Pulse Lasers, two arm mounted Ultra Autocannon 5s, a quartet of torso mounted MML5s and two torso mounted ER Medium Lasers.
yangsThoughts: An interesting change of loadout, I'm not sure I like how heavy the large variable pulses are, but the techs can probably put something better in their place.

details: Clan Diamond Shark followed up the Rampart, its first superheavy BattleMech, with the Terminus. Learning their lessons from the earlier design, the clan's designers gave the Terminus an all-energy-weapon loadout: this removes the risk of ammunition explosions, and is also better at dealing with light, agile opposition. The 'Mech's electronics remain top-notch, with the centerpiece being a Nova CEWS sourced from the clan's allies. The TMS-2 replaces the standard ER PPCs with the recently developed Refined Plasma PPCs, somewhat increasing the damage but reducing the overall range.
yangsThoughts: Better firepower overall at close range, but worse range, and harder to adjust damage output based on heat levels, so you may need to do less damage in some turns to cool off.

details: Clan Diamond Shark followed up the Rampart, its first superheavy BattleMech, with the Terminus. Learning their lessons from the earlier design, the clan's designers gave the Terminus an all-energy-weapon loadout: this removes the risk of ammunition explosions, and is also better at dealing with light, agile opposition. The 'Mech's electronics remain top-notch, with the centerpiece being a Nova CEWS sourced from the clan's allies. The TMS-3 bolsters the close in firepower, replacing one of the PPCs and all of the small lasers with 6 Streak SRM6s with range boosing launch rails.
yangsThoughts: A very binary loadout, with a hard range cutoff in the middle where enemies get too close and can then be shredded by the SRMS, just watch out for ammo explosions.

details: The Rampart is Clan Diamond Shark's first attempt to create a superheavy BattleMech. The alternate RPT-2 layout is designed to reduce the need for support by lighter allies, it replaces the HAGs and their ammo with a quartet of ER Large Pulse lasers and a quartet of ER Medium Lasers. Laser insulators are installed to reduce heat generation.
yangsThoughts: The smaller weapons allow for it to actually deal with things up close, but with all that weaponry on the arms, if you take a bad hit you'll be heavily impaired.

details: The Rampart is Clan Diamond Shark's first attempt to create a superheavy BattleMech. The alternate RPT-3 layout is designed for heavy fire support by mounting 4 RAC5s, barely capable of fitting enough ammo to run the guns, or enough heat management to allow firing them, the mech is nevertheless brutally efficient if it can bring the weapons to bear.
yangsThoughts: Well, it's direct and to the point I'll give it that, but running nothing but RAC5s is the sort of layout a madman would put together.

details: The Svarog, also known as the King Crab II, is named after the ancient Slavic god of fire. Unlike most other superheavy designs, it is intended to be a breakthrough 'Mech: it acts as the spearhead of an advance, engaging points of resistance and destroying them with massive firepower. The 3B variant mounts twin Hyper Gauss rifles sourced from the Draconis Combine instead of autocannons. The much heavier main weapons required the removal of all the flamers to support them. The Svarog has proved to be quite successful; even if an overconfident MechWarrior outruns their support, the 'Mech's sheer firepower and protection mean it can survive against heavy odds until help arrives.
yangsThoughts: Kind of a Clanbuster style rework of the 3A, it mounts two big Gauss Rifles that have enough firepower to obliterate light mechs in one hit.

details: The Svarog, also known as the King Crab II, is named after the ancient Slavic god of fire. Unlike most other superheavy designs, it is intended to be a breakthrough 'Mech: it acts as the spearhead of an advance, engaging points of resistance and destroying them with massive firepower. The 4A deviates from the formula more drastically by mounting twin MRM 40s and a pair of RAC2s, the flamers having been replaced with lighter ER Small Lasers for weight savings. The Svarog has proved to be quite successful; even if an overconfident MechWarrior outruns their support, the 'Mech's sheer firepower and protection mean it can survive against heavy odds until help arrives.
yangsThoughts: Well, it's direct and to the point I'll give it that, but running nothing but RAC5s is the sort of layout a madman would put together.

details: A rebuild of the venerable LGB-7Q Longbow that increases its weight by more than 40 per cent, the Longbow II is a veritable walking arsenal. Unlike the 9, the 10 replaces the launch tubes with a huge array of SRMs along with a set of accelerating launch rails for improved range and some Large X-Pulse Lasers to provide direct fire support. The Longbow II also mounts enough heat dissipation capacity to fire all its weapons without significantly heating up. This superheavy 'Mech was first produced by the Free Worlds League, but the design has since spread to most parts of the Inner Sphere, whether by capture or trade.
yangsThoughts: Those launch rails are handy, increasing the range of SRMs can make them very useful, but they're still pretty short range.

details: The Matar, commonly referred to as "Amaris' Folly", was an early attempt to create a BattleMech able to withstand an entire company. Carrying devastating firepower and counters to all perceived threats, the Matar's leg actuators proved incapable of moving the massive machine and it never went into production. The Matar II is a 31st Century update on the original Matar design that uses advances in technology to correct its faults, adding 10 tons in the process. The CS variant is armed with a closer range loadout of UAC10s and ER Large Lasers.
yangsThoughts: A close range rebuild of this thing can be useful, but it's pretty slow, so you'll need to find some way to push the enemy close to you if you want to use this build.

details: With an intended role of static defense, the Matar was built around long range firepower, toting a pair of Gauss Rifles in the side torsos supplemented by a dorsal-mounted ER Large Laser. To deal with faster units the Matar was ill-suited to fight, a pair of Large Pulse Lasers were equipped, one in each arm. A simple pivot of the torso would enable the pilot to even hit targets directly behind him with one of the arm lasers, both capable of dealing crippling hits to lighter assets. When coupled with the rear-mounted Medium Pulse Laser, there was no true safe angle from which to attack a Matar. Reportedly at Stefan Amaris' personal insistence, the Matar carries a pair of Flamers to roast opposing infantry alive. To guard against advanced munitions that could threaten the Matar from long range, a Guardian ECM was installed in one of the legs.
yangsThoughts: I don't know how you do it, Commander, a WORKING example of a 300 year old boondoggle and it's in near pristine condition and somehow you are the person who winds up fighting it. It's still just as potent a ranged fighter as it was in Amaris' time, so if you want to use it, it'll do a good job at anything other than getting places in a hurry.

details: The newly reborn Matar, the SAM-CS, carries a conventional yet effective loadout of twin UAC/10s and triple ER Large Lasers, all built on an XL 330-rated fusion engine for power. CASE II protects the four tons of ammunition while advanced heat control equipment keeps it all well cooled.
yangsThoughts: I can't believe that there's a new variant of this ancient design, but I guess even antique superheavy designs are still working superheavy designs.

details: Determined not to be left behind by the march of progress, the Armed Forces of the Federated Suns began work in the 3050s on a Superheavy BattleMech program. Despite a decade of fitful starts and stops, they eventually debuted a design in the 3060s, the Culverin. Named for an ancient Terran artillery weapon, the Culverin carries two of the AFFS's favored weapons, Rotary Autocannon/5s, into battle. To back up the RACs the Culverin has an LRM-20 and LRM-10 and, in the only nod to ammo-free firepower, an ER Large Laser. 7 total tons of CASE II protected ammunition ensure the guns keep running in any combat situation. Most shockingly though, the Culverin is powered by a 405-rated XL engine, breaking the normal confines of what BattleMech engines can consist of.
yangsThoughts: Similar to the Dilettante in its love of autocannons, but faster with that massive engine, I didn't even know they made them that big.

details: Determined not to be left behind by the march of progress, the Armed Forces of the Federated Suns began work in the 3050s on a Superheavy BattleMech program. Despite a decade of fitful starts and stops, they eventually debuted a design in the 3060s, the Culverin. Named for an ancient Terran artillery weapon, the Culverin carries four of the AFFS's favored weapons, LB-10X Autocannons, into combat. To back up the barrage of autocannons the Culverin has a mere MML-7 and ER Small Laser, necessitating it remain close to supply lines lest its generous 8 tons of ammo run out. Most shockingly though, the Culverin is powered by a 405-rated XL engine, breaking the normal confines of what BattleMech engines can consist of.
yangsThoughts: Very close range for something that is pretty slow, though it's faster than most superheavies. If you can get things in range, then they die pretty fast.

details: Determined not to be left behind by the march of progress, the Armed Forces of the Federated Suns began work in the 3050s on a Superheavy BattleMech program. Despite a decade of fitful starts and stops, they eventually debuted a design in the 3060s, the Culverin. Named for an ancient Terran artillery weapon, the Culverin carries a trio of silver bullet gauss rifles with a pair of clan-spec LRM-20s. Most shockingly though, the Culverin is powered by a 405-rated XL engine, breaking the normal confines of what BattleMech engines can consist of.
yangsThoughts: Long range focused and no autocannons, which is strange for the Feddies, but I can't deny the designs effectiveness.

details: Developed as a bombardment mech, the Coehorn is fitted with a gargantuan number of MML tubes, 42 in total. With a 300 XL engine and a Clan-grade endosteel skeleton, it has enough ammunition to supply the launchers for quite a while. 3 ER Medium Lasers provide backup for times when enemies can't be dealt with by the missiles. 16 Double Heat Sinks are able to keep the heat somewhat stable, but it can still overheat if it overuses the launchers.
yangsThoughts: An interesting fire support mech, a huge number of missiles mean that it can really put the hurt down, but it's vulnerable to enemies mounting anti missile systems or ecm, so make sure you bring someone who can scout for it.

details: While originally developed as a bombardment mech, the Coehorn M7 is not fitted with any indirect fire weapons. Instead a pair of Large Variable Speed Pulse Lasers and a pair of Hyper Lasers sources from the Republic Institute of Strategic Combat is mounted to hammer anything that it can get into view. With a 300 XL engine and a Clan-grade endosteel skeleton, it has enough heat management equipment to stay stable at least long enough for it's enemies to be destroyed.
yangsThoughts: I have no idea why they decided to completely change the purpose of the mech, but it seems to work ok, just watch out for the heat usage.

details: Developed by Majesty Metals and Manufacturing on Canopus IV, the Fer-de-Lance is a homegrown Canopian Star League-era BattleMech design. The Fer-de-Lance is a dedicated light scout mech, boasting a high ground speed of 120 kph and reasonable armament for a scout of its era with a pair each Medium and Small Lasers. The Fer-de-Lance managed to survive the Succession Wars in decent numbers and remains a staple of the Magistracy Armed Forces.
yangsThoughts: A decently durable scout mech with moderate armament, it won't ever win any prizes for firepower, but it's decent for what it is.

details: Developed by Majesty Metals and Manufacturing on Canopus IV, the Fer-de-Lance is a homegrown Canopian Star League-era BattleMech design. The Fer-de-Lance is a dedicated light scout mech and boasts a ground speed of 120 kph. The FDL-2E is a Star League-era upgrade of the design, armed with two each Medium Lasers and SRM-4s while a Beagle Active Probe offers some extra electronic support. A 240-rated XL engine provides the free weight for the warload. The Fer-de-Lance managed to survive the Succession Wars in decent numbers and remains a staple of the Magistracy Armed Forces.
yangsThoughts: More free tonnage means more space for weaponry, and this mech is significantly more deadly by scout mech standards.

details: Developed by Majesty Metals and Manufacturing on Canopus IV, the Fer-de-Lance is a homegrown Canopian Star League-era BattleMech design. The Fer-de-Lance FDL-3U is a rare fire-support variant of the design. It reduces the ground speed to a mere 110 kph, courtesy of a 210-rated XL engine, and builds the mech with both Endo-Steel and Ferro-Fibrous construction materials. All this free tonnage is put towards an Ultra Autocannon/5 with two tons of ammunition, giving the Fer-de-Lance an unexpected punch for its size. A pair of Small Lasers provide backup in emergencies. The Fer-de-Lance managed to survive the Succession Wars in decent numbers and remains a staple of the Magistracy Armed Forces.
yangsThoughts: A bit slower, and I've always been a bit dubious of going for ballistic weapons on mechs this light, but it does work as fire support at least until it runs out of ammo.

details: The Fire Falcon was created in the early-3050s, apparently as a replacement for the Mist Lynx; it is faster, has more front-facing armor, and more pod space, though it lacks the jump jets of that earlier 'Mech. It makes use of weight-saving materials in the form of an XL engine, an endo steel internal structure, and four tons of ferro-fibrous armor. Clan Jade Falcon first produced this OmniMech on their capital of Ironhold, but they soon began to produce it on the Occupation Zone headquarters of Sudeten. In November of 3069, Clan Steel Viper would sack Ironhold as part that Clan's effort to expel the Jade Falcons from the Clan Homeworlds. The invasion would end up destroying the Fire Falcon's production facilities, letting the design fade from use by Homeworlds based Clans. With the 'Mech featuring a total of ten tons of pod space, the Primary configuration of the Fire Falcon is best at short to medium ranges where it can bring its twin Streak SRM-4 launchers, medium pulse laser, two ER medium lasers, and two ER small lasers to bear. While this barrage is powerful for a 'Mech its size, ten double heat sinks are mostly able to keep the design cool.
yangsThoughts: Pretty fast, and lots of firepower for how small it is, a bit light on the armor, but you can't really expect better with a mech this light.

details: The Fire Falcon was created in the early-3050s, apparently as a replacement for the Mist Lynx; it is faster, has more front-facing armor, and more pod space, though it lacks the jump jets of that earlier 'Mech. It makes use of weight-saving materials in the form of an XL engine, an endo steel internal structure, and four tons of ferro-fibrous armor. Clan Jade Falcon first produced this OmniMech on their capital of Ironhold, but they soon began to produce it on the Occupation Zone headquarters of Sudeten. In November of 3069, Clan Steel Viper would sack Ironhold as part that Clan's effort to expel the Jade Falcons from the Clan Homeworlds. The invasion would end up destroying the Fire Falcon's production facilities, letting the design fade from use by Homeworlds based Clans. With the 'Mech featuring a total of ten tons of pod space, the A variant uses an UAC/2 and LRM-10 to bombard from long range. An ER small laser provides close defense.
yangsThoughts: Good fire support thanks to light clan weaponry, but I dunno if I would bother keeping that small laser instead of putting on more armor.

details: The Fire Falcon was created in the early-3050s, apparently as a replacement for the Mist Lynx; it is faster, has more front-facing armor, and more pod space, though it lacks the jump jets of that earlier 'Mech. It makes use of weight-saving materials in the form of an XL engine, an endo steel internal structure, and four tons of ferro-fibrous armor. Clan Jade Falcon first produced this OmniMech on their capital of Ironhold, but they soon began to produce it on the Occupation Zone headquarters of Sudeten. In November of 3069, Clan Steel Viper would sack Ironhold as part that Clan's effort to expel the Jade Falcons from the Clan Homeworlds. The invasion would end up destroying the Fire Falcon's production facilities, letting the design fade from use by Homeworlds based Clans. With the 'Mech featuring a total of ten tons of pod space, the C configuration was designed for scouting and work against infantry. It uses an active probe to find hidden targets, then carries one medium pulse laser, one small pulse laser, one ER small laser, and a pair of SRM-6s to quickly eliminate them. Against soft targets, four machine guns provide ample infantry killing power.
yangsThoughts: High alpha at close range this time, so even less range than the prime, but more firepower. Great against anything you can dodge counter fire from.

details: The Fire Falcon was created in the early-3050s, apparently as a replacement for the Mist Lynx; it is faster, has more front-facing armor, and more pod space, though it lacks the jump jets of that earlier 'Mech. It makes use of weight-saving materials in the form of an XL engine, an endo steel internal structure, and four tons of ferro-fibrous armor. Clan Jade Falcon first produced this OmniMech on their capital of Ironhold, but they soon began to produce it on the Occupation Zone headquarters of Sudeten. In November of 3069, Clan Steel Viper would sack Ironhold as part that Clan's effort to expel the Jade Falcons from the Clan Homeworlds. The invasion would end up destroying the Fire Falcon's production facilities, letting the design fade from use by Homeworlds based Clans. With the 'Mech featuring a total of ten tons of pod space, the H configuration uses a pair of heavy medium lasers and a pair of heavy small lasers. Another pair of medium pulse lasers provide more accurate fire, while three additional double heat sinks keep the 'Mech cool.
yangsThoughts: All energy weapons, so no ammo explosions to worry about, but it's not very accurate with those heavy lasers, maybe upgrade to the improved ones if we have any.

details: Developed as a super-fast scouting design, the Nyx Prototype is still being put through its paces by the Word of Blake. Designed for high speed and low drag, the Nyx is incredibly fast, able to keep pace with all but the absolute fastest units on the battlefield. Using Endo-Steel, Ferro-Fibrous, and a 300-rated XL fusion engine for weight savings, the Nyx still carries a respectable punch for its size. The NX-60 carries twin Medium Variable-Speed Pulse Lasers for a deadly up-close punch, which its immense speed ensures it can always land.
yangsThoughts: Crazy fast, but still some decent firepower, this thing doesn't really have much in the way of weaknesses.

details: Developed as a super-fast scouting design, the Nyx Prototype is still being put through its paces by the Word of Blake. Designed for high speed and low drag, the Nyx is incredibly fast, able to keep pace with all but the absolute fastest units on the battlefield. Using Endo-Steel, Heavy Ferro-Fibrous, and a 300-rated XL fusion engine for weight savings, the Nyx still carries a respectable punch for its size. The NX-65 carries twin ER Medium Lasers as well as a MML-5. C3i ensures it can link easily with allied units during combat.
yangsThoughts: C3i is a nice boost for this thing, but I might go for more firepower instead, increasing accuracy is nice, but so is raw firepower.

details: Developed as a super-fast scouting design, the Nyx Prototype is still being put through its paces by the Word of Blake. Designed for high speed and low drag, the Nyx is incredibly fast, able to keep pace with all but the absolute fastest units on the battlefield. Using Endo-Steel, Ferro-Fibrous, and a 300-rated XL fusion engine for weight savings, the Nyx still carries a respectable punch for its size. The NX-65 carries twin ER Small Lasers as well as trio of MML-3s. One ton each of SRM and LRM ammo gives the Nyx reasonable endurance.
yangsThoughts: An interesting idea, decent fire support with this speed, but not a lot of actual punch with it, you might need more ammo if you want to fight anything of decent size with this thing.

details: Developed to have a lot of free tonnage for a light scout, the Bee omnimech is built on an EndoSteel chassis, with a 245 XXL engine and an XL Gyro. This gives the mech a cavernous 18 tons of available pod space even with 5.5 tons of armor. The Prime variant mounts twin Large Pulse Lasers and an LRM 5 with a single half ton of ammunition and a Guardian ECM and Active Probe for effective fast Electronic Warfare duties.
yangsThoughts: This thing is interesting, it's got a huge amount of free tonnage for the weight, but it's held back a lot by the heavy weight of Inner Sphere grade weaponry. I'd consider upgrading the EW to an angel and bloodhound if we've got em. Also, watch your heat, the XXL engine leaks heat like crazy.

details: Developed to have a lot of free tonnage for a light scout, the Bee omnimech is built on an EndoSteel chassis, with a 245 XXL engine and an XL Gyro. This gives the mech a cavernous 18 tons of available pod space even with 5.5 tons of armor. The A variant mounts an Ultra AC5 and an ER PPC and a Guardian ECM and Active Probe for effective fast Electronic Warfare duties.
yangsThoughts: Entirely a long range fire support variant, it's great as long as you don't let the enemy get too close.

details: Developed to have a lot of free tonnage for a light scout, the Bee omnimech is built on an EndoSteel chassis, with a 245 XXL engine and an XL Gyro. This gives the mech a cavernous 18 tons of available pod space even with 5.5 tons of armor. The B variant mounts an MML-3, a pair each of ER Large Lasers and ER Medium Lasers and a Guardian ECM and Active Probe for effective fast Electronic Warfare duties.
yangsThoughts: More heavy laser focus like the prime, this is better at range overall with the ER Lasers, but overheats even worse.

details: Developed to have a lot of free tonnage for a light scout, the Bee omnimech is built on an EndoSteel chassis, with a 245 XXL engine and an XL Gyro. This gives the mech a cavernous 18 tons of available pod space even with 5.5 tons of armor. The D variant mounts a Large X-Pulse Laser, a Medium X-Pulse Laser and a Guardian ECM and Active Probe for effective fast Electronic Warfare duties.
yangsThoughts: Basically the same idea as the prime, but more firesupport, great if you need something fast and hard to counter attack for a scout mission.

details: Developed to have a lot of free tonnage for a light scout, the Bee omnimech is built on an EndoSteel chassis, with a 245 XXL engine and an XL Gyro. This gives the mech a cavernous 18 tons of available pod space even with 5.5 tons of armor. The E variant is identical to the primary except for using ER PPCs instead of lasers.
yangsThoughts: Much longer range, but even worse heat management, you're really gonna wanna be careful of this thing's heat.

details: Originally designed by Vandenburg Mechanized Industries on New Vandenburg in the Taurian Concordat, the Jotunn quickly disseminated out through the larger Periphery states as a relatively inexpensive artillery platform with a twist. A battery of 4 LRM-15s provide primary firepower, with only twin SRM-6s covering close-range engagements. A rugged 285-rated fusion engine keeps it moving while DHS keep it cold. The real twist though is the Jotunn's Side Mounted Cockpit, giving its pilot a novel view of the battlefield.
yangsThoughts: Relatively simple to manufacture, but a bit limited on the weapon variety. Just a bunch of LRMs and a couple SRMs for close in defense. Definitely consider upgrading to MMLs or Clan-spec stuff.

details: Originally designed by Vandenburg Mechanized Industries on New Vandenburg in the Taurian Concordat, the Jotunn quickly disseminated out through the larger Periphery states as a relatively inexpensive assault platform with a twist. The JTN-6Q is armed simply, with two each of AC/10s and Large Lasers. However, a large XL engine and a core 380 give it unexpected speed while specialty ammunition keep the opponent guessing. The real twist though is the Jotunn's Side Mounted Cockpit, giving its pilot a novel view of the battlefield.
yangsThoughts: Decently fast for an assault mech, but really light armament for something this big, no idea why they'd go for such cheap weapons if they're gonna put such an expensive engine in this.

details: Originally designed by Vandenburg Mechanized Industries on New Vandenburg in the Taurian Concordat, the Jotunn quickly disseminated out through the larger Periphery states as a relatively inexpensive assault platform with a twist. The JTN-7Q is armed with much more advanced weaponry than previous versions, with an Ultra AC10, Light Gauss Rifle, three ER Medium Lasers and one Large Laser. A large XL engine and a core 380 give it unexpected speed while the varied weaponry can fight at multiple ranges. The real twist though is the Jotunn's Side Mounted Cockpit, giving its pilot a novel view of the battlefield.
yangsThoughts: Now this is much more reasonable armament for a mech with an engine this costly, no point in spending millions on the engine if you're gonna mount less weaponry than a lot of heavy mechs.

details: The 'Mech was originally designed to be as fast as any 'Mech that existed at the time, trading a great deal of potential firepower and armor for an incredible ground speed. While this made the Hermes nearly untouchable at sustained pace, the MechWarriors who piloted them usually had doubts about taking such an underarmed scout 'Mech into battle and it soon became unpopular. Heat management is provided for by the standard number of ten single heat sinks mounted in the GM 270 engine. In spite of using an endo steel chassis, this massive engine takes up nearly half of the Hermes' mass and is responsible for its tremendous cruising speed of 97 km/h as well as both an anemic weapons payload and paltry five tons of armor. For armament, the Hermes mounts a pair of Hellion-V medium lasers backed up by an old model Olympian flamer in the left arm.
yangsThoughts: Incredibly fast, but almost no guns, you're gonna wanna be careful with this thing cause it's easy to blow up for the enemy.

details: The 'Mech was originally designed to be as fast as any 'Mech that existed at the time, trading a great deal of potential firepower and armor for an incredible ground speed. While this made the Hermes nearly untouchable at sustained pace, the MechWarriors who piloted them usually had doubts about taking such an underarmed scout 'Mech into battle and it soon became unpopular. Heat management is provided for by the standard number of ten single heat sinks mounted in the GM 270 engine. In spite of using an endo steel chassis, this massive engine takes up nearly half of the Hermes' mass and is responsible for its tremendous cruising speed of 97 km/h as well as both an anemic weapons payload and paltry five tons of armor. The Royal Hermes introduced in 2740 has an upgraded weapon suite with four medium lasers and one small laser in addition to the flamer. It also mounts a Beagle active probe and MASC motive system. Room for these improvements were made possible by switching to an XL engine, and as usual with Royal 'Mechs, it also uses double heat sinks.
yangsThoughts: Now this is much better, it's got a lot more weaponry and it's still super fast, very much worth the upgrade cost.

details: The 'Mech was originally designed to be as fast as any 'Mech that existed at the time, trading a great deal of potential firepower and armor for an incredible ground speed. While this made the Hermes nearly untouchable at sustained pace, the MechWarriors who piloted them usually had doubts about taking such an underarmed scout 'Mech into battle and it soon became unpopular. Heat management is provided for by the standard number of ten single heat sinks mounted in the GM 270 engine. In spite of using an endo steel chassis, this massive engine takes up nearly half of the Hermes' mass and is responsible for its tremendous cruising speed of 97 km/h as well as both an anemic weapons payload and paltry five tons of armor. Introduced in 3060 for use within the Free Worlds League and by their Word of Blake allies, the 4M removes a medium laser and small laser from the 4K to upgrade the C3 slave unit to an improved C3 computer.
yangsThoughts: A much better weapons loadout and more range and better electronics, definitely a worthy upgrade to the original designs.

details: The 'Mech was originally designed to be as fast as any 'Mech that existed at the time, trading a great deal of potential firepower and armor for an incredible ground speed. While this made the Hermes nearly untouchable at sustained pace, the MechWarriors who piloted them usually had doubts about taking such an underarmed scout 'Mech into battle and it soon became unpopular. Heat management is provided for by the standard number of ten single heat sinks mounted in the GM 270 engine. In spite of using an endo steel chassis, this massive engine takes up nearly half of the Hermes' mass and is responsible for its tremendous cruising speed of 97 km/h as well as both an anemic weapons payload and paltry five tons of armor. The 4S Hermes was introduced in 3057 and based on the 3S1 variant. It carries over the MASC and endo steel chassis from the 3S1 model, but has had a complete upgrade of its weapons and engine. The original standard engine has been replaced with an extralight engine, and the armor protection has been upgraded to five and a half tons. The weapons on board the Hermes have also been replaced by three medium pulse lasers, giving the Hermes an unprecedented amount of firepower in its long history.
yangsThoughts: A lot of firepower and it's very heat stable too, so it's a great option for scouting and combat.

details: The Whitworth is something of an ugly duckling, known among both critics and fans as the 'Tin Woodsman.' It was originally produced in 2610 as a BattleMech that was meant to fill the weight gap between the lighter Wasp and the heavier Phoenix Hawk 'Mechs as a light-weight missile support platform. The Whitworth, though, is slower than either of these designs by over 30 km/h. The original WTH-1S Whitworth model saw combat in the Age of War, however since it was armed with Harpoon-6 SRMs many were lost when their pilots rushed into close-range combat to effectively use them where they were often mauled by heavier machines. The Whitworth's lack of hand actuators, low speed curve, unconventional armor distribution, and weak leg joints made the machine a poor brawling platform. At the insistence of the Star League, the Whitworth Company replaced these weapons with LRMs in 2689 and the WTH-1 would serve in the Star League Defense Force as an able if mediocre scout and light strike platform. Whitworths often took part in reconnaissance-in-force missions and raids against well-defended areas, often alongside Wolverines and Phoenix Hawks, or provided fire support for heavy attack forces with Riflemans and Warhammers when not used in common defensive roles. The Whitworth carries as its primary weapons two Longbow-10 LRM launchers with 12 reloads each in both right and left torsos. These missile launchers allow the Whitworth to strike an enemy at long ranges with a moderate amount of missiles. While against heavier units it is somewhat effective, when used to provide LRM support to a recon lance, the Whitworth's LRMs can deter pursuit by other light units. For secondary weapons, the Whitworth has three Intek medium lasers, one mounted in the head and in either arm, that allow it to effectively defend itself from units of the same weight class and lighter at short ranges.
yangsThoughts: A classic fire support design, but it's very slow for the size, you'll really need to upgrade the weaponry with things like MMLs or MRMs to get decent firepower.

details: The Whitworth is something of an ugly duckling, known among both critics and fans as the 'Tin Woodsman.' It was originally produced in 2610 as a BattleMech that was meant to fill the weight gap between the lighter Wasp and the heavier Phoenix Hawk 'Mechs as a light-weight missile support platform. The Whitworth, though, is slower than either of these designs by over 30 km/h. The original WTH-1S Whitworth model saw combat in the Age of War, however since it was armed with Harpoon-6 SRMs many were lost when their pilots rushed into close-range combat to effectively use them where they were often mauled by heavier machines. The Whitworth's lack of hand actuators, low speed curve, unconventional armor distribution, and weak leg joints made the machine a poor brawling platform. At the insistence of the Star League, the Whitworth Company replaced these weapons with LRMs in 2689 and the WTH-1 would serve in the Star League Defense Force as an able if mediocre scout and light strike platform. Whitworths often took part in reconnaissance-in-force missions and raids against well-defended areas, often alongside Wolverines and Phoenix Hawks, or provided fire support for heavy attack forces with Riflemans and Warhammers when not used in common defensive roles. The original production model of the Whitworth prior to its service in the Star League armies mounting two Harpoon-6 SRM-6 launchers and four additional heat sinks allowing the MechWarrior better heat management capabilities. However since this loadout encourages a close combat approach for which the 'Mech is poorly suited, these were refitted with LRMs to become WTH-1's by Star League defense planners and the variant remains extremely rare.
yangsThoughts: If you're fighting small mechs this can work out with good firepower, but otherwise it's way too fragile to handle bigger things.

details: The Whitworth is something of an ugly duckling, known among both critics and fans as the 'Tin Woodsman.' It was originally produced in 2610 as a BattleMech that was meant to fill the weight gap between the lighter Wasp and the heavier Phoenix Hawk 'Mechs as a light-weight missile support platform. The Whitworth, though, is slower than either of these designs by over 30 km/h. The original WTH-1S Whitworth model saw combat in the Age of War, however since it was armed with Harpoon-6 SRMs many were lost when their pilots rushed into close-range combat to effectively use them where they were often mauled by heavier machines. The Whitworth's lack of hand actuators, low speed curve, unconventional armor distribution, and weak leg joints made the machine a poor brawling platform. At the insistence of the Star League, the Whitworth Company replaced these weapons with LRMs in 2689 and the WTH-1 would serve in the Star League Defense Force as an able if mediocre scout and light strike platform. Whitworths often took part in reconnaissance-in-force missions and raids against well-defended areas, often alongside Wolverines and Phoenix Hawks, or provided fire support for heavy attack forces with Riflemans and Warhammers when not used in common defensive roles. This Draconis Combine variant of the Whitworth introduced in 3068 harkens back to the WTH-1S Whitworth though whether they were custom refits or new production models is unknown. The chassis has been redesigned using endo steel construction techniques, one of the three medium lasers have been downgraded to a small laser and the heat sinks were upgraded to ten double heat sinks. The saved weight was used to add four Streak SRM-4 launchers and a C3i unit.
yangsThoughts: This has a lot more close in firepower, and better electronics, so it can fight better, but it's still very slow.

details: Once in service, the Thorn found its niche as a light 'Mech hunter and infantry support platform, and supporters quickly pointed out the Thorn was, ton-for-ton, superior to most other light 'Mechs in terms of combat power and could reduce an opponent of similar weight to scrap in minutes. This aspect made it very popular for light 'Mech companies expecting heavy fighting and infantry in need of fire support. The Thorn carries a weapons array that is not usually seen on light 'Mechs: a Zeus LRM-5 launcher mounted in the right arm with one ton of reloads in the right torso, paired with two Hellion Spitfire medium lasers split between the head and left arm. The long reach the LRM launcher provides is necessary, since the Thorn lacks the speed to catch faster light 'Mechs, although it is easily damaged in hand-to-hand combat.
yangsThoughts: A decent fire support mech if you need something at 20 tons, but it's kind of slow for a 20 ton mech and the firepower is a bit lacking.

details: Once in service, the Thorn found its niche as a light 'Mech hunter and infantry support platform, and supporters quickly pointed out the Thorn was, ton-for-ton, superior to most other light 'Mechs in terms of combat power and could reduce an opponent of similar weight to scrap in minutes. This aspect made it very popular for light 'Mech companies expecting heavy fighting and infantry in need of fire support. The advanced model employed by the Star League Defense Force Royal Divisions in 2743 changed the role of the 'Mech from a missile to an energy boat, replacing the LRM-5 launcher with an ER large laser, but retained one medium laser and downgrading the other to a small laser. Dropping the unneeded CASE, the 'Mech also used a MASC system for temporary speed bursts and double heat sinks to vent its increased waste heat output. The THE-Nb mounted an XL engine to save the weight to mount this extra equipment, at the cost of reduced survivability.
yangsThoughts: A direct fire mech instead of a fire support unit, this has more brawling ability and can move faster, but you're gonna have to watch the heat.

details: Once in service, the Thorn found its niche as a light 'Mech hunter and infantry support platform, and supporters quickly pointed out the Thorn was, ton-for-ton, superior to most other light 'Mechs in terms of combat power and could reduce an opponent of similar weight to scrap in minutes. This aspect made it very popular for light 'Mech companies expecting heavy fighting and infantry in need of fire support. Developed in 3051 on the orders of Precentor Martial Anastasius Focht to try and close the gap between the Clans and Com Guards, the factory refit THE-N1 received its baptism of fire during the Battle of Tukayyid. Having reached a similar conclusion that resulted in the THE-Nb, the CASE was dropped and the standard engine was swapped for an XL engine to allow the standard weaponry to be stripped for an all energy weapon array: a right arm mounted Kinslaughter H-Class ER PPC and a head-mounted Intek small laser. This new weapons array, enhanced by double heat sinks, added a level of lethality never seen before on the Thorn.
yangsThoughts: Another direct fire version, this time with an ER PPC, but a single ER PPC is a bit weak at closer ranges, so you might want to put smaller weapons on.

details: Once in service, the Thorn found its niche as a light 'Mech hunter and infantry support platform, and supporters quickly pointed out the Thorn was, ton-for-ton, superior to most other light 'Mechs in terms of combat power and could reduce an opponent of similar weight to scrap in minutes. This aspect made it very popular for light 'Mech companies expecting heavy fighting and infantry in need of fire support. A new production variant of the THE-N1 developed by the Word of Blake in 3071, the THE-N2 took its cues from both the THE-N and THE-T variants, swapping the ER PPC with an MML-7 and the standard small laser for a Diverse Optics ER small laser. A two-ton CASE-protected ammo bay allowed the THE-N2 to carry both LRM and SRM munitions to make best use of the Multi-Missile Launcher.
yangsThoughts: This puts the firesupport back while not having as much direct fire damage, you might want to downgrade the mml to a 5 in order to upgrade the lasers

details: The Spector was originally designed by Norse Technologies to be a lighter version of the Exterminator BattleMech, made to hunt down light command 'Mechs. The SLDF's enthusiasm for the Spector is due to the BattleMech's stealth capabilities, combining both Chameleon light polarization shield and a null-signature system. The Spector was originally designed to use the advanced null-signature system, able to hide the 'Mech's radar and heat signatures, and Chameleon light polarization shield, capable of obscuring the BattleMech's appearance. However, these technologies were lost during the Succession Wars, the current incarnation of the Spector, SPR-5F, does not carry a null-signature system nor the Chameleon LPS because of this. Even without its advanced stealth capabilities, the Spector that Norse-Storm BattleMechs reintroduced in 3053 is an impressive light 'Mech. The Spector is built on a weight saving Norse XT-Light Type AE endo steel chassis, powered by a Magna 245 XL engine that gives it a top speed of 118.8 km/h, while seven HildCo Model 11c jump jets give it a jump ranged of 210 meters. Protection from enemy fire is provided by seven and a half tons of Starshield Light standard armor, while a Guardian ECM suite allows it to defend itself against advanced enemy targeting and range finding equipment. The Spector carries a Nightwind large laser as its primary weapon, backed up by a pair of Defiance B3M medium lasers and a single Defiance B4S small laser for close combat. While most consider this a light weapons payload for a 35-ton 'Mech, the Spector's superior mobility and mission profile do not require a very heavy weapons payload.
yangsThoughts: Somewhat light armament but nice armor and with fast and maneverable movement with those jump jets, you'll need to replace the jets if you want to get more firepower on.

details: The Spector was originally designed by Norse Technologies to be a lighter version of the Exterminator BattleMech, made to hunt down light command 'Mechs. The SLDF's enthusiasm for the Spector is due to the BattleMech's stealth capabilities, combining both Chameleon light polarization shield and a null-signature system. The Spector was originally designed to use the advanced null-signature system, able to hide the 'Mech's radar and heat signatures, and Chameleon light polarization shield, capable of obscuring the BattleMech's appearance. However, these technologies were lost during the Succession Wars, the current incarnation of the Spector, SPR-5F, does not carry a null-signature system nor the Chameleon LPS because of this. The Spector is built on a weight saving Norse XT-Light Type AE endo steel chassis, powered by a Magna 245 XL engine that gives it a top speed of 118.8 km/h, while seven HildCo Model 11c jump jets give it a jump ranged of 210 meters. Protection from enemy fire is provided by seven and a half tons of Starshield Light standard armor, while a Guardian ECM suite allows it to defend itself against advanced enemy targeting and range finding equipment. This variant carries a Guardian ECM suite, one large laser in the right arm and two medium lasers in the left. More importantly, the SPR-4F is extremely stealthy, mounting both a Chameleon LPS and a null-signature system.
yangsThoughts: I don't believe it, a genuine original model Spector, with all its stealth abilities. This thing isn't heavily armed but it doesn't have to be, since it's supposed to get behind things and kill them before they can react.

details: Following the Battle of Nox and the failure of the Gladiator during that battle, Coordinator Yama Von Rohrs ordered the creation of a new manufacturing center with a new BattleMech. 2487 saw the first Von Rohrs step out of the factory. This new 'Mech was to be symbolic of the Dragon's steps forward, as well as the Dragon itself. Following the McAllister Rebellion that saw all of the members of the Von Rohrs dynasty killed, Coordinator Martin McAllister ordered the Von Rohrs renamed the Hebi. He further mandated that the BattleMech not be salvaged from the battlefield and instead chose to focus on newer designs, a situation eerily reminiscent of the fate of the Gladiator. Production ceased in 2510 and the last Hebi was dismantled in 2518. The BattleMech itself can reach speeds up to 86 km/h. It is protected by a reasonable nine tons of armor. Primarily a long-range combatant, the Von Rohrs is armed with a Lord's Light P1 PPC and a DLC-10 LRM-10. If an enemy closes, only a single MagnaFlex Flamer lacks a minimum range, though it is particularly effective against infantry and vehicles.
yangsThoughts: I'm not even surprised at this point, why wouldn't an extinct mech from 500 years ago turn up in our mechbay in repairable condition. The impossibility of it existing aside, it's a bit light on armor for a heavy mech and doesn't have much space for weaponry compared to modern mechs.

details: Following the Battle of Nox and the failure of the Gladiator during that battle, Coordinator Yama Von Rohrs ordered the creation of a new manufacturing center with a new BattleMech. 2487 saw the first Von Rohrs step out of the factory. This new 'Mech was to be symbolic of the Dragon's steps forward, as well as the Dragon itself. Following the McAllister Rebellion that saw all of the members of the Von Rohrs dynasty killed, Coordinator Martin McAllister ordered the Von Rohrs renamed the Hebi. He further mandated that the BattleMech not be salvaged from the battlefield and instead chose to focus on newer designs, a situation eerily reminiscent of the fate of the Gladiator. Production ceased in 2510 and the last Hebi was dismantled in 2518. The BattleMech itself can reach speeds up to 86 km/h. It is protected by a reasonable nine tons of armor. A planned variant that may or may not have reached production status prior to the annihilation of the Von Rohrs family, this version of the Von Rohrs called for the replacement of the missile launcher with two SRM-4s. An additional heat sink would have allowed it to keep up a respectable rate of fire.
yangsThoughts: I'm not even surprised at this point, why wouldn't an extinct mech from 500 years ago turn up in our mechbay in repairable condition. The impossibility of it existing aside, it's a bit light on armor for a heavy mech and doesn't have much space for weaponry compared to modern mechs.

details: Initially built to fight Elementals, the Fireball was made incredibly fast so that it could come to the aid of larger units. Unfortunately, this speed came at the cost of firepower leaving the 'Mech woefully underpowered to actually kill the Elementals it was supposed to defeat. Repurposed as a Scout and Raider, however, the mech served well with its ludicrously fast speed letting it outrun almost anything on the field. The Fireball is built around a massive five-ton DAV 220 extralight fusion engine, which propels the 'Mech to a cruising speed of 119 km/h and top speed of 184 km/h, allowing it to respond to a call from anywhere in the line for immediate support. The high speed of the Fireball comes at a price though, as the 'Mech only has four tons of armor protection and a very light weapons array. A Hovertec Streak SRM-2, mounted over the left shoulder along with one ton of reload, serves as its primary weapon, giving it highly accurate short-range firepower. The 'Mech is also armed with an LFN Lindblad machine gun for anti-infantry combat, carried over the right shoulder with one ton of ammunition.
yangsThoughts: This thing is seriously fast, like run all the way to the other side of the op zone in one go level fast, but it's got no damage to speak of, so you've gotta bring other units with heavier firepower to compensate.

details: Initially built to fight Elementals, the Fireball was made incredibly fast so that it could come to the aid of larger units. Unfortunately, this speed came at the cost of firepower leaving the 'Mech woefully underpowered to actually kill the Elementals it was supposed to defeat. Repurposed as a Scout and Raider, however, the mech served well with its ludicrously fast speed letting it outrun almost anything on the field. The Fireball is built around a massive five-ton DAV 220 extralight fusion engine, which propels the 'Mech to a cruising speed of 119 km/h and top speed of 184 km/h, allowing it to respond to a call from anywhere in the line for immediate support. The high speed of the Fireball comes at a price though, as the 'Mech only has four tons of armor protection and a very light weapons array. The 8D variant of the Fireball, released in 3054, was built with the hope of reducing the Fireball's ammunition dependence as well as increasing its firepower. This is done by removing the Streak SRM-2 launcher and replacing it with two medium lasers, vastly increasing the raw firepower of the Fireball. The 'Mech has also received an additional half ton of armor.
yangsThoughts: Somewhat more firepower than the 7D but still really light overall. Gonna need something bigger to actually do the hitting alongside this thing.

details: Initially built to fight Elementals, the Fireball was made incredibly fast so that it could come to the aid of larger units. Unfortunately, this speed came at the cost of firepower leaving the 'Mech woefully underpowered to actually kill the Elementals it was supposed to defeat. Repurposed as a Scout and Raider, however, the mech served well with its ludicrously fast speed letting it outrun almost anything on the field. The Fireball is built around a massive five-ton DAV 220 extralight fusion engine, which propels the 'Mech to a cruising speed of 119 km/h and top speed of 184 km/h, allowing it to respond to a call from anywhere in the line for immediate support. The high speed of the Fireball comes at a price though, as the 'Mech only has four tons of armor protection and a very light weapons array. The 9D is a compromise between the 7D and 8D models. Released at the same time as the 8D, this variant removes the machine gun and its ammunition and replaces them with a single medium laser. The 'Mech retains its Streak SRM-2 launcher, but gains an additional half ton of armor protection.
yangsThoughts: Somewhat more firepower than the 7D but still really light overall. Gonna need something bigger to actually do the hitting alongside this thing.

details: Initially built to fight Elementals, the Fireball was made incredibly fast so that it could come to the aid of larger units. Unfortunately, this speed came at the cost of firepower leaving the 'Mech woefully underpowered to actually kill the Elementals it was supposed to defeat. Repurposed as a Scout and Raider, however, the mech served well with its ludicrously fast speed letting it outrun almost anything on the field. The Fireball is built around a massive five-ton DAV 220 extralight fusion engine, which propels the 'Mech to a cruising speed of 119 km/h and top speed of 184 km/h, allowing it to respond to a call from anywhere in the line for immediate support. The high speed of the Fireball comes at a price though, as the 'Mech only has four tons of armor protection and a very light weapons array. This upgraded version of the Fireball, debuting in 3076, uses an ER medium laser in each side torso, adds an ER small laser to the right arm, and enhances their accuracy through the use of a targeting computer. A MASC system boosts the 'Mech's maximum speed and heavy ferro-fibrous armor increases protection.
yangsThoughts: Somehow it is even faster than the regular Fireball, I can't believe a mech can actually go this fast, but it's still lightly armed.

details: Based on an old Star League design called the Sling, the Duan Gung likely shares its roots with the Koshi OmniMech, as the Sling shared many characteristics with both designs, and it is believed that the Sling was easily upgraded with OmniMech technology. The Duan Gung is a competent light fire support 'Mech, with a ground speed of 119 km/h and the ability to jump up to 180 meters, it is capable of keeping up with most other light 'Mechs. To save weight and allow the Duan Gung to carry as heavy an arsenal as possible, the 'Mech is built on a Chariot Type II endo steel chassis and is powered by an Omni 175 XL engine that weighs half as much as its standard fusion counterpart. To handle the heat from its weapons, the Duan Gung has ten double heat sinks, which are more than adequate for the 'Mech's needs. Finally, for protection from enemy fire, the Duan Gung carries four tons of Valiant DefCo armor, providing more than enough protection for the 'Mech's intended role. The Duan Gung's primary weapon is a Zeus LRM-10 launcher, which allows the 'Mech to hit targets at up to 630 meters, while also giving the Duan Gung indirect fire support capabilities. For close-range engagements, the 'Mech has two Martell medium lasers.
yangsThoughts: Decent firepower for a small mech like this, but you have to watch out for enemy fire as this thing is fragile.

details: Based on an old Star League design called the Sling, the Duan Gung likely shares its roots with the Koshi OmniMech, as the Sling shared many characteristics with both designs, and it is believed that the Sling was easily upgraded with OmniMech technology. The Duan Gung is a competent light fire support 'Mech, with a ground speed of 119 km/h and the ability to jump up to 180 meters, it is capable of keeping up with most other light 'Mechs. To save weight and allow the Duan Gung to carry as heavy an arsenal as possible, the 'Mech is built on a Chariot Type II endo steel chassis and is powered by an Omni 175 XL engine that weighs half as much as its standard fusion counterpart. To handle the heat from its weapons, the Duan Gung has ten double heat sinks, which are more than adequate for the 'Mech's needs. Finally, for protection from enemy fire, the Duan Gung carries four tons of Valiant DefCo armor, providing more than enough protection for the 'Mech's intended role. The D9-G10 was introduced during the Jihad, where it was enhanced by the new technologies of the times. The 'Mech's armor is replaced by stealth armor, and the LRM-10 is dropped in favor of a light PPC; it also mounts a Guardian ECM suite as part of the stealth armor upgrade.
yangsThoughts: Stealthy and powerful for the weight, but still fragile if anything catches it, so keep a close watch.

details: Conceptualised as a long range support platform, the Pendragon was a result of House Davion’s Jihad era MUSE programs. A 95-ton mech, the Pendragon is built around a 285-rated XL Engine and is loosely based on the Templar OmniMech. The Pendragon PDR-1X is the original MUSE prototype utilising a mix of Clan and IS technology. A pair of Clan-spec LRM-20’s along with a pair of standard LRM-20's provide range firepower, while an ER PPC with capacitor and a trio of Clan ER Medium Lasers provide closer ranged firepower. A C3i computer provides combat electronic handling.
yangsThoughts: Heavy and heavily armed, you don't often see a mix of innersphere and clan LRMs like this, but it's apparently for a cost reasons to keep the weaponry cheaper. It's still a massive amount of missile firepower and it can easily crush most things.

details: Conceptualised as a long range support platform, the Pendragon was a result of House Davion’s Jihad era MUSE programs. A 95-ton mech, the Pendragon is built around a 380-rated XL Engine and is loosely based on the Templar OmniMech. The Pendragon PDR-1R is the first production model and unlike the original prototype is entirely built with Inner Sphere technology. A pair of LRM-15’s and a pair of LRM 10's provide range firepower, while an ER PPC with capacitor and a trio of ER Medium Lasers provide closer ranged firepower. A C3i computer provides combat electronic support.
yangsThoughts: Heavy and heavily armed, they switched to all Inner Sphere LRMs on the production version. It's still a lot of missile firepower and it can easily crush most things.

details: Conceptualised as a long range support platform, the Pendragon was a result of House Davion’s Jihad era MUSE programs. A 95-ton mech, the Pendragon is built around a 380-rated XL Engine and is loosely based on the Templar OmniMech. The Pendragon PDR-3R is armed with a pair of RAC/2s that provide extreme range firepower, while an ER PPC and a trio of ER Medium Lasers contribute closer ranged firepower. A C3i computer provides combat electronic support.
yangsThoughts: Heavy and heavily armed, this time they went for rotary autocannons, personally I think they should have gone with the RAC5s for more damage, but this thing is still plenty powerful.

details: Developed as a complement to the Griffin in the later years of the Jihad, the Ghost is a medium strike 'Mech that saw considerable use in the years after the formation of the Republic of the Sphere. Developed by the Lyran Commonwealth and elements of the former Free Worlds League, the Ghost carries an extensive array of electronic warfare equipment into combat. With excellent land speed and formidable medium-range weaponry, the Ghost is ideal for hunter-killer and heavy raiding missions, especially when paired with Griffins. Though production runs started low due to the expensive stealth armor and electronics, both Defiance and Earthwerks make a simpler Ghost variant available for purchase on the open market. The Ghost is built on a standard Enran TXS2A chassis and powered by a GM 300 XL engine, allowing it to move at speeds up to 97.2 km/h. Besides this speed, the Ghost is protected by 10.5 tons of stealth armor, powered by a mounted Guardian ECM suite. These, combined with the weapon loadout, allow the Ghost to dictate the pace and range of engagements. Offensively, the Ghost uses twin Defiance 1002 light PPCs to hit a target at long ranges, supplemented by two Helga Series MML-3s that can support this fire with one ton of long range missiles. After closing with enemy forces, the Ghost can easily switch to its single ton SRM ammunition and the BlazeFire Longshot ER medium laser to damage the enemy. A Beagle active probe helps the Ghost detect enemies and defeat enemy ECM, while fourteen double heat sinks work to keep things cool.
yangsThoughts: A good stealthy medium mech, watch out for enemies sensor locking you when you don't want them to though.

yangsThoughts: A perfectly serviceable medium mech, not the best firepower but decent for this sort of thing.

details: Developed as a complement to the Griffin in the later years of the Jihad, the Ghost is a medium strike 'Mech that saw considerable use in the years after the formation of the Republic of the Sphere. Developed by the Lyran Commonwealth and elements of the former Free Worlds League, the Ghost carries an extensive array of electronic warfare equipment into combat.
